user_comment: Likes: Versatile, durable, reasonably priced Dislike: Nothing as of yet! Granted, it’s faux leather so don’t expect it to look like actual leather… I’m 5’5 and around 125 lbs and it has been a challenge finding a belt that fits my waist perfectly. I hate when belts have excess length just hanging off you. I got the small (26”-28”) and I have a 27” waist. IMO, this is a solid option for the price. Make sure to measure your waist before choosing a size!

Listen to the measurements and size chart! I typically wear a size 18 in all it's variances from 36-39 inch waist. I ordered these in a 2XL and fit a comfortable looseness around my waist, with enough room to layer under them if I needed to. The legs and pockets are BIG and comfortable. I can store my phone, wallet, whatever else I need in the pockets and probably store even more. If you're looking for very warm sweats, these are it! I experienced no shrinkage in the wash, and I line dried so I can't say anything about dryer effects. Like most sweats these get little lint balls everywhere inside of them, so don't be surprised if you have any leg stubble, you'll have blue fuzz all over them!

Listen, I didn't ask them to, I didn't know about it in advance, heck, I didn't even know they were aware of my existence. But obviously, Gap did & does. They went ahead and designed a pair of custom jean leggings made to my exact measurements. Now, even though I plan to buy every single pair of size 6/28, I decided it would only be fair to share my stats with you as well as the stats of MY custom made, comfortable, flattering & 100% perfect fitting jean leggings. Me: 140-145# 5'3" 33.5" waist (measured at the bottom of my bellybutton because that's where MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS sit) 40" hips (measured at the widest point, mid-bum) MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS (Measured flat) 9" rise 14" waist 17" hips They have a REAL zipper fly, not one of those "just for show" kind commonly found on jean leggings. Every pair I've tried in that past with a "fake fly" always had extra fabric that bunched up at the crotch & made me look like I was smuggling oranges whenever I sat down (& they didn't look any better when standing up, either). The traditional 5 pockets are fully functional (real) as well. The stitching/color are absolute perfection & the plain, proportionately sized back pockets are what my denim dreams are made of. If you have time pls check out the pic I attached to see another one of the reasons why these fit so well. I hope my humble opinion helps you in some way. Thank you for reading.

Listen!!! I never post reviews with pictures but I had to!! These are my FAVORITE leggings so far! They're not thick, great quality, very soft material. Kinda see through with the navy pair but they're so cute, I don't mind! My only problem is I wish the inseam was longer. I'm 5'6 and it comes to just below my ankle. I have disproportionately long legs, a small waist and wider bottom half. It makes finding long enough pants a living nightmare. But! I love how they look on me so I try not to focus on the length. I want a pair in every color!

I am a plus size woman My measurements are bust 40 waist sometimes 45-48 inches hips 52 inches my belly swells sometimes My weight is 239-245 depending on swelling. The pants stretch at the waist but the pant legs reminds me of carpenter pants this is too baggy to be " boot cut jeans" or " skinny jeans" I should have gotten a size 18 or maybe a 16 I'm 5'9 tall and the pants come to my ankles standing but sitting above them . I had no problems with the zipper or stitches but these pants I will be using them for work but I have a bad feeling that they will stretch big time that's why I wanted the size 18 because of the super stretch material at the waist I have a slight extra tummy .

Long but detailed review: I'm 5'6 250 for reference and I bought a 16 long for both pairs. I got the long because sometimes pants aren't long enough for me but I think I would have been fine with the regular length cause they bunch up at the bottom. The Sea sky (dark blue) color fit like a glove. I love them so much! I decided to get the night sky (black) color and they were really tight. I should have gone a size up for these ones. So be weary of the size differences in the different colors. They were also made in two different countries so IDK if that's why there sizing is different. They have a pretty decent amount of stretch to them. They're not like regular cotton jeans where they is no stretch, but they're also not like leggings where they're super stretchy either.. pretty much in the middle in my opinion. I've also worn and washed them both multiple times and they are holding up really well. So all in all really good jeans!

Loose throughout the leg and gets softer and looser as you wear them, exactly as should be in a boyfriend cut jean. It is more like traditional denim, there is not a lot of elastic in the material, but the denim is high quality and softens up A LOT. With the loser leg fit, you don’t need the stretch. These photos show wear after 8 hours and how much they can loosen up! Size down one size. I probably could have gone down 2 sizes except that boyfriend cut jeans are supposed to fit loser in the hips and legs in general so it doesn’t make sense going too tight. I’m usually at 27, at 5’3” and usually 128lbs with curves in hips and thighs, but the 26 fit perfectly. Bonus points: they are very comfy while pregnant! I’m currently 5 months pregnant too and , while my waist size hasn’t changed drastically yet the 26 is still the right size. Since there is a tiny bit of stretch material in them, I probably have easily another inch around the waist I can grow into if pushed.

LOVE these pants! Not shiny or super fake looking at all. I would agree with other reviewers that say they run slightly small - I have a big butt & hips and small waist, so it’s always a toss up between a M and a L for me with pants, however, a lot of times the waist will be too big with a large and that was not the case here at all - they fit great and made my waist look snatched. They are a little on the shorter as some other reviewers noted, but the perfect length with heels even though I’m tall (5’8”). As for the smell - there was a bit of the typical faux leather smell, and based on other reviews that said they smelled horrible by the end of the night, I preemptively flipped them inside out, sprayed with Febreeze and ran them through the dryer with a dryer sheet on low heat for about 30 mins. No more smell, and still smelled good at the end of the night - and I live in Miami where it is HUMID to say the least. If you are on the fence, buy them! You won’t be disappointed, especially for the price!

Love these, I hope they don’t ever stop carrying them. I could only find this one pair. Walmart usually carries, but they have been out of mid rise. Love that I could find a straight leg that doesn’t flair too much, or drag. **waist =31” **Regular length = 32” ** GREAT FIT** I’m a size 12 weight 167 lbs. 5’6” I am a med bone size, so that is where some of my weight is. I have narrow hips, and a big butt. (which I hate, but that’s genetic. Why in the WORLD would anyone want a large Ass is crazy! But whatever.) **GREAT FABRIC/MATERIAL** Soft but still jeans, and not too tight, loose enough to still wear while hangin’ at home. Usually when I get home, the first thing I take off are my jeans. All my jeans are too, stretchy. Stretchy is great, until they fall down a few inches, after a few hours, like tights do, and I end up having to wear a tight belt. And my regular cotton jeans have no stretch, are a hard & stiff fabric. They fit great until I sit down once, or twice, because reg, non stretch “hard/stuff fabric” , actually just stretch out, and never stretch back. **Stretch But with these Signature jeans the fabric has the perfect amount of stretch, a bit stretchy, and a bit reg jean. Just enough to wear 2x before a wash. Hope you try them. Happy jean hunting.

Materials/Design/Construction - I don't like wearing work/office pants in general, just constricting and uncomfortable. I also hate polyester which most work pants seem to be. Saw these in gray, noticed the cotton blend, and the flat waistband, no crinkled bunchy elastic. AND, there is stretch in the waist even though it is flat, proving it is possible to create a flexible waistband without looking frumpy. Straight leg, sleek, not too wide. Material feels so much nicer than stuffy polyester. No complaints with construction. Fit/Sizing - I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6, long torso. Amazon sells globally so I never just order my typical U.S. size, but I couldn't find a size chart so used my general Amazon store rule which is, go 1 size up from U.S size. Ordered size 8 (regular, not short) and like the fit; a little roomy in the crotch and thighs but I like how relaxed the waist feels so wouldn't go down. Some reviewers said pants were too long but I'd actually appreciate another inch or two, so consider your own height, shoes and preferences. Photos taken right out of package, the little wrinkles on the fly could easily be steamed or ironed out, otherwise rather wrinkle-free which is impressive.

Me: 5’10ish 140 lb woman. I’m typically a small-4/6 34 inseam 27 pre pregnancy 33 postpartum I bought a small- before babies Bought medium- postpartum To all my tall girls that feel out of place… MY TALL GIRLS, THESE PANTS COULD BE FOR YOU! These pants can definitely be dressed up or dressed down for a more chic look. They are thin and perfect for spring/summer weather. The material isn’t high end material but it isn’t trash either. The stitching is nice through out and clean. Normal trousers sit lower low but these are true high waist pants. The bottom is cuffed up and its sewed to keep in place(see picture) If you carry your weight in your midsection I can see these highlighting that area for you. Consider wearing shape wear to smooth everything out. I noticed it was less forgiving on my postpartum lower belly but pre-pregnancy I did not have that problem. The inseam seems to be approx 32 but because it’s cuffed and only sewed on 2 opposing sides- it slightly drapes adding a some additional length. If you’re short, these will be way too long for you. Pros: THEY FIT TALLER WOMEN!!!!!! I put these on with a low block heel and also platform sneakers. Classy/chic look can be achieve getting the most usage out of styling these for different occasions. Iron/steam easily Cons: They aren’t wrinkle free and hold a bit of wrinkles if you stay in a position for way too long. It doesn’t wrinkle nearly as bad at linen. 👍

Me: 5’6.5” (5’7” with shoes), 135 lbs, 36-30-37, still haven’t lost the last 15 lbs of baby weight and 10 of those are at my middle; used to be a 0-2 in pants. Pros: good material, stretchy but they have weight to them so they don’t look like stretchy pants. Inseam was actually 33 inches (measuring guide said 32”) which was good for me since wearing flat dress shoes it made them perfect but they are slightly long in just my slippers. They have pockets - real ones! I can actually put my phone in a back pocket. Liberating because I can wear nice pants for work that feel as good as jammies while not feeling bad about my weight. Cons: They are slightly loose on me but not in a falling off way, but just a bit below the waistband they are slightly bunched (due to mom pooch) - however my shirts fully hide this but for others with my body type, I wouldn’t wear my shirts tucked in. The other thing is the cut of the back pockets would look more professional if they were the horizontal slit-type (pocket hidden inside) as opposed to sewn on top jeans-type cut in a pentagon. Again, I wear longer loose tops with cardigans so it is not too noticeable. I just bought another pair in small long because I am for real going to get some more weight off and I fear that if I got down 5 lbs these will be baggy.

Moderately flared bell bottom, high waisted jean leggings with side and back pockets. The fabric is a blend of 57% cotton, 29% polyester and 14% spandex with a slightly distressed horizontal pattern that runs across the front of these pants in the pelvic area, as if to mimic natural creasing. They are generally stretchy, with a comfortable waist design that allows for ease of movement and good leg length on a 5'6" frame. The inseam on the left leg does rotate toward the front once past the knee; however, these pants are a dark charcoal color and with the flare at the bottom it is less obvious than it would be on a straight leg pant. The fabric is heavy enough to hold its shape and also provide some warmth for cool weather wear. I did run them in the dryer until just damp without any apparent shrinkage, although the label does advise a cool wash and line dry.

Most comfortable pair of pants. You can dress them up with heels or down flip flops. It actual does have the linen texture and feel. Elastic waistband is stretchy. Could probably fit 2 sizes up. My first pair is 2 years old and still good, but it got a bleach stain so I wanted another pair. They are light and loose, and I’d go easy on the washing as the material tend to thin out a little. Love them…you will too if you like comfy pants that you can wear out and still look nice even though they feel like you’re in Jammies. At 5’8” they fall right at my ankle and the are cut wide like the photo and drape well.

Most jeans give me muffin top, sag at the butt, slouch down with my active lifestyle. Eventually I have to wear a belt and my jeans look sad and worn after a few washes. Instantly after putting these on, I just knew. It was heaven! They somehow made my lower stomach/hip area feel snug and controlled, yet No muffin top. I practiced squatting around in them, did a few jumping jacks, etc. There was no shift or need for adjustment. I was amazed. Truly, I did not want to take them off. Felt like second skin but durable. I don't know what kind of magic is in these, but keep it coming! If I wash these a few times and they fall apart, I'll revise my review. But after raving to family about these and those who know about Democracy already, I have a hunch they'll stand the test of time. Okay, they're a little pricy, so start off with a pair that are on a good discount. So far, I can say I'd spend the retail price for the luxury of a good, flattering pant. My body type: 5’ 5” and 242# with short, thick legs and a belly. I’m not familiar with this type of sizing, so I went with a 38 Short (I usually wear a 20 short in “regular” sizes). These jeans feel like they were made just for me! The material is very soft and stretchy, and the bootcut isn’t too flared at the bottom. The front of the pants can be pulled up over my belly without riding too far up in the “front” (if you know what I mean). Also, the back of the jeans doesn’t gap out if you pull them up in the front which I LOVE! I have washed and dried these jeans which made them shrink a bit, but the stretchy fabric allows them to still fit and loosen up after a bit of wearing. Highly recommended for my shorter, bigger girls out there!!!

My daughter and I were debating whether to order these in my daughter’s regular size small or go down a size and order x-small. She is 5’5” 115 lbs and wears size small in jeans. According to the included chart these numbers put her in size small. However, her hip and waist measurements put her in size x-small according to the chart. Ultimately we decided to get size small because we have a couple of other pairs of jeans from this brand and size small in those fits her great. Unfortunately, these pants are too big for her in the waist and the hips. In the pictures I took the frontal view looks great but make sure to look at the back view picture too, so you can see how my daughter tightened them with a shoe lace to make them wearable. The fabric content according to the tag is 65% cotton 20% polyester 15% spandex. I’m not a fabric specialist by any means but 15% spandex? I have never seen jeans with such a high spandex content. And wouldn’t that make them very, very stretchy? These jeans however are not very stretchy. They are definitely not all cotton because they are soft for denim and have some give but I would not describe them as stretchy denim. All in all, the style is trendy, the workmanship is good, but size wise I wish they were running more true to the standard size small jeans.

Because of her height, they don't look low rise, like they do on the model, they sit a bit higher but in a flattering way. They are snug on her but look great and with the stretch, I think these will fit for quite a while. If they added some more sizes on, I'd buy some for myself.

My favorite outfit! I'm 5' 4" and always have to have pants hemmed, but not these. They fit perfectly for flat shoes, slightly short for boots. I've only worn it once and it hasn't pilled up, but there are a couple of spots it's starting to, which is why I gave it 4 stars and not 5.

I can’t believe what a great deal these are. $10, compared to another company my daughter sent me around the holidays for a gift idea, they were charging $29 + shipping for one pair of fleece lined leggings & only offer in 4 colors. Also super amazing delivery time 2-3days!I’m 5’3 150lb & they fit so amazingly perfect. I typically wear a 12 in Jrs which is the last size b4 being considered a plus size so I got the plus size just to be safe. I am not usually comfortable wearing anything skin tight. It’s not very flattering in my opinion, plus I tend to get overheated easily so the thought of something stuck to me when I’m a sweaty mess is not my idea of comfortable. So I can understand everyone’s obsession with everything leggings/jeggings, they’re so damn easy & u never will have to un-do ur top pant buttons ever again after u eat or feel bloated, they suck u in everywhere! They can be casual, workout gear, dressy with a tightly fitted sweater or blouse, even leggings Under a dress! “What’s not to like?” is my new motto. I don’t think they’re too hot to wear being fleece lined but it’s only 64 in PA. so that’s hard to tell just yet. I am a changed woman & still hold some reserve when & where u wear these,don’t think they should be acceptable funeral attire or for a job interview but with that being said-I plan on buying these in every available color I find , and my favorite part is all the millions of prints & patterns they Have out now. I would absolutely recommend, size is true to description as well as color! I did NOT receive any promotional codes, free clothes/product, browser traffic.

My husband gave me the thumbs up on these -- so that is a pretty strong recommendation in my book. But I am loving these jeans!!!⭐They are a little stretchy so they are super comfortable. The waist isn't cutting in and when I sit down the jeans move with me (and the strings of the cut out don't cut into my legs or cause weird fat rolls).⭐These are mid to high waist. They aren't mommy jeans, but they do come up to my belly button so they fully cover my tummy. When I sit, they don't slide down. As a fluffy mom, I hate when belly rolls pop out over the top of jeans -- no such issues with these.⭐SIZING: I am 5'5" and usually wear an 8/10 (L) in pants. I can wear these all day. They aren't slipping, but nor are they uncomfortable or tight in the waist. Love them!! For me, they are standard length jeans, going to right below my ankle. No adjustments necessary.⭐Zipper w/ single button front; Pockets - one in front at each hip and one in back over each bum cheek.I love jeans, but my fluffy body doesn't always agree with the fit. These are great -- comfortable, forgiving, a little stretchy, higher waist, and pretty flattering in my opinion. I 100% would recommend!!

My info: I am 5'9", 215 lbs, long legs, short torso, large tummy, normally a size 20 OR 2X. I had originally ordered the 20 Plus Long but they were baggy on me under the tummy and lower butt areas and the waist & legs areas were really loose, so I return those and ordered the 18 Plus Long. They are still a little baggy under the areas I mentioned above, which I would have to wear a long tunic to cover these areas. The waist & legs are more fitted but the length is PERFECT!! These are a true LONG as they reach exactly even with the bottom of my ankles. The fabric, IMHO, can be worn in ALL seasons; it's thick but not heavy thick, similar to jeans. The only negatives I have are, I wish there wasn't a zipper & buttons AND I wish they had belt loops. I like that, if I roll them up, they have jean-like coloring on the inside so they look very much like a real pair of jeans. I am 5"5", 139 pounds and got size medium. I also tried the size small and they were just too tight around my gut to be comfortable. The mediums fit really well around my stomach, hips, butt, and legs. I do have thin legs so these don't cling super tightly like a pair of high Spandex leggings but that's fine. That makes them look even more like real skinny jeans. My measurements: 5’4”, 38DD, 40” waist, 48” hips, 32” inseam. The XXL barely works. I wear this belt (30-35”) at my waist / stomach and I like the slightly wider appearance to balance my broad shoulders and bust in relation to my waist. Buy the size you are currently with no hesitation - it comes with 7 holes and a simple piercing tool to add additional notches if you ever lose weight, which was a pleasant surprise! I have absolutely nothing negative to say about it. I saw these Sweezarmo denim stretch joggers with an elasticized drawstring waistband and ordered them in my usual size small after consulting the company’s provided size chart. The Sky Blue color is represented accurately in the description photos, and the size runs true to the chart measurements. In person, the fabric looks like a true pair of denim jeans and to touch, feels like regular denim….with a little stretch. I was surprised at how much stretch they have in them. The weight of the denim is even close to what I’d expect for a pair of summer jeans. Prior to receiving them, I had some was concern about the waist having enough stretch, since my waist measurement is at least a couple inches larger than the posted measurement on the size chart. Sometimes elasticized waistbands can be constricting without much stretch, but that’s not the case with these. For those with a smaller waist, the drawstring is functional and can be cinched up for the right fit. On me, I feel the pants make my hips look big…but maybe some of that’s due to me being fuller in the waist!?! One feature I absolutely love about the pants is the pockets. Two in the front and two on the back. The front pockets are nice and deep for slipping hands or a cell phone in. I feel the pants are comfortable to wear and on the right figure (i.e. models in the listing photos) they look adorable! The quality of these is good from the materials used to the craftsmanship in the stitching. The pants are actually long enough that I’d be comfortable wearing them with sandals or flats without cuffing them, or rolling them up once or twice for an ankle or cropped look. Due to the thickness of the denim fabric, the elasticized waistband is a little bulky looking and feeling, so I think cropped tops or tops tucked in look the best with these bottoms. Overall I’m pleased with the denim pants (I personally don’t refer to them as joggers). They’re comfortable and wash up well, following care instructions. I will get wear out of them, but they won’t be bottoms I wear out and about often. They are so cute, but just not flattering on my body build. I appreciate the opportunity to try these, but in seeing how they look on me, I probably wouldn’t order them again for the price. But, for others that are considering them, I would recommend them for their quality and suggest staying true to size. For reference, my measurements are: Height - 5’4” Bust - 35” Waist - 30” Arm length - 22.5” Shoulder width - 15” Weight - 120# Inseam - 31”

My sis-in-law recommended Bandolino jeans. I was in need of nice, dark jeans for jean day at work, & although the description said high rise, I could tell by the photos that they weren't high rise -- which is great because I'm short from my waist to straddle. I ordered these in my normal 16. They fit well, almost a tad loose, & a bit loose in the rear (& at 180, 5'3", my rear is not tiny). I just ordered again in a 14 to see how they'll fit, & for when I drop a few pounds. :) They seem to be made well, & the regular length I ordered are good & long, but I'm sure they'll shrink a bit in length, even with the polyester. The buttonhole is very messy looking & tight. It was a struggle to unbutton & button back the jeans. Will trim it myself, then might have to sew over it to reinforce. All in all, they seem like very nice jeans. Will update after I've washed them, on whether or not they shrunk. I'm not expecting a huge amount of shrinkage, since they contain polyester, but will know in a day or two.

My stats: 5' tall, 114 pounds, 29 1/2 inch waist, 36 inch hips, 22 inch inseam; 60 years old, carry my weight in my abdomen and thighs. I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering. While I do not wear nor want to wear them with a shirt tucked in, I am attaching photos of the pants with my shirt tucked in so you can see the waistline, which also feels a little too thick for my tastes. An additional negative: It gives me a slight camel toe, of which I am constantly aware, though it's not so bad that I won't wear them. I am also attaching a photo of me in a pair of pocketless exercise pants so you can see what I look like without pockets poofing out.

My waist measurement (smallest point where I might wear a belt) is 31", which is at the top of the small size and low in the medium size, so I ordered both to compare. The small size is about 38" total length (not counting the buckle), and the medium is 43" inches long. The small belt definitely fits my small waist measure; I would be using a hole in the middle of the range. However, it would be way too small to fit lower on my hips, where I sometimes wear a belt (my hip measure increases to 41" ). The medium size belt is a touch too large for my 31" waist (shown in the photo) -- I will have to punch another hole. However, I will keep this one so that I can wear it lower on my hips as well. So my advice for sizing is to take into account where you wear your belt, and if you're near the ends of the measurement ranges, size up for versatility! Too big is better than too small since you can punch another hole, or I guess shorten the whole belt by removing the buckle.

My wife is disappointed in the Lee Women’s Sculpting Slim Fit Slim Leg Pull On Jean. They simply don't live up to the "sculpting slim fit” description. If anything, they do the opposite. My wife thought the stretchy waist would be comfy and slimming, but they feel more like maternity pants. They are loose around the abdomen and crotch and the size 8 is way too long on her 5'5 ½" frame. She has to wear heels to keep the hem from dragging. The back fits okay but it's not overly flattering or special in any way. The sizing seems way off. 8 medium is more like a 12 large. No thank you to vanity sizing in this instance. The material is smooth but not breathable. The stitching is decent in some areas but seems messy in other spots -– definitely not refined like lux jeans, so you're getting what you pay for. The overall style is unflattering and makes my wife feel weighed down and uncomfortable. These are jeans she probably won't wear again — she flat out dislikes them on many levels. I would agree that they do not bring out the best in her form and style and I noticed she had to keep pulling them up while we were shopping. ~ Kort

Neutral: I cannot speak to machine washing them, I hand wash. They recommend cold water. However, I do like to dry my pants in a dryer -- and that's not recommended with these. Drip drying these takes a mite bit of time. Yay! They fit and they feel comfortable. I'm built-low-to-the-ground 5'2" and ordered a Medium. They come down to within tolerable "high waters" to my ankles. For those who are taller, they will actually be capri length (mid-calf) The material is soft - not a hard denim fabric while still giving a denim look. The legs are roomy. Just the way I like them. Ugh! I am not enamored of the "raw hem". It's a raw edge, not a hem. And it's not a raveled edge out of the box, it's simple a cut edge. As I wash each time, I'll observe the ravel situation -- I'm not thrilled that it throws off little threads hither and yon. (minus 2 stars) Either finish it with an actual hem;or a stitched single line slightly above the raw cut. The way it is now is simply a straight unfinished cut across the fabric. The price-point is too high in my estimation for an unfinished raw cut that ultimately throws threads. The vender/manufacturer (whichever) calls it a rustic edgy look. I call it a way to cut manufacturing costs on offering a finished product. I would not give them as a gift, the concept feels low-quality to me -- which offsets their positive attributes.

I’m 5’3 and for size 4 and will wear a platform or heel so they don’t need hemmed. The back is elastic band so no gap back there and they fit my waist and are loose on my bottom which is a bit hard to do in most pants. Four real pockets! 95% poly. 5% elastane so flowy, but a cotton-like feeling too. This pair is really quality made. The pocket pick is a better representation of the shade of olive green. Will machine wash well and steam to perfection!

Normally when I buy jeans, I spend 20-30 minutes crying in front of the mirror in a self loathing kind of way. I’ve suffered from BDD since I can remember and pants, jeans especially, have always been my biggest nemesis. All that being said… sweet baby Jesus these jeans are the freaking epitome of comfort. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 130lbs and haven’t seen the inside of a gym in probably 10 years and my ass is clearly nonexistent — I purchased the Maui Views (Waterless) in a size (4) 27 Short. Unpopular opinion but I detest high waisted jeans. I’m “short torso - long leg” so hard pass on anything that even gets near my belly button. I was nervous about the rise of these but am so glad I took the chance because they truly sit just perfectly under my belly button and at my waist. I’ve never felt so comfortable and honestly, like myself, in a pair of jeans. I could not recommend a pair more than these. *only hiccup in my purchase was that the original pair claimed to have been delivered but was in fact not. Amazon customer service quickly rectified that and sent a replacement pair overnight.

Not a huge fan of these pants. 1. They are not what you would consider linen. When you think of linen you think of a thin, "flowy", wrinkly, lightweight, and breathable summer pant. These are none of these things...they are more like rather stiff chinos with texture. The description pictures definitely paints the picture of linen but it is deceiving and misleading in my opinion. 2. The pants went all the way down to the ground when I thought they would be above the ankles per the description picture (again, very misleading...this model must have been 5'11" or taller!!). I had to roll them 4 times to get them "cropped" looking and then they just looked weird. I am 5'6" and 190 pounds so I am not super short and I got size Large. I expected more for pants that are $35. I will wear them to work but they won't be my go to summer beach pant sadly :(

NOTE: I am a member of the Amazon Vine Program. I try out new products, and then provide an honest, unbiased review, based on my personal impressions and experience. I am not paid for my reviews. I love these jeans. They are so comfortable and true to size. Flattering to my curves, but still stylish. I'm 5'6", so it's hard to see the "crop" effect in the pic I've posted with this review, but they fall just above my ankles. I've gotten lots of wear out of them already this summer, because they go with everything, and it's so easy to dress them up or dress them down. Excellently constructed, and comparable to any pair of jeans I would find in a high-end department store, but for half the price. Highly recommend!

NOTE: I don't typically wear shirts tucked in, but for this review I felt I needed to show the fit better. Don't judge too harshly! :) Size Reference: Ordered XXL. I am 5'5", 180 pounds, a 35" waist and 46.5" hips. Weight is carried in my hips and rather large backside. I highly recommend checking the size picture before ordering. I ordered the XXL according to my largest measurement, my hip size. I always find I have a waist gap anyway, so I expected a bit of gap here. Lately I have been struggling more to find pants that fit my hips without highlighting every single fat globule under my skin. If I had realized how stretchy the denim was, I would have ordered an XL. There is so much stretch at the front right at the belly area that it just gathers there. When I sit down I have a big denim poof sticking up. The back has the expected gap, but the thighs fit nicely. There are no belt loops, so there is no cinching the waist in like that. I will likely sew in some darts at the back to help with that. The stretch is so stretchy that I don't even have to unbutton these to take my pants off. I just pulled them right off, no problems. Hopefully giving my measurements and showing pictures of my body proportion will help you as you look to order the right size. I love how soft and stretchy they are, but I definitely needed a smaller size!

Now I am about 160-165 lbs and 5’5. I love the high waist of the jeans! Knowing they were flare jeans which I usually don’t get because of my shape... thought I’d give these a try. They are SUPER cute but I personally don’t think this type of jean flatters me... Feel as if they make me look shorter

Now I got these beautiful pants to wear to work but you can definitely play with this look. You can put a crop top on with it and some sneakers and it’s a look. I got a large and the waist was perfect . The pleats gave me thigh room without looking baggy. The color is rich and can go with any color. I’m a color girl so these pants give me plenty to play with. The fabric is not heavy. But I did wear them in 30 degree weather in Michigan…. I wasn’t outside long, my car to the door but they were fine. I don’t think these are tall girl friendly, they hit me perfect and I am 5’1.

Now that summer is over it’s already time to start bundling up. These jeans kept me warm all night in 40F weather! The medium fits very well around my waist and I usually wear a size 4 pants. The cons are the button and knees. The button seems very loose like it may pop off at some point in the suture. The knees are also on the looser side and I’m not a fan of the baggy-ness so I cuffed the bottom to try and make them look a little more fitting. These are very stretchy like jeggings which is a great plus because I could bend in them with no problem. And the softness inside is so much better than expected! It’s a very thin layer of fur rather than some scrappy fuzz inside like the old navy pair I own. The fabric itself is thick and good quality. They really do insulate and provide warmth! I could easily wear a pair of leggings under them for extra cold days. Overall I would recommend these to anyone looking for some warmth!

Now, this review is being written based on initial try-on. I haven’t worn them for any amount of time, so I can’t speak to longevity. For reference I’m a heavy woman. 5’5”, short legs, 240 lbs. The size 18 was perfect, altho a tad long. But that’s ok. I like to cuff anyway. These are extremely comfortable and I plan on buying more pairs.

Objectively a good product, they look really sturdy and well-made. The fuzzy lining is not as thick as I’ve seen on other pants, but is still heavier than a standard fleece-lined pair. Would probably do a great job of keeping you toasty. I’ll never know, sadly, because I ordered the largest size, L-XL, and they are way too small. Absolutely size up when ordering, because in no world would these count as an XL. They look to me what is typically called M. I am 5”2’ and about 170lbs, a L/XL usually fits comfy on me. Not this time. I will be returning. As long as you are able to order a size larger than normal, I would recommend these.

I’m very, very happy with these jeans, I skipped and moonwalked down the hallway!!

OK here’s the real deal on this shirt, after reading multiple reviews. I am 5‘7“ and 125 pounds. Someone else my weight/height bought an XS and said it was perfect, and you can see in her pic, her sleeves are too short. I am a true small most of the time (or med.) and this shirt is the right fit in my size. I wanted something more fitted, this shirt has princess seams on the back. Yes it smells a little funny, so wash it! The mid blue is the perfect color for a slightly faded vintage look. The fabric isn’t true denim which it shouldn’t be as a shirt, it’s more like a lighter chambray, but it has the look of denim, which is great for me, I didn’t want it stiff. You can wear it open as a shacket, but it won’t provide much warmth. I wanted to wear it as a shirt. It’s 100% cotton so I expect a little shrinkage, but to me, it would fit even better. [Update: washed/dried, it shrank a bit and fits better! Mostly sleeve and shirttail length.] If you want it more oversized, I would suggest going up as size. I love fashion and can’t wait to style this in endless ways! It’s SO the classic that belongs in every woman’s closet, that’s why they call it wrangler retro!. The pocket placement and size is perfect for us bustier girls.

OK so first things first, I feel like some reviews need a but of a reminder that these are inexpensive Amazon shorts. They arent BAD quality, just don't be expecting the nicest of zippers or buttons. I have huge thighs compared to by waist (I wish that looked as good as it sounds but I promise it does not). My thighs will pull up any shorts at all unless I go full out knee length which I am not doing. Finding shorts to fit my thighs is near impossible. This will be my third pair. The stretch and little side slit helps immensely. For size, I got a medium. I'm about 175 5'6" 28" waist 43" hips. I can't see the size chart right now but I think that put me at an xl. After reading the reviews I kept seeing "i sized down and would have been fine doing so again". And if you have big thighs you know you have to err on the side of a little tight or you get that excess bulge of fabric in the middle that's not a good look. These fit super comfortably. As you can see, more on the snug side but not tight. They have plenty of give. If you don't have the spare fabric when you sit issue you can size down just one I think. Kinda wish there were options without lots of fringe that didn't involve cuffs (those REALLY don't work with thighs), because I'm not 13 anymore, but honestly I'm gonna trim it off, replace the buttons, and these will be worn a lot. Overall 10/10, I'm happy.

I have hips and long legs so the medium is a little tight in the hips (but still wearable) and a little too short in the length as these are boot cut so they’re supposed to hang longer. I actually might have needed a size UP!!! I cut the hem out of the bottom to try to gain some length but they’re still slightly too short for my liking. The other thing that pissed me off is that I bought them for $19.99 and then the next day they were $14.99. I’d say they’re worth $14.99 but had I tried them on in a store I wouldn’t have bought them for $20.

Ok, first of all, these are authentic. All pockets are real pockets, and they are extremely well made. I'm 5'0", 108 lbs, and I bought the 27s. The fit is PERFECT, but I will definitely need to have them hemmed. For now I've been folding the excess pant leg up and under, and it works just fine, because the fit is slim enough that it stays in place all day without budging.I can't gush enough about how thrilled I am to have found these jeans. The high rise pant trend needs to die. It's not really flattering on anyone, but it is murder on us short girls. At only 5'0", even "low rise" jeans come up so high on me that the waist band always covers my belly button. Some "low rise" jeans come up so high on my torso I can practically tuck my tits into them. Not only is it crazy unflattering, but it's incredibly uncomfortable. The jeans always dug into my stomach when I sat down. For years I have had to rearrange the waistband of my jeans every time I sat. I so missed the 90's style, "sit so low on your hips you flash your crack every time you sit" jeans. They were so much more comfortable and flattering on me! I've been searching for years, but couldn't find anyone that still makes a true "sits on the hips" jean. And then I found these! I couldn't believe it when I put them on. After trying on hundreds of pairs of "low rise" jeans that came up to above my belly button, these actually sit right at my hip bone, right where they should. And unlike the 90's low rise jeans, these fit so well they don't have that weird gap in the back of the waistband when you sit, and they don't flash your undies. They stay put. They are AMAZING. So flattering and comfortable.The only slight drawback is that the back pockets do sit a pretty low, so they're not the most flattering butt jeans ever. And if you're someone who is constantly shoving your phone in your back pocket like I am, the pocket flap is not the most convenient thing ever. But those are minor issues for me, considering how overall thrilled I am with these. After the first pair arrived and I saw how well they fit I immediately ordered two more pairs, and they are equally amazing. Love 'em.

Ok, I ended up buying two different sizes, & since they didn't have the large in the same color, I got it in a lighter blue instead. For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & 138lbs & have what they say is more of an 'hourglass' figure with an apx. 29-30" waist & apx. 40" hips and the darker blue pants are the Med. & the lighter are the large! The med. fits more like a small in the waist, that's why I decided to try the large. I was thinking it'd be perfect, but it fits more like an XL! Odd to say the least! I can't decide yet which I will be keeping, so thought I'd post this with BOTH so you could see for yourself & hopefully it will help you decide. The colors are oddly close, & pretty close to true in these pics.. This is how they look right out of the package, so yes, they are very wrinkled! I have no doubt though, being as I've dealt with this type of material before, that once washed & hung or dried, they will be fine! Worst case, nothing an iron won't fix! :-) I love the flowyness (if that's even a word LOL) of the pants, but prefer the large for this! Just a little more like a skirt with that size, which is what I like better! Love the flirty aspect of the slit in the front too! It's on both sides, but I just posted the pic of the one. I like the length better on the large too, as the Med. just seems a little TOO short for my taste! But, I actually prefer the darker blue?!!? So I'm torn!! I think I will end up keeping the lighter Large & just take in the elastic some so it'll fit me better & return the Med.! The waist is mostly elastic, except for the very front where it has the tie, so it's on the forgiving side for fit which is nice if you fluctuate in weight like I do! I tend to go up or down 5-10 lbs now & then! LOL Nicely made though, and is priced ok, so I would recommend. They have some nice detail that other pants like this don't which is a huge plus for me! The material is nice & light weight & perfect for spring/summer, but then they could just as easily be paired with nylons for those colder times! Love that they can be either causal or dressy too! Very versatile! I'm always on the lookout for those kinds of buys! Especially now when our dollar isn't going as far! The material has a sort of texture to it to which is kind of nice! Not something that is distracting though! All & all, they are cute, & I like them! LOVE that they come in so many colors too! I just might have to get another couple of these in other colors soon!! Only wished they had a royal blue and/or a hot pink! Maybe they will hear me and add some?! Well....hope this helps! Thanks much! ENJOY!

OK, I got these 2 days ago, and I just tried them on. I bought a large, because I am partially paralyzed so I have one working hand to pull them on. They fit perfectly! ok, i was pleasantly surprised with these. I’m 5’10” and ordered the tall length and they are long enough. i’d love for them to be about a .5” longer but i feel like this was a great deal and the pants fit well. my only complaint is that there are only two belt loops on the pants - both on the front. not sure why that is, but other than that i’m satisfied!

Ok, I’m SHOCKED by how much I love these jeans!! For reference, I’m a very athletic build- 5’4” 39-27-39 measurements, very thick thighs. Buying jeans can be a nightmare. I recently purchased some $150 jeans designed for athletic builds and they don’t stack up to these. The fabric is medium-thick so it smooths you out and holds you in, with a really nice amount of stretch. Sizing- according to the size chart I should have been in a 29 or 30 with a 27 inch waist and 39 inch hips, however I’m a 4/27 probably 90% of the time in other jeans so I just went ahead and purchased my usual size (27) and it’s a perfect fit. I will absolutely be back for more colors!!

Ok, ladies! Especially my BBW sistas. This jacket is the truth! I love denim jackets and have about 12 of them. Different colors, of course. I like the way this one hangs and feels on me. I have big upper arms, a large belly, large breasts, and an apple shape. It was snug across my stomach when I buttoned it, but who button's a denim jacket? It's a fashion faux pas! Although it looks snug on my upper arms, it did not feel snug. It is very comfortable and not heavy. I have worn with dresses, jeans, leggings, and even rocked them with a skort. I usually wear a 5X in tops so I purchased the 6X. Just for reference...I am 5'8" tall and 350+ pounds (other details mentioned earlier. Lol). All of my other denim jackets were purchased from stores that specialize in the BBW. This jacket stands right next to them! Maybe two steps ahead because of its affordable price. I quickly ordered the other three colors. I highly recommend these denim jackets. Try it. Listen, Amazon Essentials is showing out! Price, fit, and quality are all on point! Cheap quality just doesn't look good on me. Loving them, for sure!

Ok, so first things first, I noticed alot of people in the reviews complained about the size. These are a rigid denim, which means NO stretch, they are true to size,so if you're normally a 30, but wear a 28 in stretchy jeans...order the 30. I'm 4'11" and around 115 and the 24 fits like a glove!! There are no gaps at the waist, I love the cute little detail at the bottom of the leg, and your butt will never look better than in a pair of 501's!! These jeans are beautiful with adorable distressing and the 5 button fly I've come to love in a pair of Levi's jeans. I had these in my cart ans caught them for $20 on sale and wish I had ordered a few more. These are a great fit with a straight leg, Snug through the hips and thighs. You're going to love these jeans!!

The fabric is good for summer nights to winter weight. These pants have very little stretch which is ok with me BUT if my hips get wider I may have a problem pulling them up to my waist. OK, these are super soft and stretchy and they fit mid to high waist just right! They might be a little loose in the waist. I’m 135 pounds and got a medium I’m also 5’5 and I will say that the pants are a little short but when you do the scrunched bottoms it’s the perfect length but if you don’t want the scrunch bottoms, I will say it’s like a half inch inch too short. Ok, well...since there were no reviews, I wasn't sure about these, but thought I'd give them a try! I have to say I was surprised with how comfy they are! VERY Stretchy, so they are VEEEEERY forgiving size wise just wished they were a wee LONGER!! I went with their measurements & this is a Large! For reference I'm apx 5'7" & 135lbs with an apx 29-30" waist & 40ish" hips. You can see how MUCH it stretches at the waist in the pics! That does make them feel nice on though!! I would have sent them back & went with a med, but I am afraid that would be too short then, so I'm mulling over just sending them back?! Haven't decided yet though!? They seem well enough made, & at prices as they are, they seems reasonable I suppose?! I like the high waist & button fly! Hubby liked them, so heck, might keep them!? LOL They ARE comfy! They'd be great fishing jeans for sure as they give when you bend & move!! They do have a strange odor though right out of the package, that I HOPE will go away after washing! Kind of a petroleum type smell?!? NOT pleasant at all though, but maybe due to the hot weather & being in plastic? But, I'm sure washing will help! All & all they are nice & would recommend. I'd say the sizing is pretty much spot on, so pay attention to that & you should be fine! Hope this helps! :-) Thanks much!

Ok... first, I gotta say: these pants are super cute and comfy. I have always had a harder time shopping for pants, because of my extremely curvy figure. Usually, if it fits my hips, it's too big for my waist. These hug me in all the right places. I am 5'7" 270lbs with 55" hips! My shape is in between hourglass and pear. Typically, in stretch pants, I wear a size 2x to 3x depending on the brand. In jeans I wear a size 24. So, I purchased these in 4x. They are just right (provided they don't shrink in the dryer!). I cannot comment on durability, as I only got them today and only tried them on to see if they fit and take pics for your review. If anything changes I'll update. However, right now I do recommend these and now I think I want to get a couple other colors, as well. Update: they did not shrink in the dryer on standard low heat. The only thing that I don't like is the elastic bad twists, however they still seem to lay flat once I get them pulled up. So far I still like them and will probably order other colors in the near future.

Okay so first off I didn’t pay for expedited and it still came the next day! When it says earliest delivery date, most times Amazon pushes through and it’s COVID-19 so I’m shocked. I am 6 foot I weigh 280 pounds and I’m thick thighs no butt. For the thick thighs women with no butt is best to get your exact size because we want to also flatter our butts just as much as are thighs. If your thighs are bigger than your butt or vice versa to an extreme amount probably get one size bigger but it may be a little loose. I like tighter fit jeans in my thigh area to complement my hips and thighs also because I have a small butt once again lol! And you also know the more you wear jeans they stretch. So I know that my thighs will start to make room for themselves if I need any more LOL.These are perfect. They stretch but you can tell There’s some denim in it because it doesn’t have an extreme stretch. These are not jeggings! They are the regular polyester plus size material for big girls I just have jeans are being right now with a little stretch but I still denim feel! I don’t mind that lip space under the abdomen because I know my thighs are the reason why! Also it goes all the way down ! No high water !!!!

Okay so my view of these and how I feel about them. Pairs bought:size 14 black, size 15 black.Material Contents: 98%cotton 2%Spandex each pair. I'm 5'6" 185-190lb on my heavy side rn, w/meas. of 38",33",46". Size 13-14 is my usual size currently. These in sz.14 are what I have on in all 4 pics.The waist fits me alright, the shorts as a whole feel like they suck me up with moderate belly/flab control, and is slightly forgiving with a small gap in the back as pictured. Although they're 98%cotton there is almost no breathability and very little stretch in the fabric and waist. The crotch is on the tighter side for me even though they have a longer 7" inseam, the cut is weird through the groin area and I wouldn't want to wear these in a hot humidity environment. Similar to how others have stated, I can see how these won't hold their shape after wear, they wrinkle easily and would have to be ironed even after the dryer, they flare/bow out at the bottom sides, and they are soft in the inside and outside however any kind of marks show up on this type of material especially since it's a darker shade of black. The front pockets are of decent sizing, can be difficult ti access with the shorts tighter around the hip and waist, and the back pockets exist except they're sewn in of which can be carefully cut out and removed. They are naturally snug around my problem area where my belly pooch is however this isn't the case with my other Lee shorts sz14. These are a nice casual short that I'd wear to a business-casual event outside or preferably inside where extrenuous movement wouldn't be involved although these would be my last choice if nothing else was available--this type of short is either for a unique body type, someone who doesn't mind the flaws, or the flaws work for them. Returning and on the hunt again.Happy Shopping y'all 😀

Okay these might be tricky. I NEVER believed I could find a pair of jeans that fit my waist (usually a size 6 I just got back from vacation) and my butt and thighs which are an 11. Okay wow! I love this jumpsuit. Fabric is comfortable and light. I’m 5’1 and 125 ibs and a small fits perfectly. My only complaint is the weird smell it has coming out of the packaging. But it looks and fits great!

Okay y’all first off all when I opened up this package I thought, “there is no way in hell my ass is squeezing into these pants.” Let me just say, these fit like a dream! The fit is super flattering, the waist is nice and high, the pants have a good bit of stretch and the length is not killer! For reference I am 5’2, 160lbs, measurements are 39 34 41 (last I checked at least). Bought these for a 1970s roller disco party we are throwing for my husband and they are PERFECTTTTT. The tag says 90% cotton so be prepared for shrinkage if you place in the dryer; even on a gentle or low heat cycle I’m sure. I’m attaching two photos of the front and rear of the fit of the jeans and in the photos I have just taken them out of the box, no washing yet.

Okay, my fellow tall ladies! These pants are a must! Make sure you get tall and not long. I'm 6ft and the legs go for days. I can finally wear heels without pulling my pants legs down a million times hoping I will stretch the fabric enough to stay lol. I'm ordering every color now ❤

Okay, first of all, BUY THIS... you won’t regret it! I’m 5’9”, 215lbs with thick legs and 36DDD, I’ usually between a L and XL (12-14), but I sized up two sizes to a 2xl. Okay, I'm short (5'3"), so these are not "capri" on me, which is fine--I'm used to it. I can hem them. I like the style of these and the fabric is a nice stretchy cotton, a bit thinner than expected. I wouldn’t say they’re super “stretchy”, but they do have some stretch to it. I usually wear an 8 now since gaining weight, but I purchased a size 6 because it was cheaper lmfao. Just get your normal size, and it should fit. It’s for sure true to size.

Okay, let me start by telling you that every time I wear these pants I get a lot of compliments, EVERY SINGLE TIME! These are my favorite pants because they are the perfect length for my height and style all together! I am 5'6" and 133lbs, size small. Ordered a small/31" inseam in black and it could not have been more perfect! VERY happy with it!

Okay, my quick review photos definitely do not do these justice, but I did wear them out recently and I got a bunch of compliments! The fit is great, the color is the perfect shade of burnt / rust orange, and they are super comfortable and fun! I got a size large because based on the size chart I probably could have gotten a medium but I wanted them a little on the looser side and I think they're perfect. For reference, I am 5'6" and currently around 160 lbs. Of course the straps are adjustable and it has the buttons on the side to help get them on over the hips, although I can get them on and off easily without undoing any of the buttons. Ooh also they have a hidden inner button on either hip so you can expand the waist another couple inches if you need, which is a nice feature! The overalls themselves feel very well made with sturdy buttons and seams. The fabric is maybe a little thinner than your standard jean denim, but I personally like that they are a little lighter. They feel more like a twill pant material than true thick denim, but they still feel like decent quality, and most importantly they are comfortable! I honestly feel like these will become my go-to outfit this fall and I can't wait to wear them again and again!

Okay, so first things first: I am a size 6 (most places) and I always need to order Short because I’m petite at 5’4”, 135 pounds. I like my flares to hit right around mid foot with no shoes. I so ordered a Standard 6 Short for both pair. That said, the length on the black pants are PERFECT. However, they are longer than the light indigo, even though they both say 30. That was a bit disappointing since the indigo flares are actually too short for my liking. So for that, I took off 1 star. They didn’t have any left of the regular length so I’m keeping them and will wear them with flats. Second, these jeans are FLATTERING. A lot of jeans are not but these fit in all the right places. I used to be die hard American Eagle as far as jeans go but now I’m done bc of these jeans. I didn’t know jeans could look this good. I know someone said these aren’t real Levi’s but who cares? They’re amazing. I do wish the holes in the light colored pair were placed on the actual knee but it’s fine. Overall, fantastic purchase.

Okay, so I actually really love these! However, one star deduction for not having a size chart anywhere—that drives me crazy, since clothes can vary so much within the S,M,L etc. sizing. I am usually between a small/medium in pants, size 28 roughly because I’m newly postpartum. Anyhow, I opted to get the medium because I didn’t want it to be too tight in the waist. Well… I could’ve gotten the small probably, and would’ve preferred those as I’m still losing weight. The medium DOES work for me still, it’s just a bit bigger and baggier. I think it does work with the current style of looser jeans, so I can make it work. However, as I said, a small might’ve been a bit better fitting. There isn’t a whole lot of stretch, maybe the teeny tiniest bit in the waist, but not enough to be labeled stretchy pants by any means. The makeup is 75% cotton, and the rest was viscose and polyester. They feel like medium weight denim, fairly good quality. I was actually super impressed with the color/pattern. Sometimes leopard patterns can look so incredibly cheap if not done correctly. These do not, in my opinion. They can be dressed up or down easily. Also, I am 5’7” and they go to about my ankle, a great length in my opinion. They are high waisted as well. The barrel look was the perfect amount, not overly barrel-y, but enough to clearly be barrel jeans. I found them to look quite flattering on me. Overall they felt quite comfortable! I am happy with them, I just wish I had gotten the small, but honestly I might buy the small because I like these enough to have a second pair! They also came way faster than I expected. It said it would take several weeks but came within about a week and half, so I’m super happy with that as well! Now if they could just get a size chart and actual product photos, I think these would sell out.

Okay, so I bought these pants because I needed them for a work/volunteer gig. I had high expectations because I have had other Lee pants and I really want them to fit properly. I'm 5'3 and 110-113lbs, not exactly the curviest person around. I ordered a 4-Short. So...they do fit at the waist. Perfectly, I might add. Not tight, but definitely not loose. Very comfortably, exactly like how I think a pants should fit. However, they are both VERY baggy on my relatively skinny legs and a little bit long even though I bought the short version. When I put it on, I loved how comfortable they were, but disappointed how baggy and unkept they looked on me. I would get a 2, but I doubt the waist would fit. For my short queens (those sub 5'3), you might want to look elsewhere. I have a feeling they'd look rather long on y'all. They are otherwise brilliant pants and great Lee quality. Such a bummer.

Okay. So. Sequins are kind of my thing. I'm a singer and have been wearing sequined apparel for 10+ years. I'm familiar with all the common issues (improper inner lining so it's scratchy, sequins coming loose and getting snagged, falling off, bending, etc). In many cases, pants in this style are EXTREMELY scratchy due to thin lining and are highly unpleasant to wear. Not the case here - they are soft, stretchy, and comfortable enough to wear for hours (which is usually how long I'll need to wear them for so that's good!) Additionally, most of the time sequined pants highlight my legs in ways I'm not a fan of, as I'm on the curvier side. They usually make me look heavy and short. Due to the flare, these are very flattering and will look great with a cute top or cropped blazer. The only issue is that they're long. I'm 5"1' so most pants are obviously going to be too long on me. These are probably a good 3-5 inches too long and unfortunately it won't be remedied with high heels. However, I'm so in love with the way these look that I'm planning to get them hemmed so I can wear for gigs - it's worth it!

On first try-on I LOVE these jeans! So much! They’re the first jeans that really fit me since pregnancy. But shortly after wearing them, the flaws really started to show: 1- the belly cover is nude/skin color (why??) it makes it so you absolutely have to have a long shirt to cover it, or it looks really weird 2- the jean area barely comes up enough to cover your crotch area. I don’t know why they didn’t bring the jeans line up higher on my stomach, it would have made it easier to wear with normal shirts. And when I sit down, it says low on my bottom too. Not super flattering. 3- they don’t stay up very well. With the stretchy fabric, and the jeans not coming up very high, they just sag a bit. This is definitely not a deal breaker, just a side note. Overall these jeans fit SO good on my legs, and made me feel happy to wear jeans again. I’m currently 140 lbs (120 pre pregnancy) and I’m 6 months pregnant. If you have lots of long shirts, it’s definitely worth it to have nice jeans! I probably would have gone with the dark wash next time, as the stretchy material matches the jean color better. I’m sad that the medium color has a nude stretchy belly, but maybe they can change that in the future.

One of my favorite purchases to date. I’m 5’2”, 155lbs, 38DDD, & carry my weight in my belly post-baby. It’s super duper comfortable and can easily be dressed up or down. I love that it covers enough that I could wear a tank top with it and still be modest.

One of the appeals to this style is the wide legs. For women like me, with rather thick thighs, these pants are liberating and offer much comfort. These did not disappoint in that aspect. But overall, the length was a huge drawback (reduce a star). They were like 5 inches too long. I attempted to roll the waist band to reduce the length but they were still too long and doing so caused the pants to bunch in the rear end. I attempted to roll them from the bottom at no avail; the cuff would not stay. Nevertheless, the feel was super soft with rather shallow pockets for such a relaxed style (reduce a star).I did order my normal size 12. For reference, I am 5'6" at 175lbs with a waist of 35". I felt like the elastic would help to frame my waist, but I wish I had ordered a smaller size because I did not feel any curve was sightly in these pants.

One thing I would say is Amazon recommended I get an eight and I followed the recommendations because they had my measurements correct at 5‘8“ and 145 pounds. But the eight Was Way too large. I’m going to exchange the eight for the 66 fits perfectly, they’re super comfortable and feel very good quality for the Price point. I ordered the black and a six and they’re great the one complaint other than size I had about the blue pair I ordered and an eight was they smell kind of toxic I don’t know if it’s the dye or if it’ll wash away But it has a very strong odor compared to the black pair I got. Hopefully the pair I exchange it for will not have that same intense smellu

Only buy these jeans if you are into the distressed/worn look. Unfortunately, I am not. I wish the photo would have shown there would be holes, because I wouldn't have bought them. I actually really like the fit and think they are super comfortable. I am 5'9, 150lbs and the size 8 fits perfectly. With the hem rolled down at the bottom, they come about 1 inch from the top of my ankle. I definitely would have kept them if it weren't for the rips/tears. I prefer new jeans that aren't pre-worn out and ragged looking. But hey, if that's your jam, you'll love these.

Only downside for me, is have a steamer handy because this fabric wrinkles easily. It feels kind of silky. Lot of perks though- it’s very breezy and comfortable and makes for a cute outfit! For reference, I’m 5’6” and bought a size large which fits comfortably (I normally buy a large in pants). With the waistband resting at my belly button, the pant legs land at my feet, but I wear wedges which help lift them a bit. Each pant leg is about the size of my body width and the waistband is very stretchy. If you are shorter than 5’6 you might need to get the pant legs hemmed.
Only giving 4 stars because the short length is almost too short. and that’s coming from someone who is 5’2! Other than that, I love them! I’m a size 8, 175 lbs, and ordered a large.

Oooh these jeans are so fun. I love the fit and feel. The jean material has a decent stretch and did not come with any funky odors. I did wash in medium heat and machine dried. These jeans fit perfectly in length and size in an x-small. I didn't have to roll the bottoms as they fall right to my ankles, but felt like it while wearing my huaraches. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small fits me best. So, do order to size. All the stitching and sewing is done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. I do recommend these jeans. I'm really loving the cut.

Order the size 2 regular. For reference my measurements are 24.5" waist and 35" hip. The jeans fit perfectly around my waist without needing a belt which is a rare occurrence for me. Besides the sizing, however, the jeans are way over priced and the color is off. As you can see, I ordered the "regular blue" yet, the actual color that they came as is not as blue as pictured and actually seems like what the "light blue" option looks like. The material is also not real denim, so there is no vintage look to them at all. The material is like a jegging: super stretchy. Also, the button is really flimsy and seems like it will fall off easily. I returned them because $46 dollars for these is over priced.

Order up a size or two, I ordered an up one size to an XL and they’re still a little snug. Luckily they have a good stretch. I like them and will probably but another paid but go up to an XXL. For reference I am 5’7, 185 lbs and curvy. Length on these pants are perfect

Order way up. I ordered a 1x/2x, here's why. I read many of the reviews carefully and referenced their size chart. I was reading... too small, annoying stripes, etc. That's because these are small. They are super stretchy, but have a snug waistband. They are surprisingly slimming... if you order right. I am 5'5" and 150 and a 3-c-section belly. I usually wear size medium or size 8-10. This is why I ordered the 1\2x and they fit absolutely comfy and amazing. I am not the type that orders small and tries to stretch to the max. I'd rather order big and be extremely comfortable. These have no negatives on any part of my body for ordering big and no overly stretchy lines. I just think their size chart and measurements are like Japanese and European that run extremely small and make us feel fat. Lol. I hope this helps. I will absolutely order more colors and in the bigger size.

Order your true size, don’t size down. Man, I wish I hadn’t listened to the reviews saying to size down because they thought these ran big. I’m 5’6”, 140#, and normally a size medium (28 in Levi’s) bottoms for reference. I ordered these shortalls in a size small so that they wouldn’t be too big, per the suggestion of other buyers. But unfortunately, they are more fitted than I prefer. I would have been much happier with these in my regular size. They are still wearable and fit like a true small, so I can’t fault the size chart or the garment. These just aren’t going to be as comfy casual on me as they would have been in a medium. I’m still living out my 90s glory days in these regardless. Just trust in Levi’s size chart and order your true size to get the proper vintage fit.

Orderd a medium im 5.4" and weigh140lb. Its not a light weight fabric its more of a jean material and i love it well made.

Ordered a Large and they fit perfect. For reference: I’m 5’5 150lbs size 8 in jeans. Super cute, comfy, and light weight!

Ordered a Large: I finally bit the bullet and bought these as I have outgrown all of my prepregnancy jeans (23 weeks). So far, they are perfect and fit great. I do worry they will stretch with continuous wear and unfortunately they are too short on me (which I expected as I'm 5'11) but all in all, very comfortable and good quality.

Ordered a medium and small in Apricot just to compare sizes. I am 5’2 about 135 pounds. The small fit, but was a little snug especially with the elastic and they didn’t sit slouchy at the bottom like I expected. The mediums are perfect in length, stretch, and size. Super cozy and not suffocating at all. Neither were severely see through either. Smalls are the first photo, medium is second. I am keeping the mediums — exactly as expected!
Ordered a medium, and it fit great. I’m 5 ft 1 with smaller waist and bigger thighs and this skirt fit like a glove.

Ordered a medium, based on size chart, and these high-waisted baggy cargo pants fit nicely at the waist. They have elastic on the back. I am a short 5'-er, so these are long on me (no fault of the product) but I'm okay with that. They are definitely wide-legged. At first, I wasn't sure about it, but I think I am good with the width. I like the feel of the material. It's soft, has a nice weight to it. Love the pockets--big.

Ordered a size 16 short. I’m 5’0” and usually wear a size 16. This are just long enough for me to wear heels with. I love the slightly oversize fit without it being too baggy. Very comfortable and great quality
Ordered a Size S but probably could’ve have gotten an XS. I’m 5’2”, 115lbs. Grear fit, Baggy is trending right now. Has a Side Zip on right side. Pockets in front & back. Very comfy!

Ordered a size small and absolutely love this skort!! I am 145 lbs and wear a size 4/6. Super stretchy and comfortable. I did have to hold the back to get it zipped over my butt, but that was only because I was nervous about the reviews saying the zipper wasn’t great. 100% would order again or in a different color.
Ordered a size small in tan and size medium in black for different styles but both fit super cute! I’m 5’5 and size 27-28 in jeans.

Ordered a size small, am 5'7" 148lbs, 36b, 37"-30"-40". Fits well & comfortably. Very high quality satin, slippery and was very comfortable all night and well into the next day. Entire set is flowy and loose, as you may know satin is not particularly forgiving, but I am satisfied with the size I got. However, it is a bit tight innthe chest-I prefer my clothes fitted usually so and it is in no way restrictive or uncomfortable-that being said, if you're at all bigger than me in the chest, I do recommend you seek a size above. The color is gorgeous, and the whole set is just very high quality and finely made. Sleek and elegant
Ordered a size small. Next day delivery. 5’9, 125 pounds. Super comfy and well worth the value and will be ordering in other colors.
Ordered a small in white since I already had the dark denim. Fits great. I’m 5’1” and 100 lbs. Should have ordered this brand in the first place in white. Wrangler just didn’t fit well.

Ordered a small in white. I’m 5’3 and 120lbs. Being so short a wide leg makes me look shapeless and frumpy so I like that these are tapered but it isn’t too tight and still gives a nice relaxed look. Fabric isn’t stiff & length is right. The only thing I’d change is the length of the drawstring. It’s too long so I’ll probably trim. I’ll also probably buy one more color. Would like to pair with a wht linen button up opened w/ crop top or bikini underneath all summer and to Hawaii.

Ordered an xl and they are way big by at least 2 sizes. I am 5'8" 178lbs also the bottom of pant legs are wide. The picture in the post showed a snug fit and this is not the case even if they would have fit the leg is a wide flare which also doesn't work. Have to return.

Ordered both a Large ( my recommended size per Amazon) and an XL per reviewers saying it was tight in the chest (I’m a 38B). The XL came first and fit fine with a shirt underneath and comfortable. Then the Large came and it also fit comfortably so I decided to measure them and found they were identical in size 🤷🏼‍♀️. Both were 21” across at chest (42”) and 33 ¾” in length from top of shoulder. I’m 5’4 1/2” and it is above the knee so a great length for me. Both are great quality denim fabric, well made and flattering on as well as comfortable. Both tags say 85% cotton 15% polyester ( not spandex). As to stretch, there is stretch up and down but Not side to side as one might expect. I decided to keep both as it’s such a great versatile wardrobe piece that is hard to find. Just don’t know if this is a fluke that both sizes were identical or what. 🤷🏼‍♀️

Ordered for a '70s themed work event, took advantage of the overnight shipping that was available & was thrilled & excited to get my pkg. until I tried them on & realized they were over 3 inches shorter than they were SUPPOSED to be!... plus they're HUMONGOUS and unwearable! Ordered for a Motown theme party. I’m 5’2, so i had to wear 3” heels. Jeans are stretchy and very comfortable.

Ordered Size 0 according to Size Chart & it fit perfect. See attached pic. I’m 5’2”, 115lbs. Grear quality & price. Front pockets not functional only the back is. That’s fine with me! Highly recommend.

Ordered size 14, 5'7" / 202lbs and I don't love the way anything fits me at this weight. That being said, these jeans are not bad. They fit well, not super tight and not baggy. There is lots of stretch in the denim, so they are very comfortable even when sitting down. The denim is not thin so that it does not show every lump or bump. The waist goes to just at my navel, which means there is no muffin top. I posted a picture of the inside of the jeans front panel. I don't know that I look "thinner" but they don't make me look worse and thats a good thing. The jeans are long enough for 3 inch heels (I only had 2.5 inch heels on in the picture.) The denim is thick enough and has enough stretch to cuff them at the bottom to make them ankle length to wear with flats and still look cute. If someone wants the jeans to be tight and form fitting (for the lifting and tucking) I would suggest sizing down one size from usually size.

Ordered size medium A light green. I’m just shy of 5’6” and thought length was good. I’m size 29 waist and typically order pants in size 8 or medium. I liked the fit of these and flare at the bottom. As others have said, the elastic band means I won’t be wearing shirts tucked in but can pair with a sweater or a loose top. These are cute and comfy, and I’m happy with my purchase!

Ordered size XL. Fits as expected. They are stretchy. I'm on a weight loss journey and normally would wear a XXL. I'm down 30 pounds. I'm 5'7" and currently weigh 197. The waist hits at my bellybutton and bottom of the legs hit below my ankles, not touching the floor. Satisfied with overall fit, length, and quality.

Ordered size XXS in two styles but only keep one. The all buttons style seem to be more flatter. Size XXS fits me well for 5ft2 107lbs. Furry inside, stretchy. A little pricey but worth it.

Ordered small 28" inseam. Inseam is 26". Size guides states s is 4-6US. I'm usually 2-4 therefore if anything these should have been loose. They fit like jeggings with bell bottoms. I bought in the cream too and didn't even bother to unpackage those. Both are being returned. I can't bother with online exchanges. I bought 2 pairs from gap each very close to same price. These look like something sold on those cheap sights from overseas - no company tag sewn inside.

Ordered the aged blue and expected it to look like as shown in the product image(s). The color of the shirt received has a much faded look. The fabric is thin and light. A bit disappointed with the extra fade above and below the pockets. It came all wrinkled, which should be expected for 100% cotton material. Not a big deal as we always wash and iron clothing before wearing them. Ordered size small for a 5’4” tall person. The shirt has a relaxed fit, and will be a good shirt to wear during summer days. Looks good on dark shorts / pants. If not for the excess faded look, I would have given it 5 stars.

Ordered the Blue As1 size SM and it fits like a glove!.. I’m 5’6”, 132lbs. I thought the waist would be tight as I always wear M but I recently lost some weight and the first thing to show that weight loss are my legs lol. I’m so so happy I can wear small again 🥳. Both fabrics, denim & fleece are fantastic. I am impressed for the price paid. I looked all over for anything imperfect and nada. Color is great and love the distressed design. Don’t normally buy inexpensive jeans but I was tired of wearing winter wool stockings under my normal jeans during winter time so the second I put one leg inside these ones omg heaven. My long legs say thank you.
