Ordered the dress for a concert I attended with the family. I am a size 14-16, 5’3, and I ordered in a size XL and it was roomy so I added a belt to give it some shape. We had to drive two hours to the concert so it looks wrinkled in the pics, but could have been due to the drive. If your bigger busted you may have issues w the top buttoning, mine had a slight gap, a size bigger in the bust and I would have had to show the girls or wear an undershirt. Otherwise, super comfy, not heavyweight material and it’s not sheer. Perfect for the occasion.
Ordered the khaki and dusty lavender in medium. The khaki were longer and slightly larger overall. The other color fit perfect. Will return khaki and order in small. I'm 5'2, 125lbs.

Ordered the medium, but returned for the large because the other size was tight in shoulders and a bit short even for me. This straight out of the package, so I have no idea how it washes up. I am 5 feet 4 inches and weigh 155 lbs for reference. I like the color and the weight of fabric is as expected for denim.

Ordered the size of my regular Levis I still wear & these run small (couldnt even get them past my hips). Based on their sizing chart the long is supposed to have a 34" inseam, these only have a '32 & are too short for me (I'm 6'). Quality fabric, feel like real button jeans. But unfortunately these don't fit. I really like this try before you buy feature 👍
Ordered these for a 80’s party. They were a perfect fit. I’m 5’8 weigh 140 and ordered size Small

Ordered these for my daughter who wanted a size large 10/12 but there is plenty of stretch in them hoping after the wash they will shrink a little. The legs are pretty wide legged but they are still cute on. My daughter is 5”2’ weighs about 145lbs. Should have sized down because she likes her pants a little loose so I think the medium would have fit her perfectly

Ordered these for my wife. Ordered size medium; she’s 4’11” and waist around 28”. Fit as expected, pants are usually slightly long in the leg because she’s petite. Because they’re tight, the excess length just bunches in the ankles slightly and doesn’t bother her. Plenty of pockets, and they’re actually usable! Rear pocket is OK for an iPhone “Pro” model but might be pushing it with a Max size phone. Stitching and materials are nicely put together, comfortable to wear and overall they’re great. No complaints so 5 stars!

Ordered these for part of my Lollipop Guild Costume for a Wizard of Oz Movie Themed Event. They are SO soft and stretchy. I was extremely comfortable in them all night. No pinching, pulling, tugging. Just straight up comfort. I'm 5'4 and about 210 lbs with a butt. Hips are 50in, I probably could have gotten away with an XL but I'm wearing a XXL in this photo.

Ordered these in the coffee color, size 4 for my daughter - she's 5'5" & 112lbs. The material feels nice, soft with a little stretch, and the thickness is just right - not too thin & not too thick. The construction is well done with neat seams, sturdy zipper, and easy buttoning. As far as the fit goes, these were perfect before washing & drying. The length was just right. After washing & drying we both thought they shrank too much lengthwise, and will look weird if she wears these with any type of heeled shoe. So, if you're on the tall side, don't put these in the dryer! Also, the color is solid brown, not the acid washed look in the pics on the product page. Four stars because they're too short & color is misleading.

Ordered these on a whim and absolutely love them! I’m 5’2”, 116 lb, and almost always get a medium in pants. The medium is a little baggy, but was what I was hoping for, so if you want them tight I’d suggest sizing down. They’re so soft and stretchy, great for chasing around a toddler all day! 😆

Ordered this dress for a concert and super happy with the way it shaped me body. It’s stretchy and firm. Also very comfy. I would recommend this dress. I’m 5ft and weight 140 and got a size small and it fits great.

Ordered this for a Red Clay Strays concert. Was pleasantly surprised how flattering it is. the material has bit of stretch to it. only reason i didnt rate it a 5 stars because the material and buttons arent the best of quality. but who cares for a cute fun faux denim dress like this! i am 5’7 and 140 lbs with an athletic build.

Ordered two different washes; one was 27x32 length regular/medium but the other was 27x30 length short-not what I ordered. I’m 5’8” and I need a 32”. Also back pocket stitching on the 30” length was defective-loose and sloppy. Even if they did fit I’d send them back because of that.

Ordered two pair of these, an 8L (recommended by Amazon) and a 6L (because 6 is my usual size in pants). For reference, I’m 5’7” and 125 lbs, abt a 37” hip. The 8L were big, boxy, too long and too loose (wrong size); the 6L were a better fit—definitely not tight, though at least 4” too long. Returned both these Long-sized Amazon orders, and since Amanda regular 6 was not available, I ordered a pair in that size elsewhere. The reg 6 fits better, though still longer than other regular-sized jeans I own (a plus—can launder without worry). The Amanda style is a more loose-fit style than skinny jeans, and definitely look looser on me than on the model. But they are a higher-waisted, stretchy style, esp good for when you’re going to be doing a lot of sitting (eg. travel). *UPDATE: The 6R seemed to stretch out and get even looser after a few days of travel wearing last week. Based on reviews by others my size or larger, I ordered a size 4R (Zermatt), even though Amazon was now suggesting I order 6L. The 4R is actually the best fit in this Amanda style, and is still plenty long. *PHOTOS are of the 4R. Third time’s a charm! (These pants did need to be washed right away due to strong chemical smell.)

Ordered two pair. One was legit, the other knock-off. The 4 stars is for the legit pair. 36-28-40 - Ordered Size 9. A little snug, but they're stretchy so they work and look great on. I'm a fan of Vibrant (so long as you're receiving the real deal).

Ordered two pair. One was legit, the other knock-off. The 4 stars is for the legit pair. 36-28-40 - Ordered Size 9. A little snug, but they're stretchy so they work and look great on. I'm a fan of Vibrant (so long as you're receiving the real deal).
Orders the blue, what I received is much darker than the photos. Fit is perfect. I’m 5ft 105lbs, length of the small is great.

Orginally ordered a large based on the reviews and size chart. They were too big in the front. Ordered a medium and they fit much better. I'm 5'6 at 133lbs. They are super stretchy and so soft. Probably going to buy in other colors

Original review: I liked these when I got them because they're FINALLY a decent length for me. I'm 5'6" and regular length jeans typically have a 29" inseam which is just too short. These have a 30" inseam which is perfect. When I purchased, I bought a 34 and a 33 and the 33 fit perfect. They're reasonably soft while also being a decent weight. However, after wearing just a short while, they're immediately sagging and stretched just a little too much. If I was to buy again, I'd size down to a 32. More disappointing than the sizing though which is just a minor thing is that I've worn them about two weeks and I already have two holes/pulls on the butt. I have worn jeans for 30+ years and never, ever had a pair develop holes like this. For material that I thought was a heavy and good quality, I'm surprised they got damaged so easily. Edited 12/20/23 - more pulls/holes in the butt. See pic I added. I wear jeans literally every day and have never had this happen to a single other pair of jeans I've owned. I have to assume this is a problem specifically with Levi or this batch or something but I will not be ordering another pair. They also shrunk (shortened) after a few washes. I'm moving on and will look elsewhere for my next pair.

Originally bought these bcuz someone left a review about how comfortable these were and versatile they can be dressed down for a more casual brunch or dressed up for a corporate office or event setting. They weren’t lying , these are so cozy I have even used them as sweatpants. I bought a small and it fits perfect. I am just under 5’4 and they are perfect length when worn with doc martens but without the slight heel they would drag. So if you’re a short girly you may want to wear with heels or shoes with a thicker platform. The material is soft and sturdy. Overall this brand is great
Originally got a medium and was too big. Returned and bought a small that fits perfectly. I’m 5’4” and about 125lbs.

Originally I had purchased the large, as I am 5’8”, size 10, 34DD. It was too large, so I went down a size and the medium fits great! I was concerned the buttons would pucker across my chest, but there is still ample room. My one complaint is that it smells horrible, but that is easily fixable!

Originally order my normal size large but the waist was way too big. I returned and got a medium, it fit perfectly! Its actually a stretchy type of thick material not denim. Will definitely be ordering in more colors!! 160lbs 5’7 for reference.

Originally ordered M of the gray with roses when I was 4 mo pregnant, and they were the only thing that felt comfortable to wear. Once I got to six months pregnant, I ordered the large in the same color. However, the waistband of the large was 1 inch smaller than the waistband of the medium! (See pic. The medium is the wider pair on the bottom.) I still loved the pants, so I decided to return the large and order an extra large in a different color, the khaki with roses. I figured maybe in a different color they would actually be larger. I received the khaki ones today, and the fit is what I would’ve expected the large to be, but they are see-through. So now I am trying the gray ones again, this time in xxl, hoping at some point the larger sizes actually get bigger. Apart from the size inconsistencies, these are the comfiest lounge pants, and I highly recommend them for pregnant women! They are silky smooth and feel great against tender stretched belly skin. For size comparison, my pre-preggo size in most clothes is S/M. 5’6, 142lb, hourglass. Now I’m 166lb at 6mo pregnant. The mediums still fit, but I anticipate needing a size larger as I get closer to due date.

Other reviews stated they were true to size but I'm 5'4" 130lbs so I ordered a size 6 and these are skin tight on me. Too tight for a business conference and they pucker at the pocket so I had to send back.

Out of the package, once I saw the elastic waist, I admit, I was a bit taken back. Ugh. What the hell is going to happen here? Then I put them on, and what is this magic?! I don't have a huge butt, but I have a butt that is larger than what the average pants are made for and I did not struggle getting these over it. Dare I say, with just a tad bit of room. Very unusual to have butt accommodations in pants. I got them in " A 22 Green" that was hard to read on the screen but is actually a beautiful teal. Amazing. 😍 Medium tad bit of room (5'7", 145-150)

Over the years, I've gone from living stretchy jeans, to essentially trying to LIVE in super stretchy, jegging type jeans. I don't have a lot but I want more. I'd love to look good while feeling extremely comfy, so I took a chance on these. I am 5'3 and have a 32" inseam but these are almost perfect as they are. They have a cute fit. I'm not crazy about the little stitches on the back pockets (seems unnecessary) but the fit is just cute and flattering! Style wise, I love that these look like regular jeans as they have real zipper and button closure, real pockets, but have the comfort of Jennings or pajama denim. I want these in a million colors!

If you want them to be very oversized I would say size up but I like how the large looks on me. I’m also 15 weeks pregnant and the waistband has a ton of stretch so I will probably be able to wear them even when my belly is bigger. Overall I'm pleasantly surprised. They are a sturdy/thick material and seem to be well made. The length is a bit too high-watery for me but that's par for the course being 5'7". Some day I'll find pants thar crumple up a little on top of my kicks like all my short(er) friends... but until that day comes: the option to tie the bottom of the pant leg is my saving grace. The pants sit pretty much waste high, which has been my overwhelming preference from about age 33 onward. Overall a decent purchase. Oh and they're very comfortable too.

Overall the jeans are so comfortable, soft and warm. I am 5’4” 130 lbs and I ordered a Small. The fit is a little loose but comfortable. The lining work it’s magic when I wore it outside in the cold. However, I am really disappointed because I just wore it 4 times and the material in between the thighs are already worn out. I don’t know if it was already defective when I received it and didn’t noticed it. Obviously, it has a poor quality material.

Overall these are great, and I primarily bought them for the warm factor, which they do excellently. However, the fabric does bag quite a bit, especially in the knee area, which I personally can’t stand. It’s not super stretchy in the way that leggings usually are, and runs a size small, so order up. Plus after 3 or 4 washes, a small hole appeared in the knee area. Despite the issues, these are a great value and definitely keep you warm, and are even long enough for me at 6”1’!

Overall, awesome pants! Pros: - Long enough! Probably even too long (I got large tall) but as a tall girl who rarely finds long enough inseams, I have a tendency to overdo it just to relish in the rare experience of too much length. - super comfy. The outside is smooth fabric and the inside is a thin, soft fleece. Warm enough for cool weather but not so insulated that they're a deep-winter-only pair. - pockets are deep enough, ankles are snug enough to look good without being tight Cons: - the sizing chart was a bit wonky, so out of caution I sized up to a large knowing I'd rather be comfy than sausage-cased. There's a lot of extra room, so I really should have stuck to the usual medium. For reference, I'm 5'10", 160lbs, and typically wear size 8 pants. I have a 33" inseam. - the outside is kinda shiny. Coupled with the extra material, they feel a little like 80's windbreaker pants without the awful swishing sounds The photos are done after washing and drying to see if there was any shrinkage (anyone how has felt the disappointment of bringing home pants that were long enough in the store to end up having yet another pair of slightly too short house pants after the first wash knows why I waited to review) and I didn't detect any shrinking, so that's fantastic for buying true to size. These pants have me excited for autumn weather so I can get cozy in these pants that'll actually keep my ankles warm!

Overall, I like the skirt a lot it came out perfect. Although, I will say size down. I’m 5’1 and 198lbs, I got an XL and it was pretty big/long on me. I had to cut it to get the right length and bring the waist in a little. The red color is a tad brighter than I expected and it was fine. Overall, I like these pants. I am 5’5” and 135 pounds, and medium size fits me perfectly. These pants are very comfortable, but they look classy enough for any occasion or work at the office. I would not wear them for yoga, though, as the description says. They are a bit too thick for that. The material is stretchy and is of a good quality. It is thick and heavy, which would probably help the pants retain the shape quite well after repeated machine-washing.

Overall, I would say that these pants are average, which is why I am giving them an average 3-star rating. The main reason I'm not a huge fan of them is because they are a weird length. In the photos they look like a capri length, but they're much longer than that. I'm tall (5'11"), and they are as long as pants on me. So if you're taller they might be cropped/capri length, but if you're shorter they would probably be too long even for pants. The material is quite thin and see through. It doesn't have much stretch to it at all as it's almost like a chiffon type material, so I would definitely size up if you want them to fit flowy and loose as pictured. I sized up two from my regular size, and they fit well in the waist and legs. Finally, although it says they are ivory white in color, I would say they are more of a tan color. I don't mind the color though.

Overall, these are nice wide leg sweatpants. They are cute and comfortable. In terms of size, I would say that they are true to size and they also have a lot of stretch to them. On me, they're tight on the upper legs like yoga pants, and wide leg below the knee. They're also long enough on me, which is nice because I'm tall (5'11"). Only taking off one star because it says they are dressy pants, but they certainly are not. They are like sweatpants. So describing them as lounge pants would certainly be more accurate. I was hoping to wear these out, but I would only wear them around the house.

Pants looked nice online with description 100% cotton, which I was looking for. And made in Bangladesh. The jeans that I got delivered, was ONLY 57% cotton, 41% polyester & 2% spandex made in Egypt. As I called customer service 04/09/24, they said I will get the same if I ask for another replacement. And they will make note of my comment, that it is only 57% cotton. But still - That is clearly FALSE ADVERTISEMENT. See pictures. Be aware.

Paper-like. May say genuine leather, and there may be leather in it, but it’s not a “leather” belt by my standards. It’s bonded leather, at best. Also, I didn’t find the color to be true. Finally, I’m 5’3” and 105 and the medium was too long, even though I went by the size chart.

Pay CLOSE attention to the size chart! I think the size chart might be pretty accurate. I am 5'4" and about 200 pounds. I usually wear around a size 14 but I got the 16 just in case because of the measurements. These are still too small and much tighter than I like. The only reason they even still fit is because of how stretchy they are. So don't expect the sizes to match what you are used to. Check the size chart. If these were the right size for me I think I would like them ok. The stretchy material should be very comfortable. The length is good for me. The zippers is not smooth and takes some effort. It gets caught up a lot. The pockets are not very deep and the fabric feels like it won't last very long. The size on the pants says 32 everywhere. Nowhere on the pants does it have the size 16. Only on the plastic wrapping does it say size 16. So it isn't surprising that this doesn't match closer to actual US sizes. Overall it is an ok pair of pants. Just ok though.

Pear shaped, 5'4", 160lbs, these are PERFECT in the thick option. I will be buying several more. The medium fit but large had a better drape for my wide hips. I tried the thin option as well and the sizing seems to be similar but the thin fabric shows everything, cellulite, underwear lines. Perfect - the stretch, length, material, EVERYTHING… except the knee holes I have to fix and make a tad wider. I’d relate them to leggings but a little more tighter. The second set of pictures (with the red shirt) I ordered my normal size XL and they fit PERFECT. These will hands down be my go to! For reference, I wear 14-16 in jeans and my measurements are roughly 47” hips 40” waist.

Perfect fit and length. I feel like I can wear these as everyday pants or even dress them up with flats and a blouse. I’m happy I ordered them and now I’m thinking about ordering more. I’m 5’4” about 150 and size small feels really good on me. My husband loved the way they looked on me.

Perfect fit and very comfortable. Flexible and easy to remove and put back on when using a restroom. Great color! Easy to dress up or down. I wore it for a gala. The green color was vibrant. 5:5" 185lbs

Perfect fit I got a size small and I’m 5’2” perfect for business casual or just to hang out. Quality feels good not cheap. Appearance was perfect for work and to go out after for dinner. Loved them!!!

Perfect fit. 5’1, 112 lbs.

Perfect fit. For reference I wear 34B and a Small petite in tops. I’m 5’3. The jacket feels great, has some stretch to it but not too much. The color is as shown as well. Now let’s see how it looks after the first wash. Will edit review if anything changes.

Perfect fit. I am 5'2" and 105 lb, and in my 60's. I usually wear a size 2. I ordered the 2-Medium. perfect for an 80’s theme night! Ordered the petite and couldn’t have been happier. Far exceeded my expectations. For reference, I’m 5’1” and 108lbs.

Perfection! I bought this in both White and Wishful Blue. They are both a soft, comfy denim fabric with a slightly fitted shape. Not too fitted, but not that typical stiff boxy jeans jacket. At 5’2” somewhere between a 12 -14, I was pleased to find the size Large was the perfect fit. The “hem” hits me right above/at the hips, which is perfect for wearing over a spring/summer dress. I love the pockets, which are big enough to actually tuck your hands into. I like that it’s not too distressed. No rips or holes; just the frayed hem and a few “worn” spots. The wishful blue color is so pretty, It’s a nice worn blue. Not white washed. Not acid washed. And definitely not that weird yellowish dirty denim that I hate. Another reviewer called it cropped, which is not my opinion at all. It doesn’t cover my tush, but it hits lower than my waist. Delivery was quick. It arrived in one day. Hope this helps you find your best size and color.

Personally I ordered the yellow because it’s mustard!!!! I can see where some might say it runs small as there’s not much difference between sizes…. Im about a size 12 in most skinny jeans/jeggings (my waist is about 33-34” & hips 43” (I have kind of a flat butt)) & I think I’ll keep both the 12 & 14 unless the 12 shrinks in the wash a ton! 12 fits a bit tighter (but not uncomfortably so) & 14 more roomy comfort. Even tho I’m only 5’3, i’m short waisted (I don’t find the rise too high like mom jeans), & my legs are longer (33” inseam) & these are the perfect length on me! Folded inseam 25”, unfolded 27”. The fabric has a little stretch (97% cotton, 3% elastane) so I don’t anticipate it to stretch out much while being worn but I will update the review if I notice that!

Photo size reference: I am 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4/27 but decided to order the 28 in the trouser jeans. For my desk job, I was glad for the extra inch as there is very little stretch in the fabric. However, the denim did lose some shape after sitting a few hours and I did not love the “fupa” effect of the loosened lower abdomen area. Super high-waisted, with hip and back pockets. My silhouette was fly when I first put the jeans on but by the end of the day I had some lumps and bumps from all angles. I absolutely counted down the minutes until I could change though because the jeans are not comfortable for cross-leg sitters. The stiff fabric dug into all my crevices and made a few more. My opinion is that Joes Jeans is a sought after brand of high-quality denim…but this particular pair is best worn standing for this wide hipped female.

Pictured: Front and back of the pants before and after wash. To me, the term "Palazzo pants" evokes a longer, more flowy silhouette. The length feels more akin to long gauchos or very baggy capris. The inseam on these pants are 24" so bear that in mind when you purchase. They must have used models that are maybe 5' 5" or 5' 6" for the hem to almost hit the ground. Or maybe the photos are edited. I got these pants thinking they'd at least hit my ankles or skim the top of my feet. No such luck. The cotton fabric feels like light denim or twill. It's kinda stiff, so the pantlegs retain their voluminous shape. The contrasting white stitching on the seams give these pants a very casual look. I'm not 100% sold on the wide white string tie. I may remove it altogether since the waistband is elasticized. Be sure to air dry as these pants are cotton and will shrink up even higher!

Probably closer to a 4.5 really. I went with the Fit Type: 28" Inseam, Size: X-Large in Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. The fabric on these pants reminds me of the polyester pants my mother would wear to work in. It's rather a bit of a heavier weight with no stretch other than the back elastic band. The feel is smooth and it's so loose that it doesn't really come in contact with the skin much at all. When it does, like when sitting, it hasn't aggravated my sensitive skin. It has been through the washer and dryer, than sat over night in the dryer before I folded them up and stored in a basket for a few days before I got to this review. Yes, they are a bit wrinkled but not as badly as I would have expected given the way I treated them. They do iron out well. Otherwise, there was no harm in the cleaning cycle, didn't bleed, shrink or twist, pill or pick up excess lint. Really what drew me to these pants was that they were different. Yes, they are a bit large on me, but I like to order a size larger so I can alter if needed. Only minor alterations will be needed to make these a really nice set of slacks for me. That's the best compliment, in my opinion, if it's worth messing with to make them exactly what you want and these feel like they are. Overall, I'm happy with them but can't see wearing them in warmer weather. Peace Peace

It's rather a bit of a heavier weight with no stretch other than the back elastic band. The feel is smooth and it's so loose that it doesn't really come in contact with the skin much at all. When it does, like when sitting, it hasn't aggravated my sensitive skin. It has been through the washer and dryer, than sat over night in the dryer before I folded them up and stored in a basket for a few days before I got to this review. Yes, they are a bit wrinkled but not as badly as I would have expected given the way I treated them. They do iron out well. Otherwise, there was no harm in the cleaning cycle, didn't bleed, shrink or twist, pill or pick up excess lint. Really what drew me to these pants was that they were different. Yes, they are a bit large on me, but I like to order a size larger so I can alter if needed. Only minor alterations will be needed to make these a really nice set of slacks for me. That's the best compliment, in my opinion, if it's worth messing with to make them exactly what you want and these feel like they are. Overall, I'm happy with them but can't see wearing them in warmer weather. Peace Peace

Purchased based on the photos, the green tie dye print (it isn’t actual tie dye) was not matched up right. So there was a litter colored blob above the belly. Real issues are with the fabric. Stretch fabric on the 2XL was clingy not flowy like the photos. It was almost like it had static but it didn’t. The sizing was off. 2XL was almost smaller than the XL. Both fit way too close to the body. I am a size 14 and these fit like a size Large. The inseam is very small if you wear these pants as they should be- with the gathered elastic up high. If I pulled them lower to get the length right, there was sagging in the crotch and in the back. Not flattering. Seller should get better fabric, and make sure of consistency with sizes and fabrics. I am almost 5’9” but these don’t fit well for me when worn as intended. NOT recommended for tall women.

Was worried about the baggy loose leg but they are not over done even the length is just to the shoe anymore and they would have dragged. I am 5' 4" and wear flats. At my age, still looking good with the straight leg but baggy not sloppy. I will by a darker color next. Comfortable! Great jeans.

Purchased these cargo jeans because they reminded me of the ones I had in the 70’s, which I wore to death! I’m a 73 years young woman who is still young at heart 💜 For reference I am 5’3 @ 144lbs. I purchased the size 8(normal size)light blue color. These jeans are very comfortable with just enough stretch at the waist to allow me to breathe and not “suck in my pouch “. I love the fit and the higher waist. The back pockets are placed just right on the rear, the front pockets are nice and deep. As far as the length I feel they are just right for me. I don’t wear high heels just Sketchers and the hem falls just enough that they don’t hit the ground. The weight of the jeans are just right, not thin fabric. I am very pleased with my purchase. Haven’t washed them yet but I am sure they will be fine.Just a side note, IF you are young and don’t have a “pouch”, fit and trim, you might get by with one size down from your normal size. There’s nice stretch with these jeans VERY comfortable.

Reading reviews is the best thing you can do when ordering anything online, especially clothing! I'm 5 ft 7 in, 145 pounds, and typically I would purchase Large legggings. Really can’t decide if I’m going to keep it - I was looking for something a little more modern / trendy, but maybe I’m just middle aged now, haha, because it looks like something my high school students would wear. It feels like an odd fit - really boxy when I’m used to more fitted, and really wide in the body. The sleeves are so long - like to the middle of my thumb. I’m 5’7”, 220 lb, size 16 (bust 44, waist 36) and I ordered XL. I could have sized down which surprised me. Maybe I’d actually like that fit better. My friend LOVES it and says keep, and my husband says it looks like an “art project”. I like that the pearls and rhinestones are attached really well, like shot through the fabric like a permanent earring if that makes sense, not glued or sewn, so they seem like they’ll last. Another reviewer said to wear your hair up or it’ll get stuck - true! Maybe I should keep it to update my boring wardrobe. I think it’ll look great with black denim and a T-shirt, or a simple T-shirt dress. I love that it’s soft denim and not too thick and stiff. Okay, guess I just talked myself into keeping it!

Really comfortable, these are my go to work pants. They are well made and the belly band is a nice material that provides a little support. Relevant measurements: 5'2", about 125-130 lb, 26" to 28" waist, 34" hips I usually don't buy jeans for myself, since I find I have a hard time finding jeans that fit me in both the waist and hips -- there's an 8-10 inch difference between them! Nevertheless, I decided that I wanted to splurge and try on some name-brand jeans for once in my life. I ordered the size 27W/32L in Medium Wash. I don't like these jeans. I'll start off with the positives. They are well-constructed, and I doubt they'll fall apart anytime soon. They are definitely the most high-waisted jeans I've ever purchased for myself, which is what I was looking for. And, surprisingly enough, there wasn't a ton of excess fabric bunching up by my ankles -- a problem I commonly have as a 5'2" woman. The color of the jeans is great, and they have an almost "rustic" feel to them, in my opinion. The price is what you'd expect for something by AA. But the negatives, oh boy. The most prominent one: these jeans are THICK and NOT STRETCHY at all. It was a pain trying to get my feet and ankles through the cuffs -- and an even bigger pain trying to get them out. These aren't jeans you can just "slip on" in the morning -- it'll take you a minute to get them on. Then, of course, the thickness -- it means quality, but it also means that I feel constricted when walking and sitting down, to the point where it's actually uncomfortable. Then, even though my waist is a 26", there were still 2-3 inches of gapping between my waist and the jeans. Conclusion: I'm returning these jeans.

Returned another brand to try these. Bought them in white. I'm 5'6.5 and 138 pounds (feel my normal weight should be 130 to 133... I'm working on it, lol!) Anyway, I bought the size 6 short (I'll explain the short part at the end). I could button them, but when you run around in leggings most days, normal pants can feel a little too snug. I thought, "I know! I'll wet them and stretch them over a chair!" No! Bad idea! the next day, they were huge! I wore them and had to pull them up constantly, lol! So I wet them again and put them in the dryer. Fortunately, they tightened up - but that's the point. JUST wear them! Sit in them, walk around. They will loosen up on their own and feel fine! I even thought the thigh felt a little too snug... no... they're fine. Just needed to break them in!The reason I bought short (I normally don't) was just because I heard they ran long... and in the summer, I like to roll up my jeans - especially white ones - just for the look (I like them casual and relaxed) - so I thought if I get short, less rolling and I was right. They're just what I wanted!

Reviewing PACIBE Womens High Waist Wide Leg Linen Pants, First off, I assume a lot of people are reading the reviews hoping for some insight into the sizing, since the size chart is obviously bonkers, showing an XXL as having an 18.5 to 22.8-inch waist. A clue that the size chart is wrong is in the model details. The listing pic says the model is wearing a size S, with a 25.20” waist, but the chart shows an S has a 13.5 to 17.8-inch waist. I normally wear a 14 to 16, depending on the brand, for brand name clothes and an 18 for made-for-amz, East Asia brands. I ordered the XXL and it fits fine. An XL would’ve worked fine also. The waist is not only elastic, but has a drawstring, so you can cinch it in. The title says it’s linen, but these are a linen blend, as detailed under the product details. Since they are partly linen, they will need steamed or ironed, but since they are 50% viscose, it’s not as bad as 100% linen. The material feels like linen, although it’s thicker. I was worried about whether the material would be transparent, so I ordered khaki. It’s a nice shade of khaki and is not transparent. With East Asia clothes, I normally need to hem them, and this is no exception. I need to hem these by at least 3 inches. If I order in a size that doesn’t need hemmed, they would be tight in the waist and butt. I am 5’5”. The quality is good enough that I don’t mind needing to hem them, since it’s simple to do. The pictures were taken straighten out of the package, so they are wrinkled from being compressed for shipping. To get a nice, crisp look, you will want to iron them after each wash, though. One complaint though, other than being too long: They have a strong chemical smell and will need thoroughly washed before wearing.

Shopping for jeans is usually something that I prefer to do in person but lately it’s easier to risk buying online due to limited selection in most stores. This was a first and a great surprise. These jeans are really well made, they are true to size and are forgiving enough to weight fluctuations without excessive Spandex. They have a wide leg cut, mostly considered high waisted and have a “paper bag” waist with button closure and a bit of elastic on the back. There is a string to cinch the waist and emphasize the “paper bag design” but it is more of an accessory than a true functional part. Fabric has a nice weight and there are 6 color options, mine was Azure Mood which is like a light denim blue; overall they flow nicely without being too exaggerated. For reference, I’m 5’6” tall, curvy Latina who carry most of her weight on her lower half but still have a defined waist; size L fit me. There is a chance that I’ll remove the cord at the waist for a cleaner look or just change it for ribbon in a closer or even contrasting color. This style of jeans is a throw back to Y2K era, and can be styled with cropped tops, bodysuits, tops tucked half in/half out and may be your go to pair of jeans during Spring, Summer and milder days of Fall. If I have to be over critical of this product, I’ll agree with other customers who noted that tall people may have to proceed with caution, because they run a tad short. Overall 4.5 stars. Pros: • True to size, wide leg jeans • Fabric is soft, not too stretchy. Jeans come in 6 different shades. Cons: • Jeans may run a tad short, buyer beware.

Sidefeel Women Casual Loose Mid Rise Cargo Jeans GREAT I can see my belly button. I can breathe. Not high-waisted. They sit more-so on my hips. While I don’t really want to go back to all 90s styles, I enjoy getting away from crazy tight/too high-waisted pants when I can! POCKETS The front pockets fit my iPhone SE. It’s a little awkward when I first sit down (the phone digs into my hip a little), but it’s not like the phone is going to fall out. I think I will eventually not notice it. The back pockets also fit my iPhone SE, but when I sit down it sticks out quite a bit and I am not sure my phone will stay secure. OKAY The material is heavier than I expected, but it is not uncomfortable. Without shoes on these hit the top of my feet/down to the back of my heel. They almost drag on the floor – almost but not quite. So, the length is okay for me. I am 5’8’’. I don’t know if it will be an issue for others. SIZING If I followed their size chart based on my measurements only, I could have potentially gone with a size 4 which lists “hip 37.4”. However, I chose to follow the suggestions of previous reviewers to size up to a size 6 which lists “hip 38.39”. I am happy with my size choice. For reference, I am 5'8" and 125 lbs, with an athletic build, and my current measurements are bust 35.5, 28.5 inches for my waist, and 37.5 inches for my hips. OVERALL There is nothing necessarily wrong with the style of these pants, but there is nothing spectacular either. They are definitely for casual wear – not crazy flattering or unflattering for that matter. So, I don’t not recommend them. They are high quality and a break from the norm – you’ll have to decide for yourself on the style.

Size 8. 5'6" 155lb, (being modest) I have a tiny waist, broad hips, moderate butt. I carry my weight in muscular upper body, and dense lower body these days. I usually wear pretty curvy jeans, because the waste usually gaps in EVERYTHING I own. I wear a belt with ALL my jeans, because of the gap. These jeans fit well, enough that I might consider a size 6 next, with the expectation of a tighter fit. Not perfect fit in the waist, but they probably are a decent "curvy" fit. I liked Levi 529, this is a close next best. I wear a belt so I dont have to keep hiking jeans up, but find I did pull these up several times a day. The legs are mostly straight through the thigh, a tiny bit narrower at the knee, but not much taper after that. I have solid thighs, and large calves (boot buying is never fun) and own slim ankles... these jeans do not cling, but wearing a neoprene knee brace underneath leaves no spare room. I might have considered a 6, but was hoping for space for the brace. The fabric feels like traditional modern denim, not 1920s canvas-denim, and not leggings style stretch-denim...., just modern denim with a tiny bit of stretch... which I hoped meant 2-3 days wear, without looking /feeling worn out. But they just seem to relax over the first day, and bummer, feel like "5th day wear" on the second day... so to me, that's 1 day wear jeans. Color was good. Update: ive had them a few months, they RELAX and dont gently hug curves like they used to. The knees are stretched out. The fabric fibers are starting to give in the knees and thighs giving them a bit of a fuzzy "needs to shave" look. Still confortable as my "slightly looser" pair of jeans, but I have to belt them. Photos attached. I wash/dry them in the machine like everything else, using cold/warm water and timed dry, w name brand soap and dryer sheets... but they dont hold up like my other brand jeans of similar value.

Size ordered: X-Small, at least according to the tag Size normally wear: around a 4 in most jeans, or anywhere from a 25-27 according to jeans makers Body Type: Fit, 116 to 117 pounds, 5’2”, relatively long legs compared to top of body (I am not a petite length in pants), small waist, curvy. There is a LOT of different reviews on the sizing of these jeans, so first off think about the fit you want. If you want something a little loose and comfy—not at all tight but not baggy—then go for your size or one size down and that will vary according to how you are shaped. For example, I chose x-small because I read a review from someone who seemed to have my body type and was my exactly weight and near-exactly my height and she ordered an x-small and said they fit loose on her. Good thing I did because the small I almost bought would have been way too big. I wanted something comfortable—not baggy or ‘relaxed’ fit but not super tight, something different from all the tight skinny jeans I have. Once you select a size, try them on, and if you don’t like the fit, return them for another size. Really, that’s the only way because I think these jeans’ fits are all over the place, and according to your body type, they will fit one person one way and another person another way! Or maybe pairs are sized wrong from the get-go! Length wise too. Length: my legs are relatively longer for my height and these are long enough on me to wear heels—maybe 3-4 inch heels. I have on wedges in the sample photo I took but I wore 3 1/2" heel boots with them the last time I wore them. I probably wouldn’t wear flats with these. Mine fit loose in the waist but I have a small waist so I expect that with jeans. These come to just under my belly button, which is where I like jeans to be if they are high-waisted (these are). I have to belt these or else I'm pulling them up. However, everywhere else they fit me great. Not tight but fitted to my body. There is some stretch to them. They are almost as soft as pajamas. Really soft and comfortable. I didn’t buy them for some retro look or nostalgia, I just wanted something different—way too many skinny jeans in my closet. These are super cute, fun, and something I don’t have in my closet. A negative: They stink. Yeah. Sort of an old ‘mildew’ type odor. I had to wash them twice and I’m still not so sure it’s all completely out. I used a mixture of water and ammonia the second wash and rinsed them twice and that helped a lot. I could wear these for hours and be comfortable. I don’t know how long they will last as they are a bit on the thin side but for this price even if they don’t last, I won’t have wasted my money. Just super cute, super comfortable, and not at all what I expected as I have not had good luck lately buying jeans. I’d get another pair and wish they had these exact jeans with some other kinds of designs.

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 124-128 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and dress pants. After reading other reviews, I ordered the 4 SHORT in gray from iChosy. The fit is perfect with the legs being exactly right for flats. I LOVE these pants. They are soft and super stretchy, like sweat pants disguised as dress pants. The fit is flattering to my waistline and has enough give that I am comfortable when seated at my desk. The fabric does not wrinkle during normal well making these pants a contender for meeting days and travel. The fly is button and zipper. The cotton blend material is thicker than other slacks fabric so I’ve also been warmer at work. I am writing this review as I wear these pants in my office for the first time. I have received compliments from everyone I’ve seen and I bragged to my other half how beyond comfortable they are. In fact, I already ordered the black pair.

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs, wearing the size small recommended on the size chart. I normally wear a size 4 in dress pants and jeans and have a 27” waist and 37” rear. Fit is true-to size, and there is plenty of stretch in the waist if you are between sizes. I absolutely love these pants. I wore them the first chance I had to my business casual desk job. The material is gauzy, soft, and slightly stretchy. The waist is wide-band elastic with a draw string. The legs are really wide and the waist is high-high (like bottom of rib cage). They came without shipping wrinkles and did fine after all day wear. The legs are flowy and the high waist is flattering with a slim fit top. I wear tighter pants, skinny and slim mostly, for work most days so these wide leg palazzo pants are a nice change. I feel so comfortable while seated and stylish when I’m greeting people. Grace Karin delivered again and I’m certain I will be wearing this pair regularly. I can wear with accessories to work or could throw on a crop tank and sandals to wear casually on vacation. Either option is cute and comfy, 2 of my favorite things! Absolutely no complaints, 5/5 stars

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs. Almost all of my jeans are US size 4 or 27 and the majority of my elastic waist pants are medium (leggings, jeggings, joggers, sweats, etc.). The size chart suggested a small for sizes 4 and 6 but I decided to order my stretchy pant size of medium. Because of the cotton and rayon fabric, I decided to prepare for a little shrinkage. That was a mistake. Material: Thin, buttery soft, with just a hint of stretch. Lovely shade of army green with an elastic waist, functioning front hip pockets, and drawstring. Opinion: Definitely go by the size chart and downsize if you are on the line. These pants are so large on me that I can’t even cinch them with the drawstring to stay on. Way too big in the waist and baggy in the rear and legs. Honestly, the small probably would be extra roomy as well because these are not even close to my other mediums. They seem well made and I love the softness of the material. Definitely not a good choice for snow days but great for warmer weather. If they had fit, I would have used them for business casual attire at the office during the summer.

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and can wear either small or medium in stretchy pants. Based off prior experience, I ordered small from Amazon Essentials because they tend run big. The fit of the small is still a little big, similar to what a medium in brick and mortar store brands would run. The smooth elastic waist has a little gap when I’m standing. I have shorter legs and the leg openings touch the top of my feet. I actually like the looser fit because if they were skin tight I think they would look more like sweats than slacks. Description: I really like how these feel. The stretch of yoga pants but made with better material so I can pass these as business casual with the right top. The tag will have to be cut out because the corner does scratch my lower back. Also, and this is an issue I have had from AMZ brands before, the black looks slightly faded compared to other dress pants. It makes it hard to pair with other black pieces from my closet because the difference is quite noticeable. Other than that, I like these comfy work pants. Deducted a star for color and scratchy tag. (Didn’t deduct a star for fit because the size chart does show small to be about 2 inches larger in the waist than standard small).

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered these pants in size small. The fit is true to size in the waist. The back is elastic so there is a little give if you are between sizes. When standing, the pants are full length but when I sit the cuffs come up to mid-shin. Description: I love the design with the pleating. I can get by with pairing with heels to wear to work because the ankles don’t have the bunched elastic jogger look. I also like the real button, zipper fly, and belt loops for the same reason. The outside material is soft, like yoga pants. The inside is lined with slick polyester. There is some stretch but not like true sweat pants. I do worry that the pants will snag easily because of the material. Also, I hope the rayon doesn’t shrink if I accidentally dry these. Recommendation: I love these pants. They are comfy like loungewear but dressed up enough for business casual. I ordered the green and there is some heathering in the background. If not for that, I would probably go ahead and order the black but heathered black looks too faded to pass as work pants. This is the 8th pair of pants I’ve gotten lately and by far the most comfy! I highly recommend!!!!

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I ordered the medium and am happy with the fit. I normally wear a size small or size 4 but wanted to be sure the waist didn’t dig into me. The length of the legs goes all the way to my heels when wearing flats. The waist sits high. Description: The pants are very dressy and could easily be worn to a wedding, church, or work. The polyester material is flowy and feels like chiffon. It is snaggable and releases wrinkles with a quick tumble in the dryer. The belt is removable and made of the same material. There are 2 belt loops on the sides. The high: Wide legs and trendy style. I received many compliments from strangers. The low: The color is NOT coral but more pumpkin orange. There is not a hint of the pink shade found in coral. The recommendation: Yes! Even though the color is wrong, I love these pants. I wore them for 18 hours, including a full shift at work and a 2 hour drive, and I was comfortable and felt very put together. A nice spring change to my dark winter wardrobe. I’m glad I ordered up as I have similar styles that the waist digs into me and these do not. Deducted 1 star for the wrong color.

Size reference: I am 5'6" and 129 lbs, normally a size 4 in pants/jeans. The size chart suggested a small but I wanted a loose waist so I went with a medium. I am pleased with the up-size! The legs are full length, the waist is not digging into me but doesn't look too big. Description: I have ordered many wide leg pants so I expected the fabric to be chiffon type and flowy. However, the material is firm polyester, like a blazer with some stiffness and shape. Not thin and not see through. I love the pleats on the waistline as it does a lot flatter the under-navel region. They stick out a bit which helps camouflage pooches and sharp hip bones. The hip pockets are big enough to be functional. The waistline is extra-high, which I like from the front. There is something weird with the back view, perhaps the faux pockets are in the wrong place proportionally. As for quality of construction, the pants are well made but not name brand dupe worthy. I initially ordered strictly as work pants. However, after trying them on I think they would be cute with a crop tank and new sneakers for the weekend as well. Recommendation: I love them. I like the way the feel, look, and fit. I've already added them to my watch list and will order the navy if they go on sale. The pale pink is perfect for the spring. At the current price of $29.99, these pants exceeded expectations.

Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 128 lbs Witt a 27.5” waist and 37” hips. I wear size 4/27 in pants/jeans and have both small and medium in stretchy pants. The size chart showed the small from Noahella to have a 28.34” waist and 41.34 inch hips. I think the small is just a little snug on my waist so the button looks slightly pulled. The length is perfect for flats. Description: The material feels and looks like “real” slacks, opposed to the cheap polyester look that snags easily. The fabric is 100% polyester but looks like chiffon and is flowy. The pleated front gives a dressier style and the wide legs are comfortable but look sophisticated. The back waistband is elastic but the front is flat with a button/zipper fly. There are belt loops, deep functional hip pockets, and faux back pockets. Recommendation: I love these pants for work! I feel stylish and comfortable when I wear them. The high waist is flattering with a fitted top or tucked-in loose top. I would be okay with wearing these for business travel, meetings, church, and wedding/baby showers.

Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 but ordered the size 6 from iChosy *based on past purchases from the brand. I’m pleased with the fit. The waist has some stretch but doesn’t did into me while seated at my desk. The length is pretty much full on my shorter legs. After wearing for 8 hours, the fabric did loosen so I think my normal size would've held my shape better by the end of the day. *Past experience with iChosy: I ordered the high waist wide straight legs in size 4 short and loved them. The length was just right for me to be able to wear with flats. The waist was a little loose, which I prefer to skintight. I promptly ordered 2 more styles-Bootcut and Barely bootcut-in the same size 4 short. Both of them were much tighter, uncomfortably so, and I'm not sure if it just the brand inconsistency or the different styles of pant. I will end up donating them due to the sizing issue though, both of those fit more like a 2. So I decided to order a size up this time…. Description: I keep ordering iChosy brand work pants because the fabric is thicker and has a lot of stretch. Anyone who works in a business dress setting can appreciate having a work pant that looks professional but feels ready to lounge. My other pairs have held up well in the wash and delicate dry cycles. Another great feature is the functional and deep hip pockets that will actually hold a phone. The waist is higher than my navel. The only negative is the sizing issue based on my 4 pairs. Definitely recommend trying but maybe order your usual size and 1 up to compare.

Size reference: I am 5'6" and 130 lbs. I ordered the medium and am very pleased with the fit. The size chart recommended a small but my measurements are 27/37 with wide hips and I did not want to risk the waist being too tight so I went up to the next size. Description: The wide waistband is elastic with a functional cream drawstring. The material looks like linen with little cream threads mixed in and is not see through. The fabric is better than linen, in my opinion, with a slight silky feel. I washed on regular and dried on delicate and there was not a wrinkle to be seen! Recommendation: For!!!! I love these pants. They are comfortable for travel, nice enough for work, and can be either dressed up with a tucked in shirt or dressed down with a crop top, tank, or tee. They do wrinkle a bit during wear, especially when seated, but still not as bad as true linen. I have no critiques and would absolutely order again.
Size s, 5ft 5. 120. Fit & length perfect with flats& healed boots!

Size small 5’5”, 146-150, 32DDD, These feel like loose fit pajama pants, so if you want a heavyweight fabric skip them. I like the lightweight feel, especially for summer. They will definitely show panty lines so thong or commando required. They’re ankle length on me so I’ll wear flats, but the waist is very elasticized and would be easy to wear a couple inches lower if you want to pair with heels. They’re high waisted (sit about an inch above my bellybutton) so good for crop tops too. But if you’re looking for tummy control or thick fabric they won’t be for you.

Size small, except in the but, that area be supersized for 2-3 buts. I jist got an average to itty bitty but, one that fits a 90lb 4' 9'er chick. Dang humunigo sweatpants! All else was good. So sad. Had to order sunthin else.

Size up with this denim shirt. I ordered a small and it fits more like an extra small. I’m 5’6”, 140#, 32D, and normally a size small in shirts for reference. The shoulders are too narrow on this shirt and I have gapping between the buttons at the bust. Also, I can’t even button the bottom two buttons at all. This shirt is just too tight on me. I could only wear it unbuttoned, but even then…the shoulders just don’t feel right and my arm movement is limited. Oh and I also want to note that the sleeves are short when down, so they kind of have to be rolled up and buttoned. The quality is just alright. I ordered the black denim color for reference. Definitely size up if you’re going to order it.

Size was fine. fit as expected. Lots of stretch. i wear size 10 with 33 inseam. i bought 10 long sits right at ankle. BUT i do not believe these are authentic levi.. they are a knock off. i have bought these several times (different stores) & have always liked the way the pockets make my butt look. if you know, you know. pocket positioning is important. VERY SAD these are plain ol mom butt jeans. ( check my photo.. the distance from center seam to pockets is off) If your buying because the way the pockets look DONT BE FOOLED

Size wise, this fit like I expected. It’s not as “worn” or distressed of a denim as pictured, but would have still been fine. What I couldn’t get past though and ended up returning it for was that the stitching is bright yellow. It is very obvious and an unattractive color contrast on a blue denim jacket in my opinion. If it’s have been white or nude or blue so it blended in better, I would have kept it. It’s hard to get it to show up as yellow in photos as in person, but I tried to get a photo for reference. Otherwise the style of the jacket was good and the length was good. I’m 225lbs and 5’7” and got the Large. It fit well and still had that oversized look.
Sized down and ordered 4 for my 29" waist, in waist they are ok though, but the length is too short for 5'7" and 1 inch more in 6 size won't fix this for me unfortunately.

Sizing info first off: I'm 5'1" and weigh 125. I usually wear a 2 or a 4, or a Small, in bottoms. I ordered these overalls in a S and that seems to have been about right. I could probably fit into an XS to get it looking less baggy at the waist, but wouldn't get the overall slumpy look I was going for then. With that out of the way, let's go over some cons: my main complaint is that these are much shorter than I anticipated them being. That's not to say they're obscenely short or anything, but when I sit down I feel like these are Very Short. I'd guess these have maybe a 4" inseam? A second con is that the straps are...flimsy isn't quite the right word, as the fabric appears to be of a good thickness, but the length sliders don't move easily, and when they do, the fabric of the straps folds and clumps up, making adjusting the straps even harder. Now, some pros: the fabric seems to be real denim, not a polyester imitation, and has a good thickness to it. The stitching on the folded leg hems seems strong and more than adequate. These run true to size and the color I received matches the color in the listing. The hip pockets are real pockets, big enough to fit my phone, which in this day and age of fake pockets, is a big deal to me. Overall, if you don't mind a short inseam and somewhat annoying straps, these are a solid buy. I anticipate them lasting well.

Sizing is difficult to fully comment on because ... I ordered these based on the sizing chart, XXL indicated as 18 and 20, displayed on Amazon’s product page (image attached). I received an XXL, tried them on, and wondered why the fit was off. The sizing chart that the jeans/leggings come packaged with shows an XXL as 20 and 22. Leggings seem well made, soft jean material, but gathers at the knees. Length is great, I’m 5’ 7”. Waist band falls naturally about an inch below my belly button. These feel like they will slide down if I bend over, maybe my butt’s too big. LOL! If I ordered an XXXL (not available), would fit too large. As a woman, I know that just because these were too snug fitting for me, they may not be for you. But then again, I’m not used to wearing leggings where the material gathers at the knees—not attractive looking. I ordered the dark denim blue. Three images attached.

SIZING: Despite being pull-on jeans, I'd say these fit size wise more like regular jeans in slim. I bought two different brands of pull-on stretch jeans, both in size Medium which is my usual size for Leggings, whereas I'm a size 12 in regular jeans. The other brand fits great, this Chasenot pair I couldn't even pull all the way up to fit. To be fair - I'm curvy and these are slim fitting jeans. I would recommend sizing up - definitely size up if between sizes, and even double size up just to be sure, as I don't think thes in size Large would have worked for me either. For reference I'm 5'4" tall, wear size medium in tops, dresses, and leggings, am about 180#, and carry my weight in my hips and backside. MATERIAL: Quality material, much more like regular jeans that most pull-on stretch jeans. Not a great deal of stretch to these - these aren't in the "leggings" category at all, these are actual jeans that just happen to be pull-on.

So far I am loving these shorts. They are lightweight, not bulky material, but not so thin that they feel cheap or show underwear lines. I am 5'2" 128 lbs, waist 29" and hips 36". I got size medium and could probably have gone with small for a more fitted pair, but based on the size chart I was a combination of small and medium measurements for waist and hips and didn't want to take the chance on it being too small/unable to zip up. Medium does not look too loose or baggy on me, but the fit is definitely looser than how many ppl would wear this style and material. Based on how they fit me and the stretchy give to the material, medium would definitely still fit someone a few pounds/several inches bigger. I do like the stretchiness to the material, but it makes me wonder if they would stretch too much after sitting in them/wearing them for an extended period of time. I haven't worn them yet except to try on, so will update this review should any of those issues occur. But so far, I'm a fan and will probably buy in more colors!

So far, so good. I'm in between an 8 and a 10 so I ordered both. The 10s were gigantic and even the 8s are big. I probably could have gone down to a 6, but I washed these once and they shrank a little, maybe more next time as they're like 97% cotton. Even though they're big, with a belt they hold up comfortably and don't get the chafe-y, baggy crotch you get with some pants. They are also wide leg and very long. I cuffed them, but I'll probably have to hem them so the cuffs don't get full of sand. That being said, they're still GREAT solid, sturdy work pants. They're nice and thick duck with a double front and just enough stretch to be comfy. It's so hard to find quality work wear for women, so I would definitely recommend these pants and would buy them again, but in a smaller size. For reference, I'm 5'7", 145 lbs.

So first off, I'm 5'6", 165lbs. I have long legs and a short torso, making it difficult to find comfortable pants that fit well. I first bought the 33" inseam Medium and the waist was too long and wide. It was a comfortable fit if I rolled the waist band down, so I figured I'd try the small. Great fit! I like how thick the material is, and that there are pockets. It doesn't squeeze my midsection in an unflattering way, and the length is perfect for my legs. I'm so happy I found pants that are flattering, comfortable, and sooo stretchy! ***edit- these pants ended up shrinking in the dryer (I know they're not supposed to be dried in the dryer). I ended up buying the bubble lime yoga pants instead and am happier with these.

So I did not go down in size because I wanted them loose style boyfriend jeans looking/fitting. These pants do run big but that's because their boyfriend jeans, that's the style. I do recommend going down a size if you want them slightly more fitting but they will still be some what loose I'm sure even a size down. These are not skinny jeans styled pants but I suppose if you order down in size maybe you can get that kind of fit. But if you don't want to feel uncomfortable going down sizing know your measurements for a more comfortable fit around the waist. If you look under the sizing chart for these pants it will show you waist measurements with the sizes, buy according to your waist measurement not what size you normally buy. I'm going to order another pair down a size and see how they look less looser than what I ordered because looking at the size chart according to the waist measurement I should have ordered a size down but I'm happy with the jeans I ordered because I wanted them bigger/loose anyways, more like actual boyfriend jeans fit which is exactly what I got. Update edit 10/20/23, I brought the size 1 a size down and the fit is even better, still loose in the legs and comfortable around the waist not tight but also not overly loose as the size 3 jeans. I still like the bigger one's though because I like having all different fits and styles in jeans but going down a size is more flattering to my shape yet still loose and boyfriend jeans styled still not a skinny jeans look. Also fyi I ordered the medium color denim but received the light color denim instead, at first I debated on returning because I received the wrong color but ultimately I liked them so much that I decided to keep them. But I read other review complaints about receiving the wrong color as well so just beware that you may or may not receive the right color of blue ordered.

So I did not go down in size because I wanted them loose style boyfriend jeans looking/fitting. These pants do run big but that's because their boyfriend jeans, that's the style. I do recommend going down a size if you want them slightly more fitting but they will still be some what loose I'm sure even a size down. These are not skinny jeans styled pants but I suppose if you order down in size maybe you can get that kind of fit. But if you don't want to feel uncomfortable going down sizing know your measurements for a more comfortable fit around the waist. If you look under the sizing chart for these pants it will show you waist measurements with the sizes, buy according to your waist measurement not what size you normally buy. I'm going to order another pair down a size and see how they look less looser than what I ordered because looking at the size chart according to the waist measurement I should have ordered a size down but I'm happy with the jeans I ordered because I wanted them bigger/loose anyways, more like actual boyfriend jeans fit which is exactly what I got. Update edit 10/20/23, I brought the size 1 a size down and the fit is even better, still loose in the legs and comfortable around the waist not tight but also not overly loose as the size 3 jeans. So I don't usually right reviews but I ordered these in a 27/33 and a 29/33 the 27s were long enough (I'm 5'7 135lbs) but I couldn't get them to button lol so I figured the 29s would fit perfect, but as I was pulling them out of the bag I noticed they were very stiff and darker compared to the 27s , I tried them on and they fit except they were like an inch and a half shorter then the 27s (they were both suppose to be 33inseam)....very frustrating, so I sent them back and gave it one more shot and ordered the 28/33 hoping they would be soft and long like the 27s, and they were,yaaaa! I noticed people saying how stiff they were when they ordered a size 29 and bigger and that the 29s and the 30s were ridiculously bigger then each other(kinda why I didn't order the 30s and how everyone Said they were stiff).... why are they so different I wonder..... also are they even real silvers cause they don't look like any silvers I've owned in the past,usually they have the name embroidered in the waist. These did not. I would say they are not worth 89 bucks. Lucky me I got them on sale half off.

So I found this particular jean/pant in Macy's and bought during one of their "sales". Nedless to say, the pull on feature with no zipper in front but pockets in the back are great. Definitely can dress up or down depending on what you got going on. Anyway, thought I'd look on Prime Day for these since they had a limited number in my size not realizing they also came in black jean. When I tell you these pants are THE BOMB DOT COM I am not lying! They kicked my booty up, streched in all the right places, and for someone who is 5ft 2in/5ft 3in, the inseam was PERFECT with heels from 2 inches to 3.75 inches. They are totally slimming, the rise is PERFECT (no digging if you know what I mean), the waistband is ULTRA COMFORTABLE and I have gotten compliments all over from my husband (the biggest fan) to ladies out in the stores, restaurants etc. Being a short person having a high-waist pant does help lengthen those short legs and I felt like a mini-super model with my extra long mini-legs! Do not hesistate to buy, and if you did like me and did the Try Before You Buy feature, you will definitely NOT be returning them like me and putting more in your cart. 10 STARS ALL AROUND. (Currently on "sale" at Dillards for $69.50). Get them here while they last!

So I got them a few days ago and I didn’t think they would be very attractive on me so I didn’t try them on. They are a little more distressed than I normally wear but maybe I’ll get some Buffalo check tights or something to wear under them during the winter. They do fit nice although I’m not sure on a taller person how well they fit. I’m normally a 27” inseam on skinny jeans. I’m a little worried they won’t last long since they are so distressed. I normally wear a 6-8 petite dress pant. I ordered a 6 and I think they fit perfect. I’m 5’, 148 but carry a fair amount of muscle and a lot on top. I didn’t notice any “buttlift” but my butt just needs to get working out more. I’ll probably buy another pair of these.

So I got these and they are SO COMFY!!!! Let me just state first and foremost that I have them a 4 star review because I’m short (5ft). So they were a little long. Luckily, they don’t have sewn folds at the bottom. Otherwise, they’re cute and comfy. And for anyone reading the comments looking for what size they should order: 5ft (1.52m) 136lbs (61.69kg) I ordered a medium.

So I got this in a small, medium and large to see which size would fit me best. I am 5'5 for height and 115-120lbs(I haven't weighed myself recently to know my weight exactly). Waist is 29inches, hips is 34 inches. The size small was soo small! Size medium seemed too small because the zipper seemed too far out but because these pants are soo stretchy, it does zip up without feeling like the button is going to rip off. The size large for me was too loose feeling for my legs. The butt, it just didn't hug my body right. The size medium was the best choice for me. And let me tell you, it is soooo comfortable! My current new favorite pants for work especially! But... these are really nice pants for wearing while I am off of work too!I love that it is stretchy. I love that I can choose the length of these pants. I ordered the medium long. I love that there is a zipper too! Zipper plus stretchy and not thin quality is just super excellent!! Get this and find the right size for you!Edit: I accidentally put this in the dryer once! Oh no!!!! But actually, I still wear this for work. I am soo surprised that I can still wear it. The zipper part on the top portion of the pants remain still stretchy. My legs fit fine as well. The only difference that I could tell is the length of the pants. It did get a lil short after being in the dryer. Not high waters short, just a lil short. Still wearable for work for me. Since this product is stretchy, it does stretch back out some. I do not recommend putting these pants in the dryer though- it smelled a lil burnt.. hang dry is best.

So I have been trying to convince myself to buy a pair of overalls. I’ve always been hesitant cause I’m so short (4’7) surprisingly I only had to roll these about once!!! So I hesitated on purchasing jeans online bc im short and feel like my body is an odd shape, but I am so happy with these pants. This is my first size 10 pant which made me frown, but we can thank Covid to that. The pants feel and fit so amazing. Im 5ft2in and these fall at my ankle. I got the shortest inseam which I think was 28 on the options. I weigh 149lbs and intend on losing some wait but I feel like these pants would still be good if I dropped a size or 2 bc of the stretching of the material. Also, I love the dark denim, it doesn't have a weird scent after washing them like some pants do from dye. I am absolutely going to be purchasing a couple others. I do have muscular legs and tend to have issues with pants fitting my quads and these pants just have am overall comfort to them and I'm not constantly tugging my pants up.great quality.

So I knew going into this that the top would be a little big but I was not prepared for it to be this huge on me. There is no grip at all. Zero. It pools around my waist. So I like these shorts ! They are not too “stiff” which I have experienced with most other jean material shorts which I have never liked (the stiff aspect) Instead, these have a nice stretch to them but without looking baggy. I will say that they are not shorts you would want to go out to eat in, for example. I consider these more for summer time going to the pool / summertime outdoor activity because they are short. But that is also society’s own problem if someone doesn’t like how short someone’s shorts are (in my opinion) so really it’s what you are comfortable with. To get them to fully fit me I had to kinda get rough with them and stretch them out a bit, but now they fit great. I am 5’3 and 104 lbs so I think they do run a bit small as it is. My pair is a size S. Overall I like these in my summertime rotation wardrobe, and just keep in mind the sizing and that they are short booty shorts 🙂

So I love that these slacks are actually long enough. I’m 5’9 so the long length is nice. The material is good too. It’s stretchy and they seem like they should last awhile. The only thing I hate is that the waist is way off. I bought grey and black and both are xl, but the waist on both are super cinched in. It’s like the elastic was cut for a size sm-med size pant and sewn into a size xl. I added a photo so you can see how off it is, they are both supposed to be xl.

So I love these elastic waist jeans! They fit great and have a flattering fit. Sort of more flare than bootcut. They fit close to regular size. I got a 10 to be on the safe side, probably could have got an 8. I love the short length. I am only 5' tall. With shoes, these were fine. They do not have front pockets, but do in the back. I wish they had belt loops. I ride horses, and these are going to be great and comfortable riding jeans. Already ordered a second pair!

They have the right amount of stretch and loose to give that good fit. Can be casual and dress up for a night out. So for context, I am 5’5 (36, 38, 51) and squishy (per the wife 🤣) anyway I wear a lot of sneakers, flats, sandals and I am always looking for pants that hang just a little longer on my leg and touch the top of my foot down to toe’s. So I read the reviews and decided on these. Like I said they are great but I am on the fence about returning them. Being curvy it’s hard to find pants that really hang but I love them. Probably can’t put them in dryer, either way they are nice. Photos show how the pants fit. I say get them!

So I originally got an xL that’s my typical size. I’m 5ft at 175lbs the XL wasn’t flattering howeverrrr my friend got a medium, that’s what I’m wearing in the pic^^ and they fit perfect. I did cut them a little shorter bc I’m short but the flair is still perfect! 10/10 recommend! Size down!

So I purchased a 2L because I have long legs. They are long enough that they hit the top of my feet and half of my heel. I have a thick heel though. lol. I weigh 163 right now. Even if a gain or lose a pound or 2…they will still fit fine I’m sure. My waist is usually 28inch but I sometimes go for smaller waist sizes for the nice cinch it can create. Due to what I read in the reviews before I purchased I opted to try a size 2 long. These jeans feel like a super stretch light material. Like a begging but heavier. Not too light that it doesn’t hold but feels good and comfortable. It almost covers most of my belly button and covers my little booty. I love them. I was a little worried when I pulled them out the bag but I’m pleasantly surprised and pleased when I tried them on. They need to make more colors!

So I read the reviews on these and wasn’t sure what size to get. I don’t have a big mid section but I do have big hips and butt so I normally wear a XL. Based on what other said about the fit I decided to go with the 3xl and they fit perfectly. If you do have a bigger mid section I could see the elastic band rolling down for those people so keep that in mind. Over all they are good quality nice material that holds you in and not see through. They are long I wore them with just a 2 inch heel and they drag about a half inch. So I would suggest if you are 5’5 or shorter wear at least a 3 to 4 inch heel. I bought these for a disco party but I also started roller skating so I will definitely be buying other colors in the future for my new hobby because the length will be perfect.

So I recently switched jobs and now have to dress business casual. As someone tall and thin 5’8.5” I have the hardest time finding pants that fit especially in the length let alone online shopping. I’ve tried the Try Before You but option (highly recommend it) on a pair similar to this so I said why not if they don’t fit I can return them. Let me just say I LOVE these!!! The color is beautiful especially during the summer with my nice tan. They were a little tight pulling up but they’re brand new so they’ll loosen up. Perfect length for me to wear with either open toe heels, sandals or the right pair of boots/booties. I’m wearing a size Medium in the turquoise. They fit great in the waist even with a little bit of fluff in my mid 30’s lol. They are mid rise which I like I can’t do high rise pants. They give me a little bit of a booty just couldn’t tell by the way I was standing. Just a suggestion for your undies to wear something smooth and possibly nude or white as you can see pattern material through the front (see circled photo).

So I usually wear a 14 but amazon suggested a 16 and im glad i went with the 16! Perfect fit, and great for servers! I’m 5’7” and 195lbs for reference.. if you’re any taller than 5’7” i would suggest getting a longer inseam!

So I usually wear a size 14 in jeans. According to the size chart the XL (16-18), were closer to my measurements. I went with the XL, (I am 5’5” and 185 lbs), and they fit perfectly! The size charts always get me questioning what size to order. So if you are ordering, I would go by your measurements to get the right size.The jeans are well made, have a nice stretch, and are very comfortable. Only negative I can find is the top button is extremely hard to get through the hole provided. Over all very pleased with the jeans.

So I was in a pinch to order shorts for my upcoming road trip honeymoon, and I made the mistake of ordering 3 of these shorts in different colors.I'm 5'6", 122 lbs, waist 26", hips 34".. small fits comfortably. The shorts are definitely stretchy and in my honest opinion, overly-frayed.. look good from the back and the side. However, the front is just sub-par. It's almost as it the shorts were cut out from men's jeans.. too much extra fabric by the crotch. Therefore, when you take a step, they bunch up in a very unflattering way. It makes it look like I have external bits, if you know what I'm saying.!Seeing as they're still comfortable and don't look toooo hideous and I simply just don't have enough time to experiment with other jeans right now, I'll keep them. But I definitely won't purchase these again.. and in fact, it's scarring me from making future Amazon clothing purchases again.
