So I was really confused what size to order because some said order up and others said order down. For reference, I am 5'9 and 125 lbs. I have smaller thighs and often pants can be baggy in that area so I ordered the xs. (grey #1) BIG mistake. Way too small. These have stretch, but not that much. Size chart didn't help. HOWEVER, these are adorable and such a great price that I instantly ordered the small instead because these are too cute not to try again. Also, I feel like these have a decent amount of compression because the material is so thick so if you have a bigger backside like I do, you will definitely want to size up because they won't stretch that way.

So I was super nervous ordering these because I’m 5’3 and it’s almost impossible to find bell bottoms that aren’t crazy long on me. I pulled them out and was like yeah ok these are waaaay too small! But they are like the sisterhood of the traveling pants! I put them on and they fit perfect! I mean perfect! They are stretchy and form fitting and they are the perfect length! 🙌🏻 Also they’re not super see through which I find is the case with most white pants. I am 5’3 & 145lbs and I ordered a medium.

So I wore these pants in Boston in winter.i am 5’11 n 143lbs. These were 2 inches too short for my preference. They kept me warm but I wore leggings underneath also. They are bulky n take up a lot space in my suitcase. I would buy again if I can get longer for my height for winter.

So I'd like to start off with saying these shorts were 100% what I was looking for... however, some minor changes could improve the shorts. 1) they aren't as high waisted as I hoped, the crotch inseam part makes it so the highest they can be pulled is to the belly button 2) the pockets are placed in an awkward position, kinda placed more outwards towards the thighs. This doesn't change how much I like the shorts, it just moves them from the "I love them!" category to the "I really like them!" category instead. Besides that, I find them extremely comfy and styled just the way I wanted. I weigh 190 lbs and I ordered an XL, which seems perfect for the style I was going for. I wanted them to be baggy and loose, however I do need to tie the waistband to make them stay up. If you're going for a more fitted style, order your usual size. If you're going for more of a "boyfriend short", baggy, and loose type of style- then order a size up!

So I’m a little over 5’1 with long legs for my height and these are the perfect length. I’m about 140 lbs, most of my weight is muscle. I got a small and it’s a little loose in some areas but they’re still good. If you’re really skinny they won’t fit you probably. The material is soft but idk how long they’d actually last because they’re kinda thin. I really like them though

So I’m about 113lbs 5’6” tall and I’ve had the HARDEST time finding pants like these that look good on me, however, these ones are my new favorite!! I got a size 25 regular and they fit perfect in the waist and I love length!! So, so, SO happy with these!
So I’m about 5’9 and I ordered a large they actually fit a whole lot better they’re a little loose but they still fit really good so I’m really happy with my purchase

So I'm usually a size small but since I'm 5'9", I usually have to order larger sizes so the pant leg will be long enough to fit in the ankle area, and not short. So I ordered a large to ensure the pant leg would be long enough but the pants are too baggy in the back. Their size chart is correct, which is why I'm not deducting a star, so if I would've gone according to their chart dimensions/sizing, seems the small may have been better. I just don't like a tight waisted pant which is another reason I didn't order the small. But overall the pant quality is good for a work environment, paired with a nice blouse, even a blazer. The stitch and hems are good and the material is thick. They also feel like they're easy to wash and durable to last a good while. I highly recommend these pants. Just order the right size.

So I’ve only tried these pants on (with black fitted halter bodysuit (ordered separately). so let me start by saying that these flare pants are so glam and glitzy that they are a head-turner piece for sure. they are lined and relatively comfortable to wear. they might be a little scratchy for long-term wear, especially in between the thighs. BUT they are not short girl/petite woman friendly. these are very long pants and I will need to wear platforms when wearing them out (I'm 5'1). Otherwise, they are a few inches too long for me. also, I'm in between sizes (M-L) and ordered a Large and it barely fits me around the waist. the elastic waistband is a little snug but then the hip area is too big for me. you're going to need an hourglass (thick) figure for these to look good. I don't have hips/butt, so I'm more of a square silhouette and these look sad on me :'( make sure to look at measurements before ordering

So let me start off by saying that the first impression quality is great on these. There were a few loose threads in the zipper area, but most of those were easily pulled free and its no big deal. Moving on from that, I got the size 18 in grey twill. I usually buy 16A jeans. These jeans are very long. I admit I didnt pay attention to the inseam length as that isn't usually a problem for me being about average height (5' 5 or so). These are like 5 or 6 inches too long. See my pic, my feet are flat. I dont normally wear heels so that is an issue. Next, I think the fabric might almost be too stretchy. Or perhaps just a little too thin. I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

So the jeans showed up damaged but that’s not even the issue really my issue is anything above a 2xl doesn’t have prime so it took 6 weeks to get to me which is insane. Then it got here damaged and instead of being able to return it by the UPS ppl scanning a QR code I now have to go to a staples and print the shipping label (bc who tf owns a printer anymore) and then mail it out which will more than likely take at least a week before I get my money back so we are looking at almost 2 months of them having my money for nothing. I also ordered a size 2xl with prime and I love them but I refuse to make my rating higher because of it simply on the principal that yet again another company is inconveniencing ppl because they’re fat. Same fatphobia different company, smh. So yeah if you’re above a 2xl or bellow 170lb don’t waste your time. Side note I see a lot of stick thin women giving these pants bad reviews because they’re “baggy” on them that is because they are elastic waist with stretch they are meant for big girls if you want cute bell bottoms that will fit you get classic denim with a button closure! I’m 203lbs and 5” 2’ and the 2XL fits great

So these are AWESOME... but mostly when standing. Sitting down, the band really crushes into your abdomen and causes some serious FUPA action. I usually wear the Express perfect lift jeans and don't have this kind of issue. The Express jeans have the thick waistband feel on the entire front to hold your mommy tummy together, but on these only the waistband is thick and holds you together, and the rest of the fabric is thinner, thus the mommy tummy getting pushed out under the waistband for some unattractive bulging. But, very cute on the butt, legs and an overall slim look, just wear with a forgiving shirt if you'll be sitting a lot. I’m a size 8, 5’3” and 145lbs.

So they are super cute, comfy, and the colors are amazing. I do wear them regularly but they are definitely made for a certain body type. They aren't jean material like I thought they would be, they are stretchy yoga pant material. With that being said there are no pockets or belt loops. Let me start with telling y'all I'm 5ft 10inches and 142 pounds and ended up with the medium. If you are tall and thin like me you may run into some issues with sizing. The leg length to waist ratio wasn't too great. If I got the correct size in the waist (small) I needed 2 sizes up for length. But the size that fit my length (large) are huge in the waist. So I went in the middle and bought a medium. They are a little shorter than I would like and a little baggy in the waist. Since there is no belt loops there is not much I can do with that. So for us tall ladies there is definitely an issue in sizing.

So to begin with- these are not see-through which is nice and they fit true to size. Unfortunately, the waist band is very unflattering on my stomach/hips and accentuates those areas in a very bad way. Another downside is length- I'm 5'8" and these are just too short. They are comfortable enough that I will wear them around the house as pajamas with a longer top but I would never leave the house in them. I'm still giving these pants 4 stars because the quality is decent and they would probably look cute on a shorter person with a different body type.

So, I got these for me for an LSU game. I got a size small, and I’m 5’6 and 124 lbs. My mom is also 5’6, and I’m not sure how much she weighs, but she’s not that much bigger than me. I didn’t really like these only because when I went to sit down, the back had no stretch. It wasn’t comfortable to sit in. My mom though, she was able to fill out the back area, and she wears these all the time and doesn’t complain about the back not having that much stretch. It’s a windbreaker kinda material, so there will be no stretch, only in the scrunch waistband. The length was great, had a good baggy style to it and the purple is very vibrant. It’s pretty thin too, but as expected because of the material. The pockets are great. These pants are not see-through.

I was in highschool in the 90s and all the skater chicks listening to the sneaker pimps would be envious of these jeans! I usually wear a us 12 in jeans. 5' 4" about 140 pounds. Ordered the size 14 per the chart. Flat Garment measurements not stretched for the size 14: 16.5" waist 22.5" hip 30.5" inseam 44" top of waist to hem 13.5" button at waist to crotch seam 12.5" leg bottom hem flat....so when you double that measurment for the circumference that's at least 25" around at the ankle for each leg...lol 🤣 These pants also are very stretchy. The measurements are flat measurements without stretching the material.

So, I'm trying to switch my style and it's challenging for me to find pants. I'm 5'1, 157lbs, 40 inch hips, and 35/36 inch waist. I have a short torso and short legs haha. I got the large petite and they fit so well. They are soft, light, stretchy, and they don't squeeze my tummy which is nice haha and a common issue when you're chubby. So, it takes a lot of courage to share this photo but it was necessary, because I care about YOU as an Amazon shopper. I need to save my fellow 5’5” or shorter ladies from a huge jean mistake. Because I don’t know who decided these were considered “mid-rise”, but my Lord, y’all. The zipper is literally longer than my hand. If you are much shorter than me, you have a strapless jumpsuit. I’ve had a few pairs of Wranglers in the past for riding. And usually they are my go to because they do last. But these will be sent back and the hunt continues for a decent pair of jeans.Side Note: I got Davis, 9x32. The denim is actually really nice, I liked the back pocket detail and the length was great. So pretty bummed about the one foot Mom jean zipper.

So, just to let Amazon shoppers know...this is my second attempt at leaving a bad review for these low quality, look nothing like the photo made in China pants. As you can see from the photos I provided, the waist looks nothing like the pants on the model and the fit is big. False Advertising!!!! Returned. And I will continue to leave a bad review every.single.day until Amazon does right by their customers and posts this honest review. 5'3 1/2" 108 lbs, ordered XS.

So, let me start off by saying they don’t look as good in the pictures as I feel they do in real life. I am 5’9” and between 175-180 depending on the week of the month 🙄. Anyway! I LOVE these. It’s so hard for me to find pants that don’t give me camel toe because one of my hips is a little higher up than the other. But these pants fit so great. I thought they’d be a little longer, but I really don’t need to wear heels at all with these. I actually have to wear them a little lower than I wanted because they don’t go as long as I’d like - I wanted them to sweep the floor honestly. But if that’s not your style then you might want heels and you can wear them comfortably high on your waist. So I’d say, definitely size up bc I’m usually a large in pants and also definitely buy these pants !
So, my waist is 33”. Since I didn’t have any option to choose a size, I chose 33” waist. I am usually a size 9. These jeans are a 16. I am beyond confused. So fyi, 33” jeans mean 38” waist.

So, the plus is that I believe these fit TTS. I am 5'6" and usually wear an XL and I purchased the XL. They fit around my waist perfectly. They are a pretty heavy true denim and would be good for cooler months. The downside... I am not sure these would look flattering on *anyone*. They kinda balloon out at the knee, which I know is the trend for "horse shoe" jeans, but these are just... not good. I really wanted to love them but I don't think they are for me.

So, these jeans don’t barrel as much as others I’ve tried. But they are a dream to wear. They feel comfortable and ready for anything. I agonized over the measurements before ordering. I have a 27“ true waist near my belly button (I measured). I am normally a size 24 jeans. I an 5“4“ and 110 lbs. I ordered the XS. It doesn’t sit at my high waist. It sits on my hips. The bottoms graze my feet with an extra inch pooled in fabric at the bottom. If I tried these on at the store, I would purchase based on comfort, i am acknowledging that the price point is high for a brand I’m not familiar with. They are sewn well and made with high quality in mind.

So, these were almost a "miss" for me. The stress lines in the front just weren't realistic and look odd. But, everything else was perfect. They are true to size. I got the small and the fit and length is perfect (I'm 5'4" 120lbs, waist 27 hips 35). The high waist rests at a high but comfortable location. The flare is a perfect size for today's trend. I like the weight of the material, light yet sturdy. Since everything else was good, I decided to try to fix the distress lines. I used a black fabric pen and filled them in somewhat. And, it turned out great. Problem solved. So, now I'm quite happy with the pants and will wear them frequently.

Starting with the dislikes, this skirt took over a month to arrive. The original order got lost in the mail so they sent me a new one. The skirt was packaged inside a vacuum sealed plastic bag. STATS: 5’4” 130 lbs Tops: small, 36 B Bottoms: small/medium, size 6 pants Dresses: small, size 4 I’m always on the hunt for a good doop. I returned and purchased a medium and they are perfect! They are fitted but comfortable. The fabric is high quality. Not too thick, not too thin. Stats: 5’6”, 145lbs, 27in waist, 40.5in hips (I workout but I enjoy cookies) These fit well. They are not denim…more like “denim” like workout shorts. Very stretchy and fit like a second skin. My thighs are the thickest part of my body. I need bottoms that accommodate my thighs first and usually always have to get the waist taken in. These would likely stretch out as I wore them and get baggy in the waist. I might consider a small over a medium, but the chance it would not fit my thighs becomes the issue. I’ll never know. The wash is awful! I’m returning them specifically for that reason. They are very dark blue, and the “worn” or bleached spots are super contrasted. It doesn’t match the image from the listing. It’s giving early jegging vibes. Pass. Maybe a different color would be worth it. Overall, I would recommend for anyone who’s curvy, with a hip to waist ratio of more than 9-10inches. The material isn’t see through and you might get a summer out of them before the material wears through in the thighs.

STYLE: - The waist has an adorable 3-button detail that is super-flattering and holds in any pudgy tummy if you ate too many carbs that day ;) - Easy to wear and match with everything - we all need a good pair of white pants. - These pants make you FEEL confident & sexy which actually MAKES you confident & sexy! - I don't know what it is about white pants but men LOVE when I wear these - especially with heels!!! Seriously, I get stared at to the point of awkward, sometimes. lol SIZING: - I usually buy Wax jeans, jeggings & shorts and the sizing is pretty consistent. Also, my weight fluctuates so I like that they use stretchy, forgiving material that hugs in a flattering way. - I'm 5'8", 34D, 150 lbs, and measure 38/31/41 and I usually go with size 13 unless I want a super tight fit, then size 11. - I have pretty muscular calves (15.5" circumference) and these pants, even in the larger of the 2 sizes I order: 13 vs 11, still hug very tightly to the calves which causes creasing & gathering at the knees - see pics). - The length is otherwise perfect. - Designed for a snug fit from waist to ankle, so don't size down. They'll be tight enough unless you have zero muscles and/or fat. FABRIC: - The fabric shrinks temporarily after washed and then stretches as you wear it, so keep that in mind. - The pockets show through fabric (in white anyway) but my undies don't show when I wear tan or beige. - Thong looks best with these pants, as granny-panties don't let the fabric & back seam do it's form-fitting thing to show off your ass ;) COMFORT: - Soft, movable fabric make these a very comfortable all-day jean. - Waist is a bit loose on me, so I end up having to play tug-of-war with them if I don't wear a belt, especially if I wear them for several hours (because they stretch & relax as you wear them - as mentioned above). Overall opinion: As a curvy chic, these are by far the best fitting brand and I will keep buying Wax jeans as long as available! So I suggest: Buy them and do so in a few colors. Make sure white is one of them but also get some in colors you are confident to wear when you're on your cycle (in a larger size if needed). They look HOT with pumps, knee boots, flip-flops, strappy flats and even barefeet! Don't forget to click so I know!

I’m 5’5”, 160 pounds, & I usually wear a 29/30 waist in jeans. I ordered a medium and they fit spot-on, the length was perfect for if I’m wanting to wear flats or a small wedge. The downside is if you want front pockets, but I hardly use front pockets so it didn’t break the deal for me. I’m so glad I bought these jeans, worth the money for sure!
Super cute and fun pants- I got a large so they would be longer on me since I am 5’8 and I really enjoy these- might get another color!

Super cute and super comfy. They’re a little big on me, especially when they’re stretched out a little from wearing them, but I recently lost weight and I probably ordered a size up from what I needed. Decently long on me, but I expected that since I’m not very tall, probably perfect for around 5’5ish and up. They’re super stretchy and comfortable and fit up around the belly button and fall a little lower at times on me (like in the picture). Perfect pairing with a flowy knit style blouse or a crop top.
Super cute and very comfortable! 5'6" - 140 lbs. & ordered the medium. Got the black/white leaf design

I cuffed the sleeves because they are too long and I like the cuffed look better in this jacket.
Super cute dress, and large fit me perfectly. But the length was WAY too short! Like just barely covered my booty short! I’m 5’8” and long torso. Athletic build. Sad to have to return it

Super great belt! Love the two sided reversible concept, especially being done the white and black colors. White and black go with everything so there is no limitation. I love the fun twist of having the one side designed in the CK initials too. The buckle is a decently heavy weight but not so heavy that it weighs down your bottoms no pulls The waistline forward. The finish is a beautiful shiny silver. I would says this belt runs true to size. I selected a size large in the Black/White color option. For size reference I run 170-180 pounds and stand at 5'4". When I wear high waisted bottoms it fits my waistline on the smallest hole selection. Then when wearing low rise bottoms I have several hole size adjustments remaining that I can choose, which the belt fits perfectly each time. This as well fits my daughter who is of the same height but is 190-200 pounds. Being this is versatile for various bottoms, reversible, matches all outfit colors, and compatible with both my weight as well as my daughter’s I definitely recommend this belt!

Super happy with these pants! Finally the perfect draping fabric and I love the length! I like that its longer to drape over taller shoes but if you're shorter it can be hemmed super easy. I think the light color might be slightly see through so just wear nude colors underneath. Super excited for my other pair to come in!

Super nice belt, high quality material and cute looking. For reference, I'm size 8 in jeans, 5' tall, 125 lbs. The medium size I ordered worked well for me. I'm seriously considering buying the brown color. I would recommend

Super pleased with this purchase, here’s why. 1. I was skeptical at first, it hard to trust the length that’s provided on size charts but this one was accurate. For reference I’m 5’5 and from the side view you can see the length on the pants just hits the floor, so these are perfect to wear with flats. 2. The color is also very nice and a neutral coffee brown, not too bright or dark which makes it wearable for most throughout the seasons. 3. The pants are comfortable and loose fitting with a stretchy elastic to the back of the waist. A couple things to note. 1. The material is made of polyester according to the tag and you must line/lay it flat to dry after washing. 2. The finishing could be a little better with cleaning up the loose material/thread, but that’s just my preference.

Super quality for the price. I'm blown away. Very stretchy (necessary for my menopause bod). I'm 5'6", 150# and the 10 regular is perfect. Thanks to Beyoncé and her new team up with Levi’s, I decided to try this brand. Yes, I was influenced. I usually wear a size US 16/L in bottoms and bought these in a size 16. They almost fit. The thigh and leg area are nice, could stand to be a bit looser for my taste but not a deal breaker. What IS a deal breaker is that the band around the waist makes these feel EXTREMELY tight. I should have sized up to an 18. I’m iffy about whether I want to give it a try in an 18 or not. I might cave and give it a try. The length was perfect. I’m 5’7” and the regular length was fine. Probably helped that they’re bootcut. The material felt so soft. Like a jean/ legging hybrid. I’m too lazy to send them back but these will make a great Christmas present for a family member. These would be a great buy if you are able to pick your correct size.

Thanks to corticosteroids I've gained weight fast so I needed new shorts ASAP and for the price these work great! Comfortable, stretchy, dark blue wash color with rolled edge and deep front pockets. They sit right at my belly button. I'm 5'2" and 180-200 lb.

The - Blooming Jelly Women Linen Pants Summer Vacation Outfits Wide Leg High Waisted Pants Palazzo Flowy Pants Trouser 2024 – are fun and frilly pants that make me happy when wearing them. I love the way the fabric feels on my skin and moves when I walk. They’re not the most flattering style of pants for my fuller figure but I don’t care - I like them. The only somewhat negative comment I have is the sizing chart lists the measurements of the pants themselves and not the body. And they don’t seem to be that accurate in my opinion. I ordered the size XL which according to the chart the hip measurement is 42”. When I measured the pants I received while flat, I measured 52 inches at the hip. The X-Large does fit me but if I would like the pants to look on me as they do on the model, more roomy and gathered like a skirt under the elastic yoke area, then I should have ordered a larger size or two, if it were available. Regardless of all that, these pants are lovely and I’m very glad I chose to purchase them. For sizing reference, my Bust Measures 44”, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 47”. I’m 5’4” tall. I purchased a size XL.

The - GRAPENT Wide Leg Jeans for Women Trendy High Waisted Flare Stretchy Bell Bottom Denim Pants Baggy Raw Hem Trouser Jeans – are not on my list of favorite jeans. For starters, they have a strong chemical smell that after a few days left to air out, is still very strong. I cannot wait to launder them. Before I do that, I want to cut from the hem at least 2 inches if I intend to wear with heels. If I wear flats, I’ll need to take off about 3 or 4 inches. I’m 5’4” tall. The fit is decent in that these jeans are not too tight nor are they too big. However, because the fabric is extremely stretchy, these pants keep falling down…and I can put them on and take them off without the need to unbutton and unzip. I’m hoping that after they are laundered, this will be less of an issue. Overall, I don’t think this style of jeans is well suited to my fuller figure frame and I would not buy these again. For sizing reference, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 48”. I purchased a size XXL.

The advertised picture looks nothing like what was sent. First I ordered size large but XL was sent. Second, the picture shows a very straight leg crop fit but the actual is wide leg and super long. Realized that I'm only 5'2 so I didn't expect them to be too crop on me but did expect them to at least touch the top of my shoe. I'm definitely returning them

The AmazingStar Womens Wide Leg Palazzo Pants with Pockets Lounge Flowy High Waisted Business Casual Trousers (X-Large Long, Light Blue) are very comfortable. They are great lounge pants and nice for warm months too. The light blue is a lovely shade and the elastic waste band keeps them in place. There is also a drawstring, but it isn't needed. The length is good for me. I'm 5'5". Love having two side pockets too, they are deep and roomy. I would recommend.

The Angel's Forever Young 360 Bootcut Jeans are perfect for my everyday lifestyle. I purchased these jeans as I have gained weight and all my other jeans cut into my waist and had less give than these jeans. The waistband and denim are soft and thick with enough stretch for comfort without losing its shape. After all we all need a little support when you are as curvy as I am. These do not change your shape like butt lifting jeans do. I will be doing more squats for that...lol. I would buy these again in a different color. I bought a size 6. My measurements are waist 37.5", booty 44", hips 43.5" and inseam 28.5". I could hem them, but I like that my ankles stay warm when I squat.

The Belle Poque High Waisted Denim Skirt with Belt in the Color: “Black Denim” is Fantastic!!! I ordered size Small and the fit is excellent! For reference I’m 5’2”, short torso, and weigh 113 pounds. I love this denim skirt with belt! It’s comfortable to wear, flattering, it has a nice thickness to the denim and nice stretch as well. It’s stylish. I really like the style with the front pockets design. I really like the length. I’m so glad I found this pretty denim skirt! I definitely recommend it…

The belt color is a little reddish than brown, with a burnt looking finish on the edges, but I really like the look.q Just be aware if you are trying to match colors with other true browns on a bag or your shoes. I got this for a 44" waist pant wearer and the belt barely fits. I'm glad I went with the bigger size as this one is in the very last hole. The belt comes with a punch so you can add more holes if you lose or gain weight. Using the last hole on the belt the end just barely tucks into the next belt loop. Overall I still really like the belt.

The belt is cute. It isn’t quite as blingy as the photo depicts which is why I gave 4 stars. I purchased a medium as I have a 29” waist but thought since the waistline of many of my jeans is a bit lower I’d allow some extra inches to get through the loops. The medium was way too big. I wore it on the last hole making it as small as it could go which left a lot of belt hanging to the side. I’ll return and get a small!

The belt is very pretty and the leather is thick. I ordered the 22“-24“ for my 24“ waist and I can wear it on the second hole. It fits just right. Upon receipt, the leather has some creases in it. Hopefully this is a one off on my specific cut of leather. If I was buying this at a store, I’d still buy this belt, I do like everything else about it, but I’d search for a different one, without a crease.

The belt material has a squishy feel when you squeeze it. It's definitely fake leather, but it doesn't look bad; thus, convincing enough to pass as real leather if you don't touch it. Some observations: 1. The belt came in one size (in length). I didn't see any options to choose sizes when you placed your order. The farthest hole accommodated a 33" waist. Lucky for me, it fitted me okay, but it might not be the same for you if your waist is over 33". 2. The belt is a bit skinny (in width) for a men's belt. I have about 10 or so belts in my closet and this belt is skinnier than every one of them. All of the Seller's pictures depicted women wearing this belt. Hmmm... 3. The overall design looked great. The stitching looked okay. The fake leather looked slightly distressed so its should blended in, hopefully, when there is more wear and tear.

The belt packaging was great it came well protected. It was true to size so make sure you measure according to the size chart. I ordred the 35-39 I'm 5'3 180lb. Love the finish of the belt buckle and tip of the belt.

The belts are the size that was advertised and fit great. I am a 25 inch waist but I love how you can adjust them larger or smaller depending on how high or low on your waist you want them. Then you can also loop them through and kind of tie the end if you’re wearing them with a dress which is nice The colors came out lovely and as you can see, the quality is great. I think the actual gold buckle part looks just a little cheap maybe because of the tint, but it’s not a big deal. Really glad I got these, and I would definitely recommend!

The best thing about these work pants - the pockets!!!The fabric is super soft. Ther eis room in the stomach to grow (I'm 6mo). The pockets are large and have a lot of room. I am 5'-6" and these pants are a little too long. Invest in some fabric tape, a tailor or a sewing machine!

The black is a very soft excellent feeling fabric that will clean your house after you already cleaned. Unfortunately everything attaches to it. The fit is nice. I usually wear a 12 and the 13 fits great. 5’9” and the length is good. These are going in the garage as work pants because there’s no way to wear them without getting lint etc on them.

The black is completely faded. In the picture I am wearing a black tee shirt that I've had for 5 years next to the brand new black jeans I just opened. The difference is insane. I will definitely be sending these back. With that being said, I gave an extra star because they are extremely comfortable. I am 5'5 and they are slightly too long even with shoes on. Doesn't matter since they will be returned. I suggest ordering one size down because they have a lot of stretch. I wish the color was much better because these are insanely comfortable.

The black linen pants were selected for a woman 5'8" tall, 168lb. Based on the sizing charge, size Large was selected and they are a good fit. They are ankle length...not calf. The waist is elastic all the way round and there is a tie string. I like the attractive aesthetic buttons down the leg....they add interest. The linen fabric is not thin and flimsy...pretty substantial. The seams are well sewn, and I see no significant issues with quality. I like them.

The blue color is beautiful and the material is comfortable and stretchy. I am 5'3 and the 2 Short are a good length. They stop right above my ankles. The front pockets are fake but the back pockets are functional. I do wish the front pockets were real. The tiny front pocket does open a little. Maybe one key or loose change could fit inside. Overall, these jeggings look nice and feel comfy.

The buckle was very obviously rose gold not yellow gold as shown in the image. See comparison to my yellow gold ring. Also the cream/white colored belt was more tan/beige than white. Quality is a bit questionable but nice for the price. I got the large and I have a 34” waist so it’s true to size.

The color and design of these pants are great. They are made well. The material is good not thin and has a softer texture to them. They fit true to size. I like that I can wear these with heels to dress up or tennis shoes to wear casually. I picked these up for our Europe trip. They do have some stretch in the waste for a little flex and just a slight bagginess for comfort. I got the large I'm 170 at 5'7"

The color and fabric of these cargo pants is amazing. The garment construction is very well done and it’s just super unique to have a pair of cargo pants that are made of corduroy. I thought that these would be looser fitting and they actually are and I should’ve ordered a size up. I am typically between a medium and large and these mediums look a little tight. They are by no means too tight to wear, but I would’ve liked a loser flowing fit, and gone with a large. They are plenty long enough for even my 5’9” frame. The pockets are super functional without being bulky and the fabric is nice and thick. Even though they do appear to be a little tight on me, they are not at all uncomfortable.

The color and material of these reminds me or elastic ankle jeans from the 90s. I ordered “navy” expecting a deep blue cotton and instead it’s a faded blue and thin denim. You can see how far off they are from the photo. They feel cheap and look awful. Maybe other colors are better. I’m returning them. Sizing is about true to size— perhaps they run just a bit small. I bought a large and I’d say they fit me. I’m an 8/10 in clothes and wear either a medium or large depending on fit. 5’5 150lbs

It is slightly loose fitting that I will able to wear a shirt underneath, should I have a need for days when the weather is cold and hot. The hem can be folded in and the sleeves pushed up, without slipping down. The sweater was much softer after washing and laying flat to dry and no stiching became unraveled. It is a well made sweaterI ordered the small based on the size chart they provide. I am 5ft, 120-125lbs.
The color doesn’t match the picture. This purple is light & the purple on the pictures is much darker. The pants look much longer in the picture. They do touch the floor slightly, I’m 5’5

The color is described initially as Blush Pink. For reference I have photographed the pants on a Blush Pink quilt. Not even close to a Blush Pink. In ordering, the color is described as Coral Pink. Closer, but I would say Salmon Pink is a correct descriptor. It’s okay, Salmon Pink will work into the autumn, so maybe it’s even better. But I did order three tops to go with Blush Pink pants, and thus is disappointing. I took a star off for that reason. I ordered size 12 Long and the fit is perfect (5’7” 163 lb). It’s a thicker denim than in other leggings from Amazon Essentials, more like regular denim. Still has stretch. I think I will get a lot of wear from them.

My measurements 5'6", 136 lbs, 32DD, 29 waist, 39 hip, 12 inch rise - crotch to belly button and I've got big thighs, as you can see:) oh and as other reviews stated they are weirdly baggy at the knees but I can live with it.

The stitching is also a wrangler yellow (rather than the white shown) which I hate. The fit is good but a little loose in my natural waist area which I expect from most button up tops. I’m 5’9”, 180 lbs, 36D with a long torso and I got an XL. It falls to just above my thigh which I like and snaps well over my chest without any pulling. Overall I wouldn’t buy it again because of the color but the fit is good.

The fit is true to size as I am 5’3 around 140lbs and I ordered a medium they were perfect fitting.Overall, cute color, great fit, and comfortable.

The color seems much more dirty looking in person. They run a bit big and just not a flattering fit for me. I am about 5'3 135? Lbs and I know jeans run different sizes but my size 9 Levi jeans fit and these are way too big on me.

The color was as advertised. The length was perfect, I’m 5’9. However the waist was not the size it says. I put a picture comparing it to two other types of pants. The dark ones on the left are XL. The lighter ones ,on Right are 2Xl. Love the Xl, really dislike the lighter ones. The XXL are the ones that are what I needed. Unfortunately they are "larger" normal size. I am 220 @ 5'7. No hips,no butt, not larger thighs. I'm flatter then a board, seriously. They are larger / baggie short jeans. Was hoping for cropped capris. Their not terrible And like I said I do prefer the XL coloring.

The denim fabric of the - FTCayanz Women's Baggy Jeans Pull On Denim Joggers Elastic Waist Casual Harem Pants with Pockets – is very soft making these pants very comfortable to wear. The fabric is on the thin side so the pants fall and drape nicely. The color of the denim fabric, in person seems to be darker with less fading than it appears in the listing’s photographs. Not a deal breaker, it’s just that I would have liked more fading. The fit is great. The length is long for my 5’4” frame but I like the cuffed up look. The waistline pockets are roomy but the only back pocket, is small. I would have preferred two larger back pockets. Interesting looking aglets (see attached photo). Overall, these are cute and comfy baggy joggers and I like them a lot. For sizing reference: My Waist Measures 38", My Hips Measure 48”. I ordered the size 2XL

The denim is really nice weight in these high waist jeans. No visible panty lines, they’re not so heavy that they are hot to wear and have just enough stretch for them to keep their shape. The seams and hems are uniformly stitched and sturdy also. The legs are very wide, as shown, but I don’t feel that they have a baggy fit elsewhere. Unfortunately, on my body shape and height I don’t find them to be at all flattering I don’t know if it is the back pocket placement, the leg length or the snug fit of the jeans from the waist to the hips. (I’m 5’4” tall and wearing a size 10.) Even with a 2”heel I felt I looked short and squat. Not a look I can successfully carry off. On a more slender, taller frame they would be very cute.

The Dokotoo Womens Relaxed Fit High Waist shorts are fantastic! They look and fit perfectly! I absolutely love the feel of the cotton fabric (not sheer at all) and the elastic waist. So comfy! I already know these shorts will be favorite piece this summer! I wear Large (for a loose look and extra comfy fit), I am 5'7" and 142 pounds.

I already know these shorts will be favorite piece this summer! I wear Large (for a loose look and extra comfy fit), I am 5'7" and 142 pounds.

The dress is loose and comfortable, very soft the color is exactly as shown in the picture and I received a lot of compliments from this dress. I am five 1 1/2 and I weigh 127. I like it so much I have just ordered the black and the white hopefully I’ll be just as happy with those.

The dress was fine, but not exactly what I had in mind. The dark denim color is more of just a navy blue, at least it looked that way to me. I got a small and I’m 5’2” about 130 with a 36B/C. Fit fine except for a bit of puckering at the chest when I moved my arms a certain way, also a bit of puckering at the lower buttons with some movement. Decided I’d send it back.
The dress was super comfy. I’m 5’9” and it was on the shorter side but nothing unmanageable. Received many compliments. Wish it was a little cheaper but I would definitely buy it again.

The elastic waistband makes them super comfy. I ordered a L short initially, but had to return for the L regular length as the short was too short for the fit I wanted. I am 5’3”, 145lbs. My one complaint is that there is no button, only a metal clasp— I am expecting this to fall off pretty quickly, especially if you have to sit for a long time in these. I will add a button myself! Will likely order another color.

The embellishments on these jeans is quite beautiful. The embroidery looks of good quality., It appears to be sewn well. And the look is absolutely adorable. I’m not so sure about the button on the front which sounds a little “tinny.” I half expect it to fall off and will keep an eye on it. Do be sure you check out the detail on the bottom of the pants. They are very vintage looking and beautiful in my humble opinion, but it almost looks like an old pair of vintage pants for someone very very short, with the hem rolled out. The bottom is much darker, suggesting that the cuff did not fade like the rest of these well worn jeans. And the edges are frayed. It is exactly the beautiful vintage look I was going for, but it may not be for you, so do check out the pictures. I took the advice of other reviewers and ordered a size up. My stats 5’9”, 155 pounds, usually size medium or size 8 in US. I ordered a large and it fit fine. The waistline, tummy, hips, thighs are all good. It fits snug (not tight) and has a bit of stretch available in case these shrink or I gain a few pounds. The medium would definitely have been too tight. The backside looks nice, and again, the embellishments are beautiful. But the length is much shorter than expected. These land mid ankle, just above my foot, where my skinny jeans land. Not the right length for a boho vintage look. And I would certainly wear these with tall shoes like funky boots or 70s inspired sandals, so the length Is a big issue. I’m struggling with whether to return or keep, as they are gorgeous and I’m pretty crafty. I plan to take these on a field trip to the fabric store to find something to lengthen the bottom ... white lace, tulle, eyelet to match the embroidery? Or maybe navy/denim to blend with the bottom? If I find the right embellishment, I can make these even more beautiful. If not, I’ll need to return, which breaks my heart. But these are just too short to wear as is. Update: I added this 3 1/2” navy blue lace trim (cost $8, 1 hour hand-sewing) to lengthen the jeans. I absolutely love the results, and now these are unique and mine. So don’t fret, tall gals. A little creativity can make these short jeans just right for your height!

The fabric and construction are very nice on these pants. Made in Bangladesh from 95% Cotton, 5% Elastane, the fabric is a decent thickness, the buttons are well sewn and it even has a full metal YKK zipper. The fit is where these lost stars for me. They are super tight in the waist on me. Really they are tight all over but they are most uncomfortable in the waist. The length is ok on me, in fact if I uncuff them I could even wear them as a full length pant if they were not so tight! For size reference I am 5'4" 150lbs, pear shaped and typically wear a size 8 or 10 in pants. In the picture I am squeezed into the size 8 in black. I don’t even think the size 10 would work. These pants are also very soft and have a brushed feel to them, in black they attract lint like crazy, so consider a lighter color or keep a lint roller handy at all times!

The fabric has a proper thickness and weight that makes the pants look very luxurious. I am 5’5” and 142 lbs. A large fit me very loosely and makes me look bigger. Maybe a medium would have worked better for me. It is a very looking pair of pants.

The fabric honestly wasnt bad, would be great for summer. Cute pajama style but they do not fit like the picture. I was hopeful, but unfortunately returning. I would also order a size larger than original size, length is full length not cropped. Im 5’2”

The fabric in these pants is actually thick. It's not see through and has a pretty good quality feel for polyester. The color is spot on as well. Unfortunately, they are oversized throughout and look VERY weird in the front - see my pics. At 5'5, 130 lbs I got an M. Probably should have gone with an S, but I made this decision based on my waist size and the fact that one reviewer before me, at about the same size and weight, mentioned she wished she would have ordered an M instead of the S. Well, the M doesn't really work either. The manufacturer should consider using elastic at the back, so this item could fit more people.

The fabric is a cotton jersey material... Which isn't my favorite, look wise, but man! Are these suckers COMFY!!! I'm 5'2" and they're a tad long but you can cinch them pretty high if you want. They hang compactly so you don't really see the full billow effect... But they're sure cozy!

The fabric is a good quality and fit is as expected based on reviews. My suggested and normal size is a Small, but I chose a size Medium due to the comments around length. I am 5'6 and 140 lbs for reference. These are too short for me to wear heels.
The fabric is cheap and kind of itchy. They’re super cute on but not soft and they run short. I am 5’3 132lbs sz4 and they are barely grazing the ground. Just not really work $39
The fabric is comfortable, the fit in waist & hips and length were perfect (12 short, I'm 5'4", 140lbs). I wanted to love the pants but the legs are huge in the thigh and that was not flattering.

The fabric is comfy and seems to be durable but the sizing is definitely off. I am 4’11 and would need at least 6 inch heels for the hem of these just to hit the floor, after ordering a 2 Short. Most regular sized pants are only a 2-3 inches long on me. Peep my cute toes tho, I’ll give ya those for free. The pants were also just too big. I weigh in at about 97lbs and generally wear a 0/2, so I wasn’t surprised that these were a bit big on me. I was, however, surprised when i could stick both my arms inside the pants with the tension around the widest part of my hips. In comparing with some size 6&4 pants, the waist on these was larger. 2/5 - because the material is nice and they do seem like they have the vibes of good pants.

The fabric of these boho pants has a nice feel to it and the pattern (6blackflower) is fun. They are comfy and breathable, and great for lounging or working from home. My husband calls them my “cool pants.” My phone fits great in the pocket, but it feels like that side sags a bit; the elastic at the waist is fairly loose. I wouldn’t go take a walk with my phone in the pocket of these pants. I am 5’4” and 220 lbs with a 40” waist, and I got the XXL - which was probably the right size. An XL would have been doable but more fitted. Cat hair does stick to the pants, but they aren’t a cat hair “magnet.”

The fabric of these pants are a little bit cheap, but they’re definitely thicker than I thought they were going to be so that’s nice. And they aren’t see-through which, of course I love. So I’m on the fence about the quality, but the fabric does have this slightly fuzzy texture, I’m not sure how else to put it. But most importantly, I actually think they look pretty good on, and they are true bellbottoms as opposed to being called bellbottoms, and actually being boot cut, lol. They aren’t particularly versatile, because they are a very specific style, but I’m digging it. I think they are in fact pretty cool, and I do like the era and vibe, so I’m actually looking forward to wearing them and seeing what other people think! Lol. I think these are a size medium and I am 5’5” 125 lbs 32-27-34. They are a little tight around my stomach right now but that’s because I’m about 10 pounds heavier than I normally am so I think when I am back in shape soon, these will fit perfectly! I would certainly recommend them if you are also into this late 60s era of clothing because the pattern and the colors are spot on. I hope this review is and you have a fantastic day!

The fabric on these leggings is so soft and lightweight! The length is great on me (I'm 5'10) but the waist/tummy area is baggy and a big disappointment. But the biggest problem is on the 2nd wear, my fingernail popped thru the fabric while putting them on. It looks like a panty hose hole. So I got one wear and now they're house pants. Dang I'm mad!!! My nails are not long even. So i guess thats my fault but i realized its the weave of the material. Regardless I would not recommend you waste your money unless you have a very thick tummy and bite your nails. LOL. And OF COURSE it's too late to return or exchange. Don't piss yourself off....

The fabric these jeggings are made of is thick enough to look like real jeans, but just a soft and stretchy as leggings. Way better quality than most jeggings. I got the black and the dark wash and I really like both of them. I love having no button to cut into my tummy when I sit, or bulky zipper under my shirts and sweaters. I'm 5'2" and 110lb and the 0 fits snugly but isn't too tight to sit cross legged. The 0 is likely long enough to cover your ankles if you are up to 5'6" or so, but for shorter legs it's easy to just tuck the extra fabric under for a hemmed look. These are likely to become my go-to jeans to wear with sneakers or boots. I wish they made them in boot cut, too!

The fabric was great. I'm 5'3" and 142 lbs. ~ Flattering but a little high waisted. I am a little short waisted so perhaps these are just not good for short waisted girls. Still, they are for a party so I'm keeping them and just rolling the elastic down. Overall I like them!

The fabric weight of these pants will take you from late fall into winter. Made of 100% polyester, the material is an extra soft corduroy. I like that the pockets are on the front rather than built into the side seams. The pants feature a modern, high-waisted, wide-leg cut. There is an elastic waistband with belt loops and a zippered front. With an inseam of 28 inches, they may require hemming if you are under 5’ 4”. These are the pants you can run errands in during chilly weather. They look good with a casual sweater and loafers or ballet flats. They can be machine-washed and tumbled dry.

The faux leather leggings are okay—thin but cozy and warm. They have a stylish look that makes them great for dressing up or casual wear. They don't shape the body as much as I would like, but they still look good overall. I’m not sure they’ll last for too long but he quality isn’t bad. For reference: I am 5'8 130 pounds got a medium, maybe a small would fit better, but medium is very comfortable.

The feel and fit in the legs is nice. Can be casual or dressed up for work or events. Unfortunately, these run a little small. Going by the chart I ordered an 8, but really had to squeeze into them and had awful muffin top. I’m returning them but will reorder in the next size up. Colors were as described, I ordered khaki and navy. Measurements are waist: 30” and hips 38”

The feel the exact same as Buckles jeans. Only thing I wish was different, would be if the jeans were tapered in more. Otherwise worth every penny for the price. I’m 5’4, 155 lbs and bought a size 5X36 (36in long)

The first pair I bought broke immediately upon trying on. I thought about simply returning but I really liked the fit and style so I ordered a replacement. The second pair has been washed and dried and hasn’t broken (yet). I would hope for the price that the quality is good, only time will tell. I’m 5’ 4” 140lb and I bought a medium.

The first pair of these I bought were the “Katy Pure” style (top pair in picture). They are perfect. Then, I chose this cut, the “Katy” and the inseam is way too long for me (bottom pair in picture) and I’m 5’11!) There was no where to select inseam length; you just got what you got. Returning the second pair.

The first photo shows me trying to hold back the excess fabric but only partially succeeding. The second shows the fabric released. 5'2", 102#, 33 1/2-26-33 1/2 I use waist measurement after eating. I wanted a modest top for travel where modesty is preferred. This could be made more fitting and still not be immodest.

The first thing I noted about the barrel jeans is the weight of the denim-a true Jean weight/thickness! These are an 80's vintage vibe with the shape, style, and solid 100% cotton material. There's no spandex involved, which was a bit intimidating because I've been used to every Jean having some "give". These fit great despite not having that stretch. The waist is more of a mid rise. I ordered the blue color, not light, not dark, but a standard medium blue shade. Their large traditional Jean button is secure and fits through the bottom hole well. The stitching is solidly done, with no loose strings etc. This is pictured on a model that's 5' 9" tall and about 205lbs. I'm thrilled with these jeans and they are definitely my favorite ones and I will be purchasing more! At 49 dollars, they may not be the least, but they are still a great value.

The first thing I noticed about these jeans (Bb002-blue) were how comfortable they are and how good the rips at the bottom hem will look with heels or boots. Also, how darn baggy they are on me, especially in the thighs. My thighs are only 18” around so I’m used to this. Drying in the machine helped to shrink them a bit and make them wearable for me, although now they’re kinda short. I should have gotten a size 2, but there’s none. For reference, I’m 5’7”, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, usually a 1 or 3 in AE jeans. A comfy fit, the waistband isn’t too tight. But the back pockets are a bit on the large size, making my butt look even smaller. That being said, I’ll mention some other flaws I noticed. Nothing major. The “creases” where the thigh bends are clearly very fake looking. Not like they’re genuinely used and distressed. I was also disappointed to find the style isn’t quite flared. It’s more boot-cut, the flare being overly exaggerated in their pictures. Still, they look okay. They don’t tend to keep their shape either and loosen while wearing (as did all the ae denim). I may need to keep dry-shrinking these, as they do loosen with wear, but they’re coming to fit nicely and I think I’ll add them to my regular jean cycle. Chances are, you’ve got more curves to fill these jeans out, so they’ll fit and look much better than they do on me. That being the case, I’d recommend these for sure, especially at this value.

For reference, I’m 5’7”, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, usually a 1 or 3 in AE jeans. A comfy fit, the waistband isn’t too tight. But the back pockets are a bit on the large size, making my butt look even smaller. That being said, I’ll mention some other flaws I noticed. Nothing major. The “creases” where the thigh bends are clearly very fake looking. Not like they’re genuinely used and distressed. I was also disappointed to find the style isn’t quite flared. It’s more boot-cut, the flare being overly exaggerated in their pictures. Still, they look okay. They don’t tend to keep their shape either and loosen while wearing (as did all the ae denim). I may need to keep dry-shrinking these, as they do loosen with wear, but they’re coming to fit nicely and I think I’ll add them to my regular jean cycle. Chances are, you’ve got more curves to fill these jeans out, so they’ll fit and look much better than they do on me. That being the case, I’d recommend these for sure, especially at this value.

The first thing I thought when I tried these on... OH. MY. GOD. These jeans are so cute!! They are slightly different from the picture but acceptable in every way! They seem like the kind of pants that would fit specifc people and body types..but it seems to work for me. As a 5'2 97lbs 13 year old, you can imagine how hard it is to find acceptable clothing in the womens department for me. Especially decent pants( I got the xs brown cargos)...If you are looking for a super cute pair of baggy brown pants, I highly reccomend!!

the first thing you notice is how soft these are! model is 5'2" 105lbs wearing size 4. wish they were a size 2 but the 4 fits a bit oversized and comfortable so no complaints. they have a nice amount of stretch to them and are properly high waisted. the wash is very fashionable and well done. they are a longer leg so if you need a shorter leg be prepared to cut them.

The first thing you will notice about these jeans is the very soft fabric. They have a very good amount of stretch to them. I would say that if you are trying to decide between two sizes to choose the smaller size. Given the amount of stretch, you can easily get an extra half size if you size down. I am 5 foot one and these crop jeans fell perfectly between my knee and ankle. I love the color and I love the look of the wide hem. These jeans feel very strong and durable. I like the way the hem flares out at the bottom. These jeans give a nice shaping illusion to your legs. They are very slimming.

The first wash really helps the fit on these jeans - GAP Mid Rise Legging Pant - Dark Willoughby - 31 Regular. I tried them on after delivery and the legs were super tight (never had that problem) and the waist stretched to huge. Fortunately, after the first wash and dry (warm and medium), the fit was back to normal. I wear a 31 in all jeans and these are to size. However, there continues to be lots of stretch at the waistband, I notice significant droop as the day wears on. An initial high-rise becomes a sinking mid-rise, a belt may be useful. As a positive, they can handle generous weight fluctuations. I like the Dark Willoughby. I’m 5”5” 135lbs.
The fit and color of these jeans were exactly as expected. They are very comfortable with just the right amount of stretch. I would definitely order these again! 5'5"- size 10 reg.

The fit and comfort were definitely on point; loose and flowy. However, they did not look frumpy. I usually size up for length, and I am not sure that was necessary. As a 5'9" girl, this was huge. Too bad I had to return them to the seller. Unfortunately the low rating is all about transparency. In the image attached to this review, the silhouette of my hand is on the right and the rectangle retail tag to the right of the drawstring. They were visible in indirect, natural light standing in my office. I kept the army green pair purchased in the same order, because the transparency was less evident.

The fit is a little weird. Im 5'3 and fluctuate between 128-135. I got the 121 Little flower in a small. Material: The fabric isn't as stretchy and the feel is not flowy or soft. You definately feel the synthetic polyester against your skin. Since its a bit stiff you can't really move or do a lunge comfortably without feeling strain. The Cut: The top half is about 11.5 in. from crotch to waistband. I like high waist bottoms but if you have a short torso I wouldnt recommend it. Sure you can fold it over but the top garter which is very tapered has no give and it will bite into any soft spots in your tummy area. Any bulges the fabric will stretch and thin showing a lighter slightly see through spot. The pant leg is very long. 29" inseam and looks more like a straight pant leg than a bell bottom flow. Sure you can hem it, but you will lose any slight bell bottom shape. Design: The print is beautiful which is why I gave it more stars than I would have otherwise. The color is bright and crisp an no bleed throughs. My only gripe is that the print is not flattering to my butt. Im not sure if this makes sense but the print has a more of a camouflage effect to make things bumps appear smaller. This might also have something to do with the cut falling straight down from the hip instead of your thigh like a bell bottom should. I think even if I size up the cut will still be wierd and any hemming I can do will just make the pants boxier. I want to give it a shot because of the pretty print but its just not what I was looking for so I will be return it.

The fit of these wide-leg jeans is outstanding! I am 5’4” 138 lbs, 30” waist. I bought the Large 27” and these required NO alterations. The length is perfect, can wear flat sandals or tennis shoes. The fit on these pants is much better than I expected... they don't cling right to my hips and butt like a lot of pants like this do; they're just a tiny, tiny bit looser. That being said, you still need to order the same size you normally would. I'm 5'8" and 155, and wear a size 12. Pardon the belly in the pic...🤷‍♀️ The fabric of these pants is very nice... not too thick or thin so they can be worn year-round. Excellent quality. They came out of the dryer beautifully. The only tiny little complaint I have for these is that while the waistband appears to be about 3 inches wide, the elastic band inside is only about 1/2 inch wide, and I can already tell I'll have an issue with it rolling and twisting as time goes on. It would be nice if it was about 2 inches wide. I think it would feel more secure around the waist also, although it's fine now.

The fit on these pants was perfect and the exact look that my daughter was going for; we were especially happy with the length as she is 5'7'' and often pants end up a bit too short. My only complaint is that the material makes a very loud swishing sound when you are walking.

The fit perfectly! I would recommend if you like a tighter fit, to size down. I usually wear a XL and I got a L she they are perfect! If you like a looser fit, stick with your normal sizing! My inner thighs are smoothed out, and cellulite is hidden everywhere.The fabric: they feel high quality, well made. Soft, but durable like a denim/canvas - and they stretch and give when I bend/move without loosing shape or stretching out of shape when I srand back up. I wore these to 3 childrens birthdays in one day and still felt so confident in them to rock to a friends party that night.Size: I am 5"11, 160, bought the black long in a size 10 and they fit great. ps - the long still barely hits my ankle bone, but at least thats better than capri length.These pants are magical and I am SSOO bummed the other colors are out of stock. The garment smelled terrible right out of the package. It doesn’t fit as described or displayed in pictures; this is not a slim fit item. It fits very boxy at the waist; the sleeves are too wide. My bust is 34in and waist 25in and I ordered a small (according to the size chart)

THE GOOD: —Nicely packaged. —Hose are sheer —Husband said they looked good —Elastic top —Reinforced toe —Top will stretch to 9” (with effort) —They were appropriate length (to my knee) —Colors were as expected Sounds good so far!! But… THE BAD: —Elastic top is REALLY snug —Uncomfortable after 20 minutes wear —After 35 minutes, I had to take them off because of the pain around the top. Felt like a rubber band around my leg. I’m 5’6” and weigh 194. I actually thought these knee highs would be too large, if anything. Nope! They are definitely not plus size. Remember, the gals in the advertising look like they’re happy as can be. I’ll bet in reality, they couldn’t wait to get done with the shoot so they could get circulation back in their lower legs. Bahaha!! Unfortunately I cannot in good conscience recommend these knee highs to plus size gals. They may be great, however for smaller ladies.

The jeans are a great quality super thick, beautiful color, but also super tight. Maybe I should’ve went over the reviews a little bit better as some people did say they did go up a size. For me I probably should’ve went up two sizes. In my mind I always think I’m a size 10 maybe a size 12, with these pants I probably should’ve gotten a 14. They do go super high all the way up to the small part of your waist so the part around your waist has no give no stretch. The part around your thighs your booty your legs is super stretchy, so it was hard to think what size only because I didn’t want around my legs or my booty to be super big. They are a wide legged jeans which isn’t my favorite but I honestly don’t mind these. Especially for the winter or colder months with some cute boots or maybe some UGGs. I would’ve liked for the waste part maybe to stretch a little bit more to provide a little bit more comfort. As I said they do go super high up to the smaller part of your waist almost right under your rib cage so they are super tight and there’s no give up there. I may wear these once or twice I’m kind of a little scared to wash them. I don’t want them to get smaller or tighter. I am 5‘,4“ and for how high they go they were right at my ankle so if you are taller and they do go that high up your waist they’re gonna be very short. I feel a little 50-50 on these. Like I said the gene the stretch is very nice. Nice color, but I find them too tight maybe a little too uncomfortable for a lot of wear. Maybe for a nice outing or date I can totally wear these. I’m not very happy of how high they go only because I’m not very thin and I feel like that smaller part of your stomach at the bottom Does show a lot so a longer sweater or a longer jacket will definitely be my choice.

The leopard jeans are a nice variation to the common plain regular jeans. The leopard print is what I like the most about the jeans. The shape of the jeans is different from my usual skinny jeans and especially the legs and the hip feel a little boxy. I am 5'2" petite, 140lbs, 31" waist and 43" hips, and size 12 fits just right. If I gain a little bit more weight, I won't be able to fit. The jeans feel fine from my waist but there are some creases that form in the front, also the pants are too long but for somebody taller this won't be an issue. The fabric feels a little bit stiff and rough compared to regular jeans but after wearing them for a while they get softer and adapt better to the shape of the body. I wash the jeans in a cold regular cycle and dry them in the permanent press cycle in the dryer. I steam them to remove wrinkles. The pants didn't shrink and preserved the colors after washing.

The Levi Strauss Women's Modern Straight Jeans is a comfortable pair of jeans that fit true to size and looks attractive on most body types. I like how the jeans are high-waisted and doesn't pinch anywhere. When I crouch down, I don't have issues with the jeans crouching down with me and exposing my derriere, and I like how it doesn't suck the life out of me. Although the jeans are cut short, at 5' tall, I still found I needed to alter the jeans to fit my leg length. As far as the fit goes, it's a bit more fitted than "mom jeans", but not quite as fitted as bootcut jeans. It's not quite as snug on the thigh and butt area and it has a nice "forgiving" feel. The jeans wash well and there was no fading or fraying and the material seems thick enough to last a while before the inevitable knee holes start showing up. That said, the jean material is not as thick as other jeans I own, but it's still fairly decent quality for the price. Overall, they're comfortable and casual jeans to wear and something I would purchase again.

The Linen Capri Pants for Women Casual 2024 Summer Drawstring Elastic High Waist Capris Wide Leg Cropped Trousers are true linen pants. While they are thin they are not seethrough. They are lightweight, have an elastic high waist with a working drawstring, and are a great wardrobe addition for the summer months. I am 5'3" and very curvy, I ordered a size large to fit comfortably over my hips. These pants fit nicely, they offer plenty of room and comfort, the length hits about mid-calf and they have a nice flare. The loose flowy fit makes them perfect for hot summer days. The waistband is attractive and a shirt/blouse could be worn tucked or untucked. I look forward to wearing these capris throughout the summer and am very happy I made the purchase.

I have a long waist so sometimes have trouble with the waist-to-crotch distance being too short; felt a little tight that way when I first put them on (the photos were taken then) but they relaxed quickly as I wore them. By no means a dealbreaker, and it doesn’t impact my enjoyment of these much, but there are some spots on the back pockets and belt line that catch your shirt or skin enough that I took off a star.

The material has a really nice feel to it. Listed as 70% cotton/30% linen. They are flowy & full. Very comfortable. I am 5’4” & they are a bit long on me, but will be fine with a small heel. I mostly wear a size 12, occasionally a size 10 if an item runs large. I ordered an XL, but think a Large may of fit more appropriately, both in length & size. My suggestion would be to size down, if in between sizes like myself. They came in a vacuumed packed bag, so they were quite wrinkly, but steamed out nicely & easily. They are very pretty pants if a palazzo style appeals to you. The pockets are a decent size, but are not overly deep. I think they might look a little more swanky on someone taller than myself, but they are attractive pants. To me, the color is more of a periwinkle, than a true blue, but they are a pretty color. I wish there was a petite option, but these are stylish pants that can be dressed up or down. I would recommend, if fuller pants appeal to you.

I would suggest ordering 1 size up.

The material of these pants is a very shiny “velvet” and not at all what I expected. The material is soft and the pants washed and dried well on a gentle cycle with a low heat setting to dry. I am 5’5” and weigh about 138. I am usually a medium in most pants. These pants are several inches too long and the waist is a bit tight—just not a good fit for me.

The material of this outfit was linen-like and had a good weight to it. The fit for me was a bit off. I am usually a large bottom and medium top. The bottoms were a bit tighter than expected and the top vest was too big and boxy. The pants are high waisted and wide leg. The material was stiffer so flattering and didn't cling. There was a zipper and elastic in the waistband so they could be put on easily. For me I would need to alter the vest for it to look better on but I could wear the pants with another top. The pants were a great length on my 5'4" frame when worn higher on my waist. The pants hit right above the floor so can be worn with flat sandals.

The material on these is great- not too thin. Great stretch, but still holds it's shape. I am typically a size 10, but tend to be between a 10 or 8, so I ordered a size 8 and they fit perfectly. I'm 5'5" 145lbs for reference.

The material on these sweatpants is somewhat thin, and very comfortable. The elastic waistband is made very well and will not get twisted. The pockets are also nice and large and my cell phone does not fall out. I am 5’ 10” and weigh 145 lb and ordered the small. The fit was just right, and I will probably air dry to prevent shrinkage. The price point is pretty reasonable for this product.

The material seemed to be good quality on the pair of pants I received. However, the button was not. It was a plastic material and flimsy. I think it would have fallen off if I buttoned the pants a few times. I am 5’9 and the legs were about 3 or 4 inches from the top of my foot when I had the pants on.

The material used to make this is soft & stretchy, so these are quite comfy due to that.There have been some missteps with the construction, however. The straps were threaded backward through the hardware, so the adjustable part is on the backside & the stitching is on top, so it looks bad. I’m going to have to rip them apart & resew. In addition, the belt loops on the back are uneven, with the one in the middle sewn higher up than the other two. There’s also some messy stitching by the front left pocket.The fit, for me, is a little off. I’m 5’8” 160lbs. These are a little shorter than I’d like, a little baggy up top & a little snug across my hips. These are common issues for me though. If I want something to be long enough, it ends up being too big in other areas. These will likely be relegated to my “outside work clothes” drawer, which is totally fine with me cuz I still want to feel cute when I’m doing yard work or painting my walls. Took off one star because of the construction issues.

The Maxbee Wide Leg Pants rate high on function and style. They're super comfortable, flattering, and breathable. I got my wife a large for her size 12/5'5 form and they fit perfectly. They're not just for yoga, but can be used for any occasion. They pair well with tunics and tees and really hit the mark for style and timelessness. They're also easy maintenance and quick hang dry. The side pockets are a welcome inclusion as well. ~ Kort

The measurements aren’t accurate to fit. The reviews further made this confusing because everyone said it runs small. IT DOES NOT! It’s true to size. I could’ve went with my normal L. My pic is dark, but there’s actually plenty of space in the torso area, especially on the back side. I’m 200 lbs, 36 DD, normally size 9. The descriptions swore this ran small. Hope this helps.

The pants are very soft and stretchy but thd way the waistband sits is so unflattering. It exentuates the little pooch area and cuts off your back fat in a weird way. The pants come up a little high over my waist but that was fine for this trip bc my bottoms to my swim suit were high waisted. Also I’m 5’1 so without shoes they drag a little. Great for the beach tho. Other than that the pants fit perfectly.

The pants coming the best of yoga pants and jeans while having a cool bell bottom style. When I first took them out of the package I was afraid they would be way to small or tight. For reference, I ordered a size Medium and am 5'7", 142ish lbs. Once I put them on, I realized they have great stretch and comfort. The color is slightly lighter than I expected but I'm fine with it. They are thin for jeans, but not thin for yoga style pants. My only concern on recommending them to everyone is the length. I'm fairly tall and they are perfect so I would be concerned if a petite person ordered them as they may be too long? You can check the inseam length on the ad. I didn't find the size chart very on the ad so I just went with the size I usually wear. Good luck! They are super comfy. And I forgot to mention they have front and back pockets for your phone. 🙂

The pants fit perfectly on my waist and they are more of a light brown. No pockets sadly. They are much high waisted too, no belly showing. Have more of a sheer fabric then Jean type but that is ok. The pants fit well around the waist (NOT TOO TIGHT BUT HUGS YOUR CURVES) , and as a taller person (5’7), the length is perfect. However, after the first wear I noticed the frizzing around the pocket area. Seems as if the fabric is fraying. Not sure why or how this happened but I’ve only worn them once. I purchased the black pair and burgundy pair for work and they both pair are doing the same thing.

The pants fit well. It did give my butt a little lift. I lost a lot of muscle mass including my butt after going through a rough time and just not exercising at all. I became Self conscious since all my pants made me look like I had No butt. I wear these pants to ride my horse and have worn them and washed them at least five times now and they’re holding up good. They could be a little bit tighter around the waist. I am 5’1 and 115 lbs.

The pants have a nice stretch but does not get too baggy throughout the day and I like the high rise. I am 5'2 and 110 pounds and ordered the 4 short/ petite in green (more green in real life-the lighting in my picture is deceiving). LOVE that they have petite sizing! Will be ordering more in different colors- There are reviews of inconsistency in sizing between the different colors so I am crossing my fingers that is not the case for me..

The pants I received are 50% linen / 50% cotton, so there may be some shrinkage. Keeping this in mind, I had ordered a large and a medium. I went with the medium because I felt like I swam in the large and if I wash in cold and line dry, shrinkage should be at a minimum. Great value for your money, not super soft, but not uncomfortably scratchy either. I’m almost 5’2” and 175lbs, plus I’ve got hips! For those fun size gals whose hips don’t lie, I think depending on the look you want, either size would look great on you!

The pants look great when I wear them with a long top, as in the product listing. But the fit under the top is odd and somewhat uncomfortable, mostly in the way the fabric bags out and folds down at the crotch. If I try to pull them up higher to prevent this, they come way above my waist. The pockets are smaller than I usually find in pants of this style. I can slide in my phone (I have a small one), but the top sticks out, making me worry that it might fall out. Also, as noted in the listing, these pants are 100% polyester. I hoped they might turn out to have at least a small amount of cotton, but they are in fact entirely synthetic fiber. The one thing I really like is the brown color, which is a kind of a rich copper shade. For reference, I'm 5'4" and 155 lbs, and I ordered size Medium. I was able to machine wash these in cool water and dry them on low heat without having them change their size or shape.
