The questions we all have were fully checked off. I’m 5’2, 194 pounds, and built like a box. Seriously, you could pack me in the same rectangle box these pants arrived in. I ordered the lighter beige (think French vanilla) and the darker khaki (not a light tan but a true khaki color). I was going for the quiet luxury or “old money” aesthetic that is timeless and put together. So, how do these pants fair against the luxurious box that is me? Pros: Tummy: I have one. Do these fit mom bellies? Yes. What about the pooch effect? A little. When I sit down there is a little pooching from the fabric but if I add a blazer or sweater then it’s hardly noticeable. There is zero, and I do mean zero, stretch in the waistband so if you’re between sizes I would go up a size. I am a new size 16, from an 18, and it took me a full minute to be okay with a fitted waist since most of my pants have elastic waists. Buttons and belts: The buttons (there are 2) were sewn on well. The zipper didn’t catch and after two flights of stairs to test out whether these pants could handle the demands of being part of my closet the zipper stayed put. Belt loops are wider so while a thin belt would still look cute I would go with a standard thickness in a belt to get that old money look. Length: I could walk barefoot in them as they’re wide legs and while maybe an inch too long they’ll do nicely with heels added to them. I walked outside with a pair of booties on and they did not drag the ground. The hem on the bottom of the pant legs is nicely constructed and gives a tailored look. Thighs: Did not make me look like a can of biscuits about to make an escape. They draped nicely over my luxury edition thighs and didn’t seem to hurt my feelings. Cons: There really aren’t any that would stop me from ordering more of these. They probably require hand washing, gentle detergent, or Woolite. (Photo attached for instructions). Can you see through them? I couldn’t. I could see the line but with shape wear that would solve the issue. Bumps and lumpy bits showing? A little but this is where shape wear comes in. All in all, these are high end quality with an everybody price tag. They’re pants with substance meaning they are true corduroy trousers with a little weight to them. They’ll likely do fine through autumn but I will add a second pair a size up so that I can wear footless tights under for an extra layer of warmth during winter. 10/10 do recommend!

The reason I didn’t give these jeans 5 stars is because 1) they decreased the front pocket size and, 2) they understate their inseam by 1.5 inches. I am 5’ 8”, 138 lbs and I have long legs. I need about a 34” inseam if I’m not wearing flats. I originally ordered a 28x34, but the inseam was actually only 32.5”. Today I received the 28x36s and they fit great. I hedged my bets that they, too, would be understated by a full 1.5 inches and I was right. They are actually a 34.5” inseam (not 36”) and perfect with a 1.5” heel. The classic straight leg Wranglers give women a full hand front pocket, which I find respectful. These are only as deep as your fingers.

The reviews on the sizing of these jeans helped me tremendously, so I figured I'd do my part and pass along some information. I've had my eyes on these jeans for about a year, and when I saw that the "antique wash" version was on sale for only $20, I knew it was time to pull the trigger and a new denim staple to my fall wardrobe. My only question was...what size? When I looked at the reviews, it was clear that I needed to study the sizing charts before making my decision. My measurements: height: 5' 7" waist: 26.5 in. hips: 38 in. thigh: a little less than 24 in. inseam: 32 in. On the Wrangler's website, the sizes on the sizing chart are all listed in even numbers while the sizes offered here on Amazon are in odd numbers, so that makes things confusing. On the Wrangler's website, my waist lines up with a size 0, my hips with a size 6 and my thighs with a size 14. According to Amazon, my waist lines up with a 0, my hips with a size 5 and my thighs with an 11. On the Amazon sizing chart, the very first column says "brand size". As far as I can tell, the first number represents the waist measurement and the second number represents the in-seam. These numbers are really and may be the most in determining what size to try. I knew the jeans were supposed to have kind of a baggy fit around the thighs, but I wanted them to be more fitted across the hips and backside area so they still gave off a feminine vibe. I decided to try size 5-32 first. The length was perfect. They fit well over my cowboy boots, rainboots and lace-up/hiking boots. However, the waist was gaping and the booty and thigh area were way too loose for my taste. I decided to send them back and order a 3-32 in their place. I am much, much happier with the 3-32's. The material is stiff and feels like traditional, high quality denim, but they are not too tight anywhere. They feel equally feminine and tom-boyish (which is my style, in a nutshell). I could wear these to do yard/barn work or I could dress them up a little with a girly top and different shoes and still feel cute. The $20 markdown was too good to pass up, and I'm really glad I took the time to find my size. On the Amazon size chart under "brand size", the 3-32's are listed as 27-32. Again, I have a 26.5in waist and a 32in inseam (basically 27-32), and that is the size that ended up working for me. The rest of the waist, hip and thigh measurements that correspond to the 27-32 row on the Amazon chart do not reflect my measurements at all, but had I based my purchase solely off the "brand size" column here on Amazon, I would have gotten the sizing right the first time. I hope this review helps somebody out there who's looking for a great pair of jeans. :) Sizes purchased: 3-32 and 5-32 (compared in the photos as "3" and "5", respectively) Size kept: 3-32

The VETINEE Women’s Denim Loose Fit Overalls are definitely loose fit. There is no need to size up as a precaution like I did. Being used to clothing running a size smaller than expected when ordering online, even when ordering by specific measurements, I ordered one size up as a precautionary measure. This time, I sincerely wish I hadn’t! The fabric is medium weight and nice quality. It’s soft and very flexible. The color is exactly as advertised (at least the deep blue I ordered is). These overalls are definitely durable enough for ranch and farm. The XXL fit me excessively baggy and unflattering. Based on my measurements, XL would have been an adequately loose fit. I believe the ankle hem is at 34” for the inseam on these coveralls. They were very long. My measurements (in inches) are: Shoulder to shoulder: 17 Bust: 44 Rib Cage: 35 Waist: 34 Arm Pit to Hip bone: 19 Cloth Length (neck baseline to just below hip bone): 23 Hips: 41 Mid-Thigh: 22 Calf: 14 Inseam: 32 Collarbone Hollow to floor: 55 Dress Size: US Women’s size 12-14 or size L-XL usually. Hope this helps!

The Vetinee Women's Overalls Denim Loose Fit Straight Leg Bib Overall Jeans are the cutest and most comfy bib jeans I have ever owned and that’s saying a lot. I’ve been a bib jeans wearer for many years. As an artist and creator, bib jeans are the best to wear when working and creating. These bib jeans have some nice stretch going on, so no matter what position you’re in, sitting or standing, they are comfortable. The great thing is, they don’t continue to stretch out throughout the day. They maintain their shape and fit, nicely. According to the manufacturer’s size chart and my body measurements, I should have purchased a XXL but I did not. I went with an XL instead so the fit on me isn’t as baggy as on the model in the listing’s photographs. I wasn’t looking for baggy. I have a few pairs of those already. That said, I’m quite happy with the fit and I do love these bib jeans! For sizing reference: My Bust measures 43", My Waist Measures 38", and my Hips Measure 48". I’m 5’5” tall. I ordered the XL.

Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

There are so many things about these pants that I absolutely love! I definitely recommend these. I got a large and they fit perfect. I'm 5'2", appx. 9/10 on bottom. There are two different size charts. Don’t go by the “size chart”, I would suggest using the brand size chart. I am 5 foot 1 and 135. I saw reviews for my height and size and people bought smalls. Not sure how the smalls fit other people, because they didn’t fit me. The jeans are cute. I returned them and I’m getting a large. I will follow up when I receive them.

There is no way I can make these work! I'm a pear shape and bottom heavy, these fit great thru the hips and thighs, but even tho I have a kit that lets me move the button (I frequently have to do this!) the gap was just way too big. I also did not really like how light weight these were. I'm 5'7", 130 pounds, I bought the 29's.

There is nothing I love more than an elevated knit pant for the fall and winter. Soft, cozy, and comfortable, boy do these fit the bill. You can easily throw them on with a blazer and loafer and look put together for work. Or a cute sweatshirt and sneaker or flat for running errands or a brunch. These feel to be a really nice thickness and very plush. They are high- waisted. I’m 5’2” and the length is perfect. They hit right at the ankle. I’m not sure of how they will wash and wear so for now they are a 4 star pant, but I will update if they seem to wash beautifully and wear nicely over time. So far, I love them!!
There's a little stretch. I'm 5"8 223 lbs and I'm wearing an XL Overall, nice product
There’s an odd fit to these leggings. I am 5’4”, 114 lb. I ordered the xs and they were baggy at the knees but very tight around the waist.

There’s no hole for the button to go through?? Had to cut it with scissors for it to open 😬 Looks like the picture and doesn't feel too cheap, but you definitely have to go 2 sizes up for a true baggy fit. I’m a medium but I ordered a XL and fits how I want it to so just keep that in mind. I’m also 4’11 so if you’re short like me then there will definitely be a little extra length on the pant legs, not bad though.

There's nothing more timeless than a good pair of basic blue jeans. The Dickies Women's Perfect Shape Denim Jean-Straight Stretch lives up to its name and offers tremendous value for well made durable pants that move with you while staying in place. These Dickies are the type of jean that you'll look forward to wearing if an effortless style and fit are what you're after. They fit loosely without slippage and flatter the form with its straightforward sleek design. My wife wears size 8-10 and the 10RG fits her 5'5 ½" frame just fine, albeit a little loosely around the crotch and derrière. That's fine with her given the comfort it provides and the classic style it affords. The stitching is solid and the quality button and zipper are signature Dickies. The material feels durable yet it's not too thick or bulky. The stretchy material infused in the cotton fabric perfectly accommodates an active lifestyle. The pockets are large and practical and can be used to store personal effects like wallet or phone. The length is versatile, working with both flats and heels. My wife loves boots, so these work well as a boot cut with it's stretchy design. But as the Florida weather warms up, sandals will be her go-to footwear and these look great with open-toe shoes. The Jean-Straight Stretch stretches the form and provides a slimming effect that works great if you choose to tuck in a shirt, but it's form fitting enough for untucked longer lengths as well. ~ Kort

These jeans are so cute. I’m 5’6, 146 lbs. I bought sxecSmall.. Sxciscny normal size. I have very narrow hips and it’s difficult finding pants that fit every place correctly. Also these are not high-rise and really not mid-rise like I usually like but they work for me. The length is perfect and if the shrink a tad they’ll still be ok. I like the cotton fabric with a little stretch.

These “jeans” are pretty much perfection. They are so comfortable and fit perfectly! I took them out of the bag thinking “oh no way these are gonna work” but the second I stuck them on, just perfect. They are just a bit high at the waist for my normal taste but it’s suppose to help with tummy control and they’re still pretty loose-ish around that area so they don’t bother me when I sit down at all. I’ll have to keep tabs on the skinnies because I will definitely be wearing the heck out of these. I’m 5’2 and weigh 145. I went based on measurements from the chart and it was exactly right.

These 22Plus, Gloria Vanderbilt jeans have a huge amount of elastic give which does not allow it be body conforming in the places you want them to be. For example, they do not help you look shapely in the tush, hips and tummy. I am 5'8, 260 pounds with a generous tummy. The jeans do not hide much tummy nor subdue hips and are very loose in the bottom side of the rear, & legs due to the stretch. Without a body shaper on they are a tad loose everywhere. With a shaper on, they are tighter but still comfortable. Yet to me, still not the most flattering look. They fit a tad bit like mom jeans in my opinion. I almost exchanged them for a 20 Plus just to get a more flattering silhouette. But I decided against it as one thing that they definitely are is the most comfortable jeans I ever wore. So, I decided to keep them as I am of the age (56) that comfort trumps style. My first designer jeans at age 14/15 in 1979 were Gloria Vanderbilts (see me in them in 3rd pic) . So buying these were like visiting an old friend that you greatly missed. If nothing else, I am decidely loyal to old friends and our fond memories.

These American Apparel Easy Jeans are exactly what my daughter wanted. She is extremely tall and skinny for her age and they fit perfectly. We got the XXS and she 5'6 97 pounds but these are the kind of jeans that will fit if you fluctuate 5-10 pounds up or down. I was worried that the XXS wouldn't be long enough but these have a 32" inseam. She has a 23" waist in Hollister or A&E. They look skin tight but they're easy to move in, squat, sit, etc. Not bulky or stiff, she can actually do Gymnastics in these. I would prefer a medium/dark wash but other than that they are worth it.

These are $10 pants, but are still cute, while they last. I am 5’3” and typically a size 6. If you are petite, you will be thrilled. It appears a lot of women didn’t notice the inseam is only 27” long. So, if you are over 5’4”, these will be short on you, and you may like that or style them by rolling them up. I like the placement of the pockets on my flat butt. The front pockets are fake. The waistband is nice and stretchy and I can pull on over my pooch. I wore them for about 7 hours today and they’re not uncomfortable, however, look at photos so you can see how the rips wear. The strings coming off are nylon, not denim so they ball up. I will just trim them. I shared photos of the stringy balls, the slits, and my outfit. My daughter thought I looked pretty cute. I paired these with booties, but would look cute with flats too. I would order more, but they’re sold out now. I do wonder how see through they would be in white... these black are pretty thick though :) Will wear them until they look like $10 pants.

These are a beautiful color, a heavier gauze material, and for as big as they look when you pull them out of the package, very flattering. I'm 5'8" tall, way approximately 160 pounds and they are long enough and flatter my body. Adorable with flats, and loose enough to be cool in the summer but yet dressy. They go with everything! These are a decent pair of black dress stretch pants. I feel that these will go well with some blouses and shirts that I have for work. I will need a belt with these as there isn't any definition in the waistline of these and I feel that they may tend to slide down during the day without a belt. These fit as expected. They formed well to my body without making me feel self-conscious. I am 5'2" and these did touch the floor in the back when I was standing barefoot. If I wear these with a platform shoe or heel they will do nicely and not touch the ground. I feel that these would look nice on a variety of body types. I am rather curvy, but I think this would look nice on larger women and women who are on the thinner or less curvy side.

These are a dream! I'm normally a medium and I got the small, they're a little snug but fit well. I'm 5'2 130lbs and I have to wear 3in heels for them to fit length wise. The top is a thick elastic band. No zippers or button. They're like fashionable leggings. You need them. Be sure to follow me on Instagram for more Amazon reviews and home decor @xosimplylo

These are a dupe of EB Denim’s Fredrick Jean. I was super excited to find these since the original retails for 400$. However, the photos on Amazon are of the original, not the dupe. These are a light weight denim in a light wash with faded spots on the front and back of thighs that are almost white (and not flattering). The 3 seams across the knees are so cute. There is a fit issue for me - too much fabric in the front crotch area. It bunches up weird. I’m 5’4 and these just skim the ground, perfect for wearing flats or low heels. I’m usually a 14/16 in pants and the XL fits. However, I’m used to stretch denim and these are non-stretch. They do have an elastic back to the waist band. I find them a little uncomfortable in the front waist area. They are more straight legged than barrel jean. I think these are a return for me.

These are a fun, light weight goucho-style pant. I am in love with the cool toned 'blue grey' color, which is going to be my neutral alternative to olive bottoms that tend to be too warm-toned for my other favorite clothes/colors. I like the pockets, and the (matching!) zipper and button waist, with an elastic band at the back. There are belt loops all around the waist the lay very flat, so won't gap out be super obvious if you choose not to wear a belt. The stitching and seams are fine, but not super quality. The fabric is a (very wrinkly) linen style, that I found comfortable and not as itchy as another review had mentioned. I like the length on my 5'2" frame. I do think the size chart is a bit inaccurate in the US numerical listing but not necessarily in the measurements, which can get tricky; I followed my hip measurements so I didn't get too much fabric gathering which made the waist just a little tighter than I'd prefer, though the elastic in the rear waist helps. Overall I like these pants, but I think they might be priced a little high for their quality ($33.99 at time of review). I'm excited to see how much wear I can get out of them before they fall apart this spring/summer.

These are a great fit – they have a little bit of stretch which is nice. They’re not a heavy material or too stiff, and I like that the cuffs seem sturdy: they are sewn into the seams on the sides so that they won’t come out. I personally needed shorts that were a bit longer, but they’d be a great option for someone else! For reference: I’m 5’8, slender, shorter torso/longer legs, weigh about 145lb, waist ~31”, hips ~39”. Got size 27.

These are a great length! Love the color! But the legs are very baggy. Would probably fit well if you have thick thighs. Great if you like a loose fit. Ordering a smaller size won't work because then the waist won't fit. Unfortunately, I have to return them. I ordered a size 1-36" inseam. I am 5'9", 115 pounds, waist = 24.5", hips 34.5", upper thigh = 17.5".

These are a great soft and elastic material that are form fitting without being overly tight. That's a plus. However at the same time, the cut seems just a tad off, giving me some tightness in the seams of the interior inseams, giving a look that needs to be covered up with something longer. At the waist, the material hugs just the bit of flub I have at my sides and kinda bulges out. So... needless to say, I'll be wearing cardigans with these pants and not tucking in any cute tank tops. The material is soft and they do look flattering at the legs. I am 5'7", and I do wish they were a bit longer, but I knew the inseams wouldn't be the length I need to reach my ankles. All in all, decent dress pants that are pretty comfortable.

These are a nice light pair of summer weight denim pants. I think they're meant to be crops, but I am 5'3"and these came down to my ankles (which I prefer anyway). The material is generally quite soft but some of the seams are very rough, especially around the waist area. It's like they chose to burn the thread ends rather than tie them off. I would make sure to always tuck in my shirt with these or wear substantial undergarments that come up to the waist (at least) to protect your skin. The thread roughness aside, I like the stretch of these pants. I wish they were a bit more structured in the midsection. Since they are so very high waisted, one would hope they would provide some tummy control, but these just move and stretch with you, which makes them incredibly comfortable. The green is very light and makes for easy pairing with many colors of tops and shoes. I normally wear an 8 or 29 and these pants in a large fit me well.

These are a nice pair of palazzo pants. They are not too thin or see through. They have pockets. And the length is just perfect not going past my ankles with the right fit in a size small. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small fits me best. So, do order true to size. All the stitching and sewing was done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. I do recommend these pants. They are perfect for spring/summer wear.

These are a really comfortable pair of jeans. They are in the more current style, being on the shorter side and flared. I have to say I really like the fit. They have a good bit of stretch to them, so the size 18 fit me fine, and that's my usual size for most jeans. I like the color, which is a bit lighter, but it is still what I would consider a standard jean color. They are relatively high-waisted, but due to all the stretch, they are not going to "hold" you in the way jeans with stiff material will. I like the length on me, I'm 5'6 for reference, and they hit just at my ankle. My only pet peeve is that, basically, the entire pair of jeans is standard with no distressing, except for a random tiny spot on one of the back pockets. I think because this is the only spot for any distressing, it looks more like an actual tear or rip than intentional distressing. It's not a big deal, but that's the only thing I don't like about these jeans.

These are a well made pair of jeans that surprised me with a little more flare than I had anticipated. I thought it was a small flare, but when I got them I found they are full fledged bell-bottoms. That is fine with me. I loved bell-bottoms when I was growing up (yes, I am that old, lol) but my mom mostly did not allow me to have them, so I was thrilled the past 15 years or so when they have been in style a few times. They are of medium weight and softness. The stitching is solid and looks nice on them. I like the seam that runs down the front of them. I can remember a long while back when that was a style too. They have 2 front and 2 back pockets, all of them functional. I got the X-Large and they fit me very nicely with a bit of room to spare due to the 2% elastane in the material. The color is really nice and I think it is just right for what I was looking for in a pair of jeans. Another nice thing is that the length for my 5'4" height is perfect without needing to be shortened. I am very pleased with these jeans and happy that I got them! Definitely can recommend these for nice looking and comfortable jeans.

I have trouble with jeans being too tight in the waist if they fit elsewhere, so these are actually more comfortable for me. For reference, I have bought the smallest size jeans available to accommodate my little booty and legs but feel absolutely unwell from how tight they are in the waist (specifically mostly cotton madewell jeans in a 23). The button is a little cheap/loose as others have mentioned on this brands other listings. I don't think it will be an issue for me, but if they fit my waist more snuggly it would be. I'm 5'6" and they're perfect length for tennis shoes, but if you're any shorter you'll need heels. They have a frayed hem, but there is a hem stitch above it. So you could cut it, but I don't think it would look the same. These are definitely stretchy jeans. Wish they had a slightly higher cotton content because I fear baggy knees in my future, but like I said, the pattern helps them look sleeker than they are. I got lots of compliments. Decent value and free people dupe.

These are extremely comfortable since they are stretch denim, and they're exactly what I was looking for. Since the majority of the reviews mentioned you might want to go up a size - and I normally wear an 11/12 - I went with a 13. I am 5'8" and 145 pounds. They are just a skosh looser than I would like, and I probably could have stuck to my regular size, but the looseness is also to be expected from any stretchy fabric. And I need to breathe. Can't have it both ways. I haven't laundered them yet, so I'm hoping a little time in the dryer may resolve that. Or eating more croissants... Note: I need to mention I am 64, so you youngins may want to ignore this review. I have PTSD from trying on jeans. It has always been a horrible experience. First, I have a slight case of Dunlops disease...which stands for "the belly done lopped over the belt." But the waist on these is higher, so my midriff is under the covers, where it should be. Secondly, I grew up with skinny thighs, so jeans always bag at my thighs, both in front and in back! And with barely a booty to speak of, all the tea in china isn't going to help these jeans "lift" mine. One can't expect miracles if there's nothing to work with. But I have to admit they are a little more firming around the tush than most jeans. There's a slight looseness in the front crotch area that I'm not thrilled with but, again, that could be my lack of padding in my thighs. The length is perfect, though, and overall I'm really pleased with these jeans.

These are flare jeans that are high-waisted and cropped. I am 5,2 and these come to me as a regular pant they don't seem cropped for me because of my height. They are a very soft Gene material that has rips here and there. They are high-waisted and fit very well for me. I ordered a size based on the size chart, but I think I could have gone down one size smaller. They are very comfortable because of the material and the fact that it's softer than a standard jean. These are flattering in the right places and hide my little mom pooch. they are also long enough for my long legs so if you’re shorter than i’d say 5’9 these may be a little too long. so comfortable though and hug my waist perfectly.

These are for my 12 year old. She is almost 5'1" and very slender (87lbs) with pre-teen hips. These are so freaking adorable on her! They're definitely a bit loose in the waist so she's tied a cloth belt on as you can see in the pictures. They're also a bit loose in the hips, but it looks fine, especially for a 12 year old. They fit pretty close to the skin through the thighs, but not tight. The jeans seem to have a good amount of stretch so I expect she'll be able to wear them at least a couple years as she grows into them. They'll certainly work well for someone larger than she is depending on how snug you like them Two things to know: As you can see in the pictures, the length is pretty good on her. Other reviews saying these run short are why I decided they might work for her, and it's true. She's wearing shoes with a heel, but they're still not that long on her. You could have legs a little longer, but if you're fairly tall these likely won't hang as low as you want. It looks exactly like the hem wasn't sewn which I thought was a flaw at first. It looked cute that way so it didn't matter, but you can't clearly see how it looks like you can turn the bottom under twice for the hem, or even looks a bit like it was turned under but not stitched. Looking at the Amazon photos again made me realize they're actually designed that way. They'll probably develop a bit of a cute frayed edge. Just look closely at those pictures and be sure that's the hem you want... It stitch it up yourself, it would be easy to do.

These are great looking pants. I like the style, the color and the linen look. Because the fabric is 60% rayon and 30% linen, the pant should be breathable and will be great for hot weather wear. I am 5’ 5” and 158 lbs. I usually wear an XL but because I wanted to make sure of a loose flowy look, I ordered a 2XL (a 1XL was not available). This 2XL is comfortable, and fits at my waist but they are about 3” too long in length. They are very loose and flowy on me but because of the design they do look nice.There were loose threads both inside and outside at the seams which I snipped off, otherwise the stitching is straight and unbroken. The wide band is comfortable and well sewn. The pockets too are well sewn and lay nicely.Here is the reason that I am rating it three stars and this may just be a flaw in my pant but I caution others to check the fabric closely. On my pair at the upper back hip on one side is a material flaw. In the photo, I placed a penny for perspective. It is very small and probably wouldn't be noticeable. My concern is that because it is a thin thread spot in the fabric and being at the hip location, will it form a small hole from sitting. I am really disappointed by this. I would have given a much higher rating had there been no flaw.

These are great pants. My photo really doesn't do them justice (forgive my dusty mirror!). I ordered size Large in black. The material is nice; it has an almost crepe-y feel to it. Great for the office; I wore it with a fitted sleeveless shirt and nude flats for a stylish outfit with nice balance. Fantastic business casual pants for warmer weather.

These are great quality, just as you would expect from Levi. They are more mid-rise, not low rise and they fir nicely on the waist/hips. I have knocked a point off the star rating because of the poor sizing communication. I ordered a '27 Regular' to fit my 5'4" and 125lb frame. The sizing chart indicated that the inseam of a 27 - which is considered an Alpha 'Small' - would be 30 inches. Unfortunately, what was delivered was a 32 inseam so they are 2 inches too long for me. I'm so frustrated! They need to change the description to exactly match the jeans you're getting. So, instead of labeling them '27 Regular' why don't they label them '27 x 32' just like the Levi's label does on the back of the jeans! I would love these jeans if it wasn't for the 'too-long' issue. Ugh!

These are high-end, nice looking, stylish sweatpants, not like the wrinkly, sloppy looking types at all. They look nice enough to go somewhere in without being embarrassed to be seen. My giant cell phone will actually fit inside one of the leg flap pockets! I ordered size Medium in Black and they fit me perfectly, except that they're just a tad long (before having laundered them). For reference, I'm 5'4", long-waisted (therefore proportionally somewhat short-legged), and about 124 lbs with a 29" waist and 38" hips.*UPDATE on gradual SHRINKAGE of the LENGTH of the INSEAM & RISE: I thought it might be to that after having worn and washed & dried these sweatpants a lot over the past couple of months...they've shrunk in length a bit! I suppose that's due to the cotton in their fabric content. So, they're no longer too long for me! The length of the rise has also shrunk some, so they still are high waisted, but they fit/look a bit better in that area as well.

These are not actually what I consider "jeggings". These are colored, fleece-lined leggings. I ordered the "navy blue- fleece-lined", and they could be mistaken for jeans from a distance, but not up close, since the material is usual leggings material in a blue color, with light stitching. They are very stretchy and comfortable, and pass the "squat test" (i.e. they are not see-through when I bend over). The back pockets are actual pockets, but the front ones are just for looks. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 165 pounds, have a short torso with a flat derriere and big stomach, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 x 32 (medium leggings and pants at Old Navy and H&M). I ordered these leggings in a large since fleece-lined leggings sometimes run smaller, and they fit perfectly without slipping down or digging into my waist. They do not offer much tummy control, and the back pockets are placed a little low for my preference. On me, they are full length and not 7/8 (see my pics). Overall, I am happy with the warmth and comfort of these leggings, which I plan to wear all winter with long sweaters. I would definitely order them again.

These are not what I would call velvet. It's more of a stretchy weighty velvety material. This fabric will likely hold up to wear better than velvet which is more delicate. The material is surprisingly heavy in a good way, which makes them hang nicely like in a draping kind of manner. The weight also helps to make them appear well made and a good quality. I am 5' 6", usually order a 31 inseam, and the length is to the floor so taller girls it works. I am between a L and XL but usually opt for XL for bottoms as I'm a bit hippy and these have ample room. I'm pretty sure I could do a L and they would just not be as flowing. So I call this ample true to size. I should also mention there are seam pockets and they don't make my hips look bigger. The sides lie flat. I want these over my typical house clothing of leggings because I'm finding the clinginess of leggings bothers me, but I don't want to go all the way to PJ bottoms. These are very nice for the price and I'm especially pleased with the weight. These are so nice you could probably wear them out with a sparkly top such as New Years or Christmas, but for me they will be stylish lounge wear.I'm so happy with them that I'd say it's a favorite item from Amazon. They are that nice. Also may purchase a second and may try a L.

These are really soft, priced reasonably, and very comfortable. Good points: - sizing is pretty accurate for the chart (I'm usually a L and the chart suggested XXL which was good) - color is beautiful - fabric is lightweight and super soft but it does show every dimple and buldge - The expenditure is totally on the mark for the value. Draw backs: - the waist is so high! A good 4" above my belly button. - the top seam is tightened with elastic so you can slide it down your waist without it creeping up - it has these faux pockets, so they just add extra bulk to the belly area and serve no purpose - it came wrapped in not one but three single use plastic bags for no good reason - it has a strong chemical smell, likely due to be wrapped up in so much plastic that needs a few times washing to get out - they are little long for my 5' 5" self so I have to hem them by a good inch or so Overall, these are cute and very cozy. I love the color too. I'd recommend them but be aware of the above issues before you buy.

I wanted comfortable pants to wear during my pregnancy and even with maternity pants the crotch area would be too tight and cut into me, and with how much my hips spread my thighs felt too restricted (not to mention that maternity clothes cost a small fortune). So I took a gamble and ordered these 2 sizes larger and the XL are PERFECT! (Hoping I can get a little more wear out of them postpartum until I can get my body back) For reference, I am 36 weeks pregnant, 5'5", size Medium in Motherhood Maternity/Old Navy Maternity, and 190 pounds. Most of my pregnancy weight is in my belly and hips. P.S. The length is good too, fits about the same as the mediums. There is a little bunching around my ankles but nothing bothersome, I've worn both tennis shoes and boots comfortably with these.

These are so freaking comfortable!! I’m 5’1/2. I weigh 120lbs. I float between 2-4. The smallest they offer is a 4. They fit very well. I don’t think I would want any smaller. They are a bit stretchy too. O ordered in Uber black pin stripe too!

These are so good for the price! I shared them on my IG and they have been best sellers for weeks. I’m wearing a size small and I’m 5’1 and 121lbs. They are comfortable but super flattering and they fit so well!

These are so light and comfy it feels like wearing nothing. The short length is perfect for me at 5'1". I am between a medium and large but went with the large in hopes that if I have a pregnant belly soon, they'll still fit. Glad I did because the large fits well but with room to grow. These are so nice! I wasnt sure how i was expecting them to fit but they fit amazingly and they go all the way down to my ankles and im 5’10. These are so soft and stretchy they don’t really feel like jeans! I love how they look from the front and I thought they were perfect until I looked at the back. They aren’t flattering on my body because the rear pockets are too far apart. They are a perfect length though. 5’2” 158 lbs usually a 10-12 in pants and purchased a size 31 short.

These are soft and comfortable. However, with the high waist and smooth material good fitting underwear is ESSENTIAL, also make sure your shirt is tucked in perfectly, or wear one that needs no tucking. These have good stretch, I wear an 8 curvy L cut in jeans, these are a medium they fit my waist and stretch to accommodate my large hips and my booty. I can totally see myself enjoying these in the next few months here in Florida as it will be nice and warm, like today it's January and 80. These are lightweight and don't have that slightly stiff feeling of a pure linen pair of pants, instead they almost have a silky tendency! Now, the only drawback for me as a busy mom, is not being able to throw these into the dryer with everything else. I did wash these and then draped them across the rack to dry before trying them on again for pictures, I didn't notice any shrinkage in regular washing! I think I might need another pair for variety's sake, instead of my beloved leggings. I'm 5'10" about 160, long torso, long thighs compared to calves, for reference 4 stars because I'm being persnickity about not being able to toss in the dryer on medium with everything else.

These are soft jeans. The material stretches a touch. They are well made (the stitching is well done). I ordered a size 29 which fits me (I am a size 8). I like the light color (goes with so many colors). The zipper moves smoothly. The legs are long (I am 5’ 7”). I would need to wear high heels (I do not mean a 2” or 3” heel). I am not a fan of the extremely wide legs. I was a child in the 70s (I am dating myself with this reference), and I did not have bell bottom pants this wide. You cannot walk with all this excess fabric (well, a weird duck walk maybe). The style makes me look bigger than I am (and that is not the look that I want). These pants are a hard pass.

These are some lovely, elegant fancy pants, at least that’s what I called them! 😊 They do come quite wrinkled up, but nothing more than throwing them in the dryer for a few minutes to take out all the wrinkles, did the job! They are more on the thin side material but they are not see-through! For size reference I am 5’2 and 100 pounds. I ordered the extra small. They are a little bit big on me in the thigh area, I do wish they were a little bit more fitted. They do also come a little long on me too. I am wearing high heels(in the pictures) but if I were to wear flats they would be way too long, for that reason I will be tailoring them to my size. Honestly, I wish I didn’t have to go through the process of tailoring them, but unfortunately I am short, so I tend to need to do that very often. In spite of the sizing, I still would recommend these pants, honestly I don’t think it’s the pants fault that I’m short!!! 😊

This does NOT, in any way, take away from the comfort of the pants, just the look. The legs are loose, and nice and long. Throw a shirt over this and they'll look great.

These are some seriously GREAT pants. They are made of cotton, spandex, and nylon. This gives them a great weight and a fabric that doesn't wrinkle easily. They are stretchy including the waistband so they are comfortable. I got 8 which is my normal size. If you are between two sizes, get the bigger one. You have the option of choosing short length or regular length. I chose regular. I am 5'4" and this is just right. It would even be floor-length for someone a few inches taller. The pants are somewhat formal so I am curious to know how the shorter version would look, it is a personal preference. These pants will be your go-to due to how easy they are to wear. AND they are washable!!

These are some unique and cute jeans that can be dressed up or down. And they are long leg friendly!! Yes! It's hard finding pants long enough for my 32.5" inseam. I can actually wear low heels with these jeans. I ordered a Size 6 according to the sizing chart in the photos but these pants are too big. I usually wear a size 2 or 4. I am 5'10, 123 lbs, 32B-26-35 for reference. So with those measurements a size 6 should fit but these jeans are too big in the waist, butt and hip area. If you read the product description, the sizing they list there is probably more correct as they list a Size 2 with a 26" waist and 34" hips. Wish I would have read this instead of using the sizing chart in the photos. Also these are very high waisted. I have a really high waist, basically have no torso, all legs and in order for me not to have a droopy looking crotch area, I had to pull these up really high. Luckily, I know how to alter clothing to fit me the way I like so I will wash them first to see if there's any shrinkage and then take the pants in as needed.

These are somehow now my favorite jeans!! They are super soft and comfortable. I bought them for a disco party, and ended up wearing them all the time. I will be getting another pair.I am 5’4, and with low herls, the length was fine. I normally wear a 24/25 jean and the small was perfect.

These are SOOOOO me! I got the ones with the skulls on them. These are a lightweight material. Definitely not for wearing in the snow. Will be perfect for summer and workouts as your skin will be able to breathe. Although I said it's a thinner material, it feels substantial and well made. I shared a few photos showing with a flash on that you can't see my underwear. Stitching feels like it won't come apart. And will last through activities not just relaxing or running errands. It feels really buttery on the inside just like the outside. Very comfortable to wear. I'm 5'2" and the legs go down to my feet, but I'm not walking on them like sometimes I can have happen because I'm short. I can walk around barefoot and not have to depend on shoes to keep them off the ground. Being I have a short torso, the waist goes about an inch above my belly button. So if you have a longer torso, this should be great for you and you won't have to be concerned about bending over and your underwear showing in the back. I will say, while the leggings themselves have a decent amount of stretch to them, do pay attention to the waist measurements. Because the elastic band doesn't. So if you have wider hips or backside, you could have a issue getting over them. So measure those too and make your size choice. Totally recommend.

These are such a good find! The quality is great. They are a lightweight material, high waisted with a zip fly. They have just the right amount of barrel without looking weird. I have washed them several times and they are still in great condition. I only dry them for a little and hang to finish drying. I noticed the fly will wrinkle if I don’t. I am 5’6.5” and bought the size 10. The fit is so good and the perfect length. I have been reaching for these all the time! They feel like sweatpants and are such an easy piece to throw an outfit together. Definitely will be purchasing another color!

These are such great work pants! Front and back pockets. Front a bit small, back ones my iPhone 7 fits in. I’ve never gotten so many compliments from men AND women in my lifetime as I’ve gotten from these, and I’m 52!! I’m starting to wear them outside work. I’m 5’5”, 145lbs. I wear size 8 with this brand. I got average, they’re a bit long, but not obscene. Petite would have been better, but not offered at time of purchase. I bought my first pair at a brick and mortar store. They only offered one color so I had to come to Amazon to see if there were more options. As you’ll see in my pic, the brick and mortar store pants have a sewn-in tag while the Amazon ones have an ironed on one. While skeptical about that, they still fit very well. The ones I got from the actual store, I’ve had issues with pilling. I wash inside-out in cold water and air dry, so not sure what’s going on. I haven’t had the new pants long enough to comment on it yet. Sometimes simply getting a different color has a whole different outcome on the wash-and-wear effect. If you’re wanting comfy work pants that are actually useable and still look stylish, these are the ones to get!

These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

These are super comfortable and I’ll definitely be wearing them to work. The short length is great for my 5’2 frame. Only drawback for me is the waist was a little big. I wish it sat a tad higher on my waist since the crotch part of the pants hangs low. Haven’t washed them yet but other reviews mention not ironing the pants so I’ll steer clear of doing that.

These are super comfortable and super flattering. I am between a small and a medium but in general, medium pants are the best fit for me. These are a bit loose in the waist, so I’d say they run a touch large, but not much. I’m 5’6” and these just hit the floor, so if you are taller, they will be short!

These are super comfortable pants. I did get the extra large and there are a little bit on the big side. I'm not a pregnant individual. I am 5'7, 190 lb. So, I'm a chunky lady. So, I thought a pair of pants like this would give me that little extra around the top. I wish I would have ordered the L instead of the XL. It's a little big around the legs and a little bit loose on the waist. But, the length is good and the quality is there. They're a nice boot cut so you can wear them with pretty much any kind of shoe . These are nice jeans. I'm still going to wear them even though they're a little bit loose and I'll just add a belt. It'll look great with a sweater. I like the fact that they are thicker and we're going into a season that's cold. They might be a little heavy for summer time. But all in all, they are really nice jeans. If you have a bigger tummy or you're pregnant it'll be nice because you've got that little extra elastic on the sides. I pulled it out the top to see how far it would go and it really had a lot of give.

These are super comfy and cute, good quality BUT they will only fit if your waist is the same size as your butt. There is a lot of gap in the back. I got a size 6 short; im 5'4" and 135lbs, and this is a typical problem for me with pants. They will fit great around my thighs and butt (which these do + perfect length) but not my waist (or reverse so I can't size down) :/

These are super comfy and have a wonderful high waist. I am 5’4 and 115lbs. I got the small, which is a little baggy. I think an XS would be a better fit. Otherwise, I love them very much. These are surprisingly awesome pants for the price. The material is thick and has a lovely drape. They are soft and stretchy, and they don't feel at all like you're wearing slacks. I'm 5'10" and I rarely order pants online because everything is too short, but I took the risk because I saw reviewers saying they were long, and I'm happy to that they fit my 34" inseam perfectly (they just brush the tops of my feet)! The pants are very flattering and I feel good in them. I was nervous about size so I ordered up to a 16 because there wasn't a size chart when I ordered, and they're definitely a size too large for me in the waist , so I'd say they fit true to US sizes. I will have the waist taken in, which is easy to do, but I like the fit on my hips and thighs--they feel more modern than the skinny fit of all the pants from 10 years ago. I would definitely order these again. They wash well--I use cold, delicate--and I just hang them to dry. They don't even require ironing. They feel and look a lot like slacks I have from Banana Republic that I paid $98 for, so they are an absolute steal IMO. I will likely order a couple of pairs in other colors (and I'll order the size 14). For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape.

These are very cute but not the best fit. I'm 5'4, 123ish pounds. I have a disproportionately large stomach but ordered a size 4 since most of my 4s fit me at this weight. I immediately returned them for the 6, which I kept but the waist is STILL a bit tighter than I'd like. Compared to my other sixes, they're tighter all around but fit fine. I do wish the waist was a bit bigger and more in line with every other 6 I have (which would make them a bit too big, actually).I was surprised by the length. I’d prefer less fabric at the back and looser elastic. I wouldn’t recommend these in the light color but the darker colors are likely fine. I ordered my usual size M.

These are very lightweight flow pants. It has an elastic waist with a little bow to tie. The elastic is triple sewn and stays up, has a smocking sewn look. The length is very long. I am 5’7” and this is the length they are. I have worn a wedge shoe with this to bring up the length. In the picture I’m wearing flip-flops. I am going to try to shorten these so I could wear them with a flat sandal during the summer. Other than the length, these pants are perfect. Even though I’m 5’7” I don’t have long legs.I like the black-and-white color. I change this with an all white shirt, all black shirt or a combo.As far as washing, I wash them in the washing machine, but I do hang them to dry. These are very nice leggings. They are super soft and have that buttery, soft, and silky feeling to them. There is a lot of compression, and took a little work to get them on, but once on I think they looked good and felt great! I'm 5'6, and the length they came at fit great, but it seems they can simply be cut to shorten as needed. The inside has a sort of built-in band to create a lift. It feels a little strange at first but I think i will get use to it. It's not uncomfortable per se, but you "notice" it when wearing them. Overall, I really like these leggings.

These are very nice lounge pants, they come in an array of colors, and I am extremely pleased with them!! I will definitely end up buying more colors.- The material feels sturdy & well-made, and it is fairly thick, so these pants are a little warm for summer, but they will make great lounge pants for wearing out during spring or fall or lounging around the house any time of year.- The legs are very wide, making them incredibly roomy & forgiving of any less than tone areas- No give in the legs (not that you need it), but there is plenty of stretch in the waist and I don't even need the drawstring to help keep them up- Fits is true to size. I normally wear smalls in everything (US 4-6), and the small fits me perfectly. For reference, I am 5'3", 125 lbs, 33" bust, 28" waist, 36" hips, size 3 in pantsI normally need a petite inseam (29"), so these are a little long for my short legs, but if I tug them up a bit, they don't drag the ground & get stepped on as I walk. In the photos, I have them pulled up to my belly button- I love that these pants also have pockets! They are not deep pockets, but they are enough to hold my iPhone 12, car keys, etc. and pockets are always quite convenient- The item description says, "Dry Clean", but the tag says to wash in cold water on delicate, tumble dry low, remove promptly. I have been washing them on regular & everything seems great so far. The material is definitely not one that feels like it requires a delicate cycle.The tag also indicates that they can be tumble dried on low heat, but I decided to air dry due to this conflicting information (and if any damage occurs, it usually happens in the dryer). The material is a little stubborn & likes to hold those wrinkles, so you will need to do a little steaming or ironing, but I think this is a small price to pay for such cozy pants!I think you could dress these pants up & wear them to work or out on a date, but they make incredible lounge pants too. Most - if not all - of my pants are either one or the other, so this is a very unique quality! I am extremely pleased with these pants, and I enthusiastically recommend them!!

These ASTYLISH jeans have some fun front pockets - so in style right now. I love the wide stitched hem on the legs too. As far as being high waisted, on me they were high in the back and fit lower in the front, not like my usual high waisted jeans, that fit around my true waist. I do have a long torso, so maybe that had something to do with the fit. For reference, I'm 5'6" 120# with 30" waist and 35.5" hips. So a size 4 hips, size 10 waist - buying jeans can be a challenge!! Since these had the stretch, from experience, I went with size 8 - the fit was perfect! However, had they went around my true waist it would have been a tight fit. I took a gamble and it worked out. I also found the inseam to be 28.5" after washing in cold and drip drying. I usually prefer a longer inseam, however for the rest of summer I'll be wearing flat slide sandals or low sneakers, so they'll be fine. Make sure to scroll down in the product description for size chart, not the one on top. I'll enjoy wearing these through the end of summer, early fall. Cute!

These belts are made of soft PU leather, making them fairly standard. They're suitable for keeping your clothing in place or adding a touch of style. I appreciate the pale gold hardware, which is a nice change from the usual silver. While I prefer rose gold, this color is acceptable. I'm grateful that this brand offers an extra small size. Since I often wear high-waisted bottoms, I need a smaller belt for my waist rather than my hips. For reference, I wear US size 0/00 or XS/XXS, with a waist measurement of 24-26 inches and hips measuring 32-34 inches. The price tag for these are around $10 for me right now, which I think is a great value for your wardrobe.

These belts are perfect to accent dresses or high waisted skirts!! They are such a complimentary accessory! You need them in your wardrobe to complete the look!! I'm 4'10" with a 26" waist, & a medium fits great in all the right places! I originally got a small, but it was too tight & made it look like I had rolls! lol Mind you, I'm only 115 lbs. So it might be best to size up for comfort!

These belts are so cute and versatile! I’ve been struggling with putting together cute office outfits and these thin belts always seem elevate the look. The gold buckle adds a nice touch and doesn’t feel cheap. I’m 5’4 and weigh ~130lbs and got the waist below 37” which fits comfortably well at both the waist and hips since it’s easily adjustable. Definitely recommend!

These black slacks exceeded every expectation I had. I never have any luck with these types of pants on here because of the material always being too tight. These pants are absolute perfection. I am wearing a size 4 and I am 5’3, 135 lbs and my lower measurements are 27-36. The material has a nice elasticity to it so it stretched nicely around my waist/hips. I love that it is high waisted and that they don’t hug my butt and thighs. I love the loose hang of them. They are very flattering to wear and look great with a blazer. I would recommend these pants.

These bottoms are comfortable and I love the pleat down the front. I'm 5-11, 180 lbs. I purchased a medium, pant size 8 for reference. These came out great, they feel heavy duty and the definitely do the job, they are pretty hot to be in during hot weather but that's to be expected from pants like these, I took these out for a 2 day hike and they held up great, I felt like I could abuse these as much as I liked and they would still hold up. There are a few specific aspects of the pants that I will go over: 1. The knee pads: Trying to insert the knee pads will cause you to re-evaluate your entire life but once you understand the technique it's not a big deal, other than that they function great and really protect your knee when kneeling, only complaint is that they are very hard and inflexible so they can get uncomfortable when sitting. 2. The size: I measured my waist before ordering and ordered a 34" waist size, but when I tried the pants on, they were too loose on my waist even after tightening the waist adjustment mechanism, so I was forced to use a belt (which I was planning on using anyway so it wasn't that bad). 3. Overall comfort when walking: While walking in these pants after a while I noticed that the extra reinforcement material on the crotch area in the back of the pants would fold into itself and would bother me so I had to readjust it every once in a while, also the material of the pants is not very stretchy but I didn't find it bothering.

These capris are less high waisted than they are wide legged. I like the more modern, casual fit; comfortable without looking too frumpy. They are 95% cotton, which is rare these days. They did not shrink after washing in cold and hanging to dry. Although I am purposely choosing to roll with the slightly wrinkled look for a more causal vibe. This look is ideal for warm weather when a skirt or shorts could be warn without the concern of showing your legs. Yes please. I ordered a small, although I usually wear a medium or 6 in pants. There really isn't a need for a belt, but it does jazz up the overall look and was a thoughtful accompaniment. The belt is brown 'pleather' and looks surprisingly decent. But with the elastic waist band in the back, a belt really isn't necessary. There are belt loops that run around the entire waistband. I like the simple styling of the two front pockets. but feel the cut in the back makes my rear look a little pancake-like and baggy. I have short legs and am 5'7", 138. The size small is quite ample. Anyone smaller would for sure want an XS, which I do not believe is available. These capris will be a welcome addition to my spring/summer wardrobe. I love a good neutral color and appreciate the simple style, comfort and the modern, wide leg-look.

These denim wide leg capri's used to be called Gauchos. They are high wasted, probably a bit too high for my frame, so I have a little excess material that is not filled out in the front. I chose the Light Blue denim. I am 5'4 or 5' 3.5" tall and approximately 120 or 122 lbs, I ordered size 8. I probably should have taken a size 6 but you can never tell with the cut of each brand. The material content is 70% cotton, 28.5 % polyester and 1.5% elastane. The denim feels like a good quality of denim (even though I prefer not to have polyester in my denim). The front pockets are functional and the back two (2) pockets are not. The front have a four (4) button closure. The length of the pants are 28 and 3/4 inches long. These nice denim wide leg capris/gaucho 's would be just fine all year round but of course in the winter you would wear either tights or socks with knee high boots. It is a recommend.

These faux leather shorts are cute and fit well. They are pretty stretchy all over (including the waist), so they'll easily accommodate different shaped bodies. They seem well made (seams seem strong) and the zipper runs smoothly. The outside 'leather' is a little shiny (iridescent (?)) looking with a bit of a woven fabric pattern (it is not a smooth looking finish). I took an up close pic of the pocket area so you can see the material's finish. I like the way the material looks. The inside of the shorts is the backside of the 'leather' and it's a soft polyester/nylon feeling material, so the 'leather' isn't touching your skin, but the shorts are not lined with a separate material. I took a pic that I hope shows what I'm talking about.I ordered a size L (my normal size) based on their size chart and they fit comfortably (I have a 32" waist and 40" hips). They fit true to size. If you are in between sizes and want a comfortable fit, I would say size up and if you want a more skin tight fit, then size down. The tag says you can hand wash them or wash them inside out on the delicate cycle. They came pretty wrinkled (I haven't washed them yet and the pics show the way they arrived), so I think I'll need to wash and gently steam these to smooth them out. The regular in a 30 is 30 for length as well and is AMAZING on me. Not too short at all. Front pockets are pretty short and useless but I don’t care the rest is a dream! These can be worn dressed up or down.

These fit nicely, but first I want to make sure anyone reading this is aware of what an ordeal it was to wash and clean up after these pants, as the dye ran worse than anything I have ever seen in my life. Full disclosure: I am not 100% sure who is the culprit, as I washed both this pair of pants and a new pair of the Rafaella lightweight satin twill ankle dress pants (also in black) at the same time. I wouldn't have noticed how horrifically they color ran due to the them both being black, except that I had also tossed a brand new light pink bra in a white mesh delicates bag in at the same time - that one's on me, I was just thinking "wash the new stuff before wearing" and not about color bleeding. I used Woolite detergent, and when I pulled the previously white delicates bag out, it was the blue/purple color of ballpoint pen ink (fortunately the bra inside was just a slightly more mauve-y dusty pink, not completely dyed). I soaked the bag and bra, and have gotten some of the dye out, and I washed the pants through two more cycles. Out of an abundance of caution, I ran the washer again in case dye was left in the machine, and put in an old white cleaning rag as a test. It came out purple. I ran it multiple more times with a new white cleaning rag each time, and they eventually got lighter and lighter purple. I ran a load of Tide Laundry Machine cleaner through, and then bought a box of Shout Color Catcher sheets to run through, and after maybe a dozen wash cycles across two days, I'm finally confident I've gotten all the residual dye out and have run a load of regular laundry through with no problems. I'm nervous to wash either pair of pants again, as I'm not sure if I'll have to go through this whole process again or if I've gotten out all the dye that's going to bleed. I've included pictures of my purple rag progression (the mesh bag is also in the photo, but that's after soaking and running it through the wash again), as well as the tags from both pairs of pants where you can see that they are stained purple as well (although it has lightened significantly from the first wash). I'm actually rather glad I had the bra/mesh bag in that same load; even though they got stained, I would have otherwise just tossed a load of regular clothes in next and ruined nearly my entire wardrobe. I left this same warning and photos on a review for the other pants' page as well. But let's talk about the fit of the actual pants! These are very nice, but not perfect pants. I really struggle to find pants that fit my hips and thighs but don't have a horrible gap at the waist - I usually end up cinching it in paper bag style with a belt. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and ordered a size 14 curvy (sized up from the suggested sizing chart, which would have put me in a 12: 32 waist, 43 hips). These pants fit and flattered my legs nicely, and the waist is a bit loose but manageable. I'll probably move the buttons/hooks over a bit to help. They are quite long on me and will need hemmed, even to wear with heels, but I expected that. I wish the material was a bit thicker - I would describe it as almost midway between dress pant material and tee shirt material. In black, this isn't as much of an issue, but in a lighter color I think it would be somewhat clingy and probably showing off bumps and dimples in ways that I wouldn't like. Overall, I'm satisfied with the pants, but I don't think they were worth the laundry debacle.

These GENLECK crossover, high waist, cuffed jeans are so on trend right now! I really like the softness of the denim, good pockets and just enough distressing in the wide legs. My husband doesn't care for what he calls "the high water" look, however, when the trend is over you can clip a couple stitches for a regular fringed length. The waist has the regular button closure and a hidden button underneath. Now the sizing... For reference I'm 5'6" 128# ~ waist 30, hips 36. When I ordered I noticed 2 size charts and went with the one recommended by Amazon for a 30" waist, that chart showed sizing measurements from GENLECK ~ so I went with M. However, that chart had different size options according to style, I missed that originally. The waist sizing was smaller on the chart at the top of the page, but with the stretch (and these have good stretch) I figured M should work. When I got them, I noticed the tags had 2 different sizes labeled S and M! I'm not sure what size I received, doing my own measuring they seem to be the S from the top chart. They really are cute, but watch the sizing...

They're a slightly stretchy, medium-wight twill. I ordered a 6 based on the size chart but should have listened to Amazon's advice when they said to get a 4. If Amazon makes a recommendation, I would follow it over the manufacturer's size chart in this instance. For reference, I'm 5'8", 110 lbs, 26" waist, 35" hip, 33" inseam. I do like the length, although according to the size chart it's the same for the 4 and 6. It's a 32" inseam according to my measurement but feels longer. I'm not accounting for the rise, which is average. I expected it to be shorter as I assumed these were going to be cut like junior's pants, but to my relief they're not. Maybe I'll wash them in warm water and tumble dry to tighten them up a bit. They're 98% cotton so they should shrink somewhat if you want them to. However, the green dye is already running into the white so it might be best to use cold water. I love the flare—they're as close to bell bottoms as I've seen in this century. They're wider than a boot cut unless the footwear in question is moon boots. They're unhemmed and have fashionably frayed ends but there's a line of subtle stitches 1/4" above the bottom to keep the fabric from further fraying. There are five functional pockets in all: two traditional jean pockets on the rump and two stitched round pockets in front, plus one small coin pocket, and there are belt loops that can accommodate a range of belt widths. For such an unusual, comfortable pair of jeans $39.99 seems reasonable if you like the way they fit. Again, my only concern is that the green dye will bleed into the white.

These green and white checkered pants are adorable, comfortable and unique. Even though they're available on Amazon, you're unlikely to end up in a room with anyone else wearing them. The denim is on the thinner side if you compare them with typical jeans (Levis, etc.) but they're not too lightweight to wear in average NYC fall temperatures. These had a LOT of stretch and were SHORT with a saggy crotch area. I ordered a LARGE but I usually wear MEDIUM in pants because I wasn't sure hot stretchy or long they would truly be. The large fit ok, they were much more stretchy than expected, so I recommend either going with your typical size or sizing DOWN. I am 5'5" and these were a bit of high-water on me when I wore them comfortably above my hips. I had to pull them up a lot through the night so the crotch wasn’t down to my knees. Not sure if washing them will reset the material.

These harem pants are 95% polyester 5% spandex and so comfortable!! I am 5ft2in, 191 lbs, 40in waist, 47in hip. I usually order an XL in pants and so that’s what I ordered. I am happy with the XL fit. The waist elastic is very comfortable. They are a little long even with 3in heels on, so I will take them up a couple of inches and they won’t drag the floor. They will be fine for taller gals. They have pockets on the hips that lay flat and don’t stick out making your hips look larger. I washed them in cool water with like colors and tossed them in the dryer on medium heat. No color run and they came out without wrinkles. I ordered the Orangeflower print and there are plenty of colors in that print to choose from to coordinate whether I wear them in the fall or in the spring. I love them! So groovy!

These have become my go to summer pants! They feel slightly synthetic but not overly so. They are super cute, comfortable and breathable. Purchased these in two colors and fit and fabric is consistent. Pockets and drawstring are real and functional. I am 5’1 and I ordered a small and the length is perfect- which is was a pleasant surprise!
These have easily turned into my favorite pair of jeans. I love them so much. I would however not recommend for short people. I'm 5'7 and I need to either wear heels or my platform sandals with them.

These have turned out to be my favorite pants so far. Elastic waist with real pocket that are deep enough to hold my phone. I am 5'1 , 135pds and I ordered a small. I do wear heels but the length was perfect. The back pockets are tacked so they are non-working but look very professional. I've ordered them in Navy as well.

These high waist straight leg ripped jeans are a stylish and trendy staple in my wardrobe. I'm 5'2" and knee holes are well-placed, unlike other pants this style that are typically made for taller people. These jeans don't have much stretch and give a bit of the camel toe effect because they're so high waisted. The leg length is perfectly tailored, and I can wear them with sneakers, booties, or even flip-flops.

These high waisted 70’s style jeans have stretch, and are the perfect length for flats or heels. Very comfortable and material is flattering. For reference, I’m 52 yrs young, 5’3” and 135lbs. I bought a size 6. Normally I’m an 8, but with the stretch, it was perfect. I am short waisted(short torso) so on a normal waist, these would probably go a little less high than these do on me. The length is about 1/8” from the floor. Absolutely perfect! Would definitely try these in more colors! The front crease completely bringing back the 70’s look is the best part! 5 stars!!

The fabric is truly denim but relatively thin so it didn't weigh my luggage down.

These jeans are amazing! Light weight denim, nice blue color options, stretchy but hold their shape, nice wide leg. As a shortie (5ft) these are a fuller length denim on me, to my ankle and look great. I’m a size 6 and ordered the medium and they are so comfortable. Full travel days and nothing digging in and suffocating. These and the Anthro Colette jeans arrived on the same day I kept these instead, much lighter weight, more stretch, better denim color, and a fraction of the price. I now own 3 pairs!

These jeans are amazing. I have bought 3 pairs. When skinny jeans came around they were not making boot cut flair or bell bottoms anymore. Finally the style is coming back around. Highly recommend.

Also, the ankle would be tighter if I wasn’t short and they’re too long, because I have too much extra material bunched up at the bottom. Other than the length though I love them and they’re pretty flattering!

These jeans are beyond comfortable. They are very stretchy yet not thin or cheap feeling at all! The stitching is very well done. I love the three buttons and thick band at waistline. They fit well with no gaping at the waist. Bottom opening is very wide and length was perfect. I always expect jeans to be crazy long as I am only 5'3" but these are the perfect length to pair with my boots. I was pleasantly surprised with how deep tge front pockets are as most women's jeans have pretty useless front pockets. The ONLY complaint I had was with the ay the denim portion is cut on the front pockets. I noticed that the denim is cut very close to pocket opening so that you can actually see the stitching where denim meets pocket liner. Not a deal breaker, but I felt myself constantly readjusting that pocket (see last photo).

These jeans are crazy-comfortable. I wouldn't say that I suddenly appear to be a size smaller than I actually am, but I do like how they fit pear-me. The back waistline doesn't gap, which is very appreciated. Here's what makes me unhappy -- in several areas the stitching is inferior to several pairs of $25 jeans that are in my closet (hopefully the photo I'm adding actually appears). For the price (not the most costly jeans ever produced, but not cheap), the craftsmanship should be better. They're made in the US, so I like that and again, very comfortable, so that's important. By the way, I'm 5'4 and the jeans are too long for me by a few inches. Easily remedied at the local tailor's shop.

These jeans are cute and comfortable. They just didn't fit me the way I wanted. They are baggy in the crotch area. Maybe a smaller size would be better? For reference I am 5ft 3in and weight 140lbs and ordered a Medium. I am still giving a 5 star review because the quality seems good I think I just ordered the wrong size for me. . Shipping was super fast!

These jeans are cute but they are pretty bulky. If you are a bit wider in the hip area they will make you look even wider. These would be great for winter but in Florida they are a little too thick for April weather. They are fitted in the hips (curvy girl). I think they will add curves to someone who is more of a rectangular shape and be quite flattering. If you have a small waist and hips you may not want the extra baggage, which is why I’m torn about keeping them or getting a refund. The front of the jeans don’t have any tummy support so I would not wear them when bloated. The button isn’t as secure as mainstream brand jeans, either. For reference I’m 5’4 and a half inches tall with a short torso. If I decide to keep them, I will need to cut the stings that are hanging off of the ruffles.

These jeans are cute, but they look like pants to a sexy Frankenstein or Hulk costume. The faux distressed hem doesn't resemble normal wear and tear: it's too symmetrical. I'm going to have to either hem them and make capris or cut them to (ironically) look less deliberate. But the cotton/spandex blend makes the fabric stretchy, soft and comfortable, and the pants have five pockets (including the fob) and belt loops like regular jeans. They're a bit loose on me, so I'd have to say they run large. For reference, I'm 5'8", 120 lbs, 26 waist, 35 hip, 33 inseam (not relevant here) and ordered a 4. They're borderline low riders but don't have a short rise, which is good. In other words, not a Junior's fit, more women's (but not matronly).

These jeans are nice and stretchy. In Levi's Mid Rise Skinny jeans, I have been wearing a 10, but they are cutting me in half at the waist lately. I am about 5'4", 143 lbs. I carry my weight in my stomach, so that's where most of my "curvy" is. I ordered a 10 in these jeans, hoping the extra stretch would help in the waist. They were, indeed, very comfortable in the waist, which measured 16" flat across. But the thighs and front were kind of baggy. I sent them back and ordered two pairs of 8s and they fit much better. The waist is a bit smaller, measuring 15" across, but there is so much stretch available that it's not a problem. The butt and legs fit closer, although they are not what I would consider tight, as you can see by my picture. Inseam on the size 10 and the 8 is 30". And they are definitely high waisted on me, not mid. Maybe I'm short waisted, but they come up to just above my belly button. Kind of strange feeling at first, but even though I do not tuck my shirts in, my muffin top always wanted to show through my tops. That isn't happening as much now, so I'm liking it. The size 10 pair were the Bristol-Whiskers, a kind of light blue. The 8s I ordered are Salida and a really dark pair, Parker Whisker. Out of the 3, the Salida are my favorite. A medium blue (lighter than they appear in the picture, but darker than the 10s). The other 8s, the Parker Whisker, are extremely dark blue and I may exchange them for another Salida. The "whiskering" on all of these jeans is pretty mild, much less than it looks in the pics they show. Today is the first day of me wearing my new jeans, so I can't tell if they will bag out, but they don't seem like they will.

These jeans are so lightweight and comfortable, they accentuate your curves and hug in all the right places. I've had so many compliments on them and I've only had them for 3-4 days!
